title: Spelling CVC Words
description: Can you spell simple words like cat, bed, and hop? Build a spelling display that shows each letter being placed.
story_text: Time to be a spelling star! Chip will show you a picture and a word with a missing letter. Can you figure out which letter goes in the blank? Sound it out and give it a try!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">_ a t</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Spell It!", 30, 5)

words = ["cat", "bed", "hop", "pig", "sun"]
for word in words:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    display = ""
    for j in range(len(word)):
        display += word[j] + " "
        Lcd.fillRect(0, 45, 135, 30, 0x000000)
        partial = ""
        for k in range(len(word)):
            if k <= j:
                partial += word[k] + " "
            else:
                partial += "_ "
        Lcd.drawString(partial, 30, 55)
        Speaker.tone(400 + j * 100, 150)
        time.sleep(0.7)
    Lcd.drawString(word + "!", 45, 85)
    Speaker.tone(770, 250)
    time.sleep(1.2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Spell champ!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Show the word with underscores for missing letters: _ _ t.
Reveal each letter one at a time, replacing the underscore.
Play a different tone as each letter appears.
Show the complete word when all letters are revealed!
Loop through several words for more practice.

questions.hint: What sound is in the middle of cat? C-aaa-t!
questions.sentence: c ___ t (a furry pet that says meow)

questions.hint: What sound is in the middle of bed? B-eee-d!
questions.sentence: b ___ d (where you sleep at night)

questions.hint: What sound is in the middle of hop? H-ooo-p!
questions.sentence: h ___ p (what a bunny does)

questions.hint: What sound is in the middle of pen? P-eee-n!
questions.sentence: p ___ n (you write with this)

questions.hint: What sound is in the middle of sit? S-iii-t!
questions.sentence: s ___ t (you do this in a chair)

questions.hint: What sound is in the middle of run? R-uuu-n!
questions.sentence: r ___ n (what you do when you go fast)

title: Word Families
description: Words that end the same way are in the same family! Build a word family display showing -at, -an, -ig, and -op families.
story_text: Chip found out that words have families too! Words that end with the same sound belong together. Cat, hat, and bat are all in the -at family! Let's meet some word families today!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">-at family</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Word Families!", 5, 5)

families = [
    ("-at", ["cat","hat","bat","sat"]),
    ("-an", ["can","man","fan","ran"]),
    ("-ig", ["big","pig","dig","wig"]),
    ("-op", ["hop","pop","top","mop"])
]
colors = [0xEF4444, 0x3B82F6, 0xEAB308, 0x22C55E]

for idx, (ending, words) in enumerate(families):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(colors[idx], 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(ending, 45, 30)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    for i, word in enumerate(words):
        Lcd.drawString(word, 10 + i * 30, 60)
        Speaker.tone(500, 80)
        time.sleep(0.4)
    time.sleep(1.2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Family fun!", 20, 60)

hints.text: Show a word ending like -at in a bright color as the family name.
Display words that belong to this family below: cat, hat, bat, sat.
Show each family word one at a time with a beep.
Move to the next word family after showing all the words.
Use different colors for each word family!

activities.hint: Look at the ending of each word. Cat ends in -at, pan ends in -an...
activities.prompt: Match each word to its word family!

questions.hint: A red sign on the street says this word. It rhymes with hop!
questions.sentence: Hop, mop, top, and ___ all end in -op. (you do this when you quit)

title: Story Order
description: Put the parts of each story in the right order! Build a story sequencing display on your M5Stick that shows events step by step.
story_text: Chip mixed up the pages of some stories! Oh no! Can you help put them back in order? Think about what happens first, what happens next, and what happens last. You can do it!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">1. Wake up</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">40</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_wait"><value name="SECONDS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">1</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">2. Eat food</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Story Order!", 15, 5)

stories = [
    ["Wake up", "Eat food", "Go to school"],
    ["Get seeds", "Plant seeds", "Water them"],
    ["Mix batter", "Bake cake", "Eat cake!"]
]

for story in stories:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    for i, event in enumerate(story):
        Lcd.drawString(str(i+1) + ". " + event, 10, 35 + i * 22)
        Speaker.tone(500 + i * 80, 150)
        time.sleep(1)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Story smart!", 15, 60)
Speaker.tone(880, 400)

hints.text: Show a story title at the top of the screen.
Display the first event with a 1 next to it.
Add event 2 and event 3 below, one at a time with pauses.
Play ascending tones: low for first, high for last event.
Try different stories to practice sequencing!

title: Who and Where?
description: Read a short story and find the characters and the setting! Build a story explorer on your M5Stick.
story_text: Every story has a WHO and a WHERE! The WHO is the character -- the person or animal the story is about. The WHERE is the setting -- the place where the story happens. Let's read some stories and find them!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Who: Cat</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Where: Park</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">70</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Who & Where?", 10, 5)

stories = [
    ("A cat at the park", "Cat", "Park"),
    ("A dog in the house", "Dog", "House"),
    ("A bird at school", "Bird", "School"),
    ("A fish in the sea", "Fish", "Sea")
]

for story, who, where in stories:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(story, 5, 30)
    time.sleep(1.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("Who: " + who, 10, 55)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xF97316, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("Where: " + where, 10, 75)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    Speaker.tone(660, 200)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Story finder!", 10, 60)

Wait so the reader can read the story.
Show Who: with the character name in blue.

title: What Happened?
description: Read short stories and answer questions about key events! Build a story quiz on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip loves a good story! After you read a story, it is fun to think about what happened. Let's read together and answer some questions. Pay close attention to the details!

solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("What Happened?", 3, 5)

qa = [
    ("A dog found a bone.", "Found a bone!"),
    ("The girl ran fast.", "She ran fast!"),
    ("A bird built a nest.", "Built a nest!"),
    ("The boy ate an apple.", "Ate an apple!")
]

for story, answer in qa:

    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    Speaker.tone(660, 200)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Story star!", 20, 60)

title: Rhyme Time!
description: Match words that sound the same at the end! Build a rhyming word display that pairs up rhyming words on your M5Stick.
story_text: Rhyming is when words sound the same at the end. Like cat and hat! Or bug and rug! Chip thinks rhyming is super fun. Can you find all the rhyming pairs?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">cat - hat</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Rhyme Match!", 15, 5)

pairs = [("sun","fun"), ("cake","lake"), ("tree","bee"), ("night","light"), ("ball","tall")]
for w1, w2 in pairs:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(w1, 20, 50)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xF97316, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(w2, 80, 50)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("They rhyme!", 20, 80)
    Speaker.tone(770, 250)
    time.sleep(1.3)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Rhyme champ!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Show the first word in blue on the left side.
Show the rhyming word in orange on the right side.
Display They rhyme! below when both words are shown.
Play a matching sound for each rhyming pair!
Try 5 different rhyming pairs.

activities.hint: Say each word out loud. Which two words end with the same sound?
activities.prompt: Match the words that rhyme! (They sound the same at the end.)

questions.hint: Dog ends with -og. Which other word ends with -og?
questions.prompt: Which word rhymes with DOG?

questions.hint: Pan ends with -an. Which word also ends with -an?
questions.prompt: Which word rhymes with PAN?

questions.hint: Ring ends with -ing. Which word also ends with -ing?
questions.prompt: Which word rhymes with RING?

title: How Do They Feel?
description: Read about characters and figure out how they are feeling! Build an emotions display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Did you know that when you read a story, you can figure out how the characters feel? If someone smiles and laughs, they are probably happy! If they cry, they might be sad. Let's be feeling detectives!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">She smiles.</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">40</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">= Happy!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">70</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Feelings!", 30, 5)

stories = [
    ("She got a gift!", "Happy!"),
    ("He lost his toy.", "Sad"),
    ("A loud noise came!", "Scared"),
    ("She won the race!", "Excited"),
    ("He waited so long.", "Bored")
]
emojis = [0x22C55E, 0x3B82F6, 0xA855F7, 0xF97316, 0xEAB308]

for i, (story, feeling) in enumerate(stories):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(story, 5, 35)
    time.sleep(1.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(emojis[i], 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("Feeling: " + feeling, 5, 65)
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 95, 10, emojis[i])
    Speaker.tone(550, 200)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Empathy star!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Display a short sentence about a character.
Wait so the reader can think about how the character feels.
Reveal the feeling in a color that matches the emotion.
Draw a colored circle as a simple face emoji.
Practice reading 5 different feeling stories!

questions.hint: Jess smiled big and hugged the puppy. When you smile and hug, how do you feel?
questions.prompt: Jess got a puppy for her birthday! She smiled big and hugged it tight. How does Jess feel?

questions.hint: Leo lost his ice cream and his lip is shaking. That sounds like he might cry. How does he feel?
questions.prompt: Leo dropped his ice cream on the ground. His lip started to shake. How does Leo feel?

questions.hint: Sara heard a loud noise and hid under her blanket. Why would she hide?
questions.prompt: Sara heard a loud noise in the dark. She hid under her blanket. How does Sara feel?

questions.hint: Max is going somewhere fun and jumping up and down. How do you feel when something great is about to happen?
questions.prompt: Max is going to the amusement park today! He keeps jumping up and down. How does Max feel?

questions.hint: Ava worked hard, her teacher liked her work, and she stood up tall. How do you feel when someone says good job?
questions.prompt: Ava worked very hard on her drawing. Her teacher said it was beautiful! Ava stood up tall. How does Ava feel?

title: What is a Sentence?
description: A sentence is a group of words that tells a complete thought. Build a sentence checker on your M5Stick!
story_text: Hey friend! Chip wants to teach you about sentences! A sentence tells a whole idea. It has a WHO (like a person or animal) and a WHAT (what they do). "The cat ran" is a sentence. But just "the cat" is not -- what did the cat do? Let's practice!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">The cat sat.</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">= Sentence!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">75</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sentence?", 25, 5)

examples = [
    ("The cat sat.", True),
    ("cat the", False),
    ("She is happy.", True),
    ("running fast", False),
    ("I like dogs.", True)
]

for text, is_sentence in examples:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(text, 5, 40)
    time.sleep(1.5)
    if is_sentence:
        Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
        Lcd.drawString("Sentence!", 25, 70)
    else:
        Lcd.setTextColor(0xEF4444, 0x000000)
        Lcd.drawString("Not yet!", 30, 70)
    Speaker.tone(660 if is_sentence else 330, 200)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Grammar pro!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Show a group of words on the screen.
Wait so the reader can decide: is it a sentence?
A real sentence has a capital letter, makes sense, and ends with a period.
Show Sentence! in green or Not yet! in red.
Play different sounds for sentences vs non-sentences.

questions.hint: A sentence needs a WHO and a WHAT. Which one has both?
questions.prompt: Which one is a complete sentence?

questions.hint: A sentence tells a whole idea. Which one tells you WHO likes WHAT?
questions.prompt: Which one is a complete sentence?

questions.hint: Which one tells you WHO does WHAT? Remember, a sentence needs both parts!
questions.prompt: Which one is a complete sentence?

options.text: The cat sits on the mat.

questions.hint: Who is singing? A sentence needs to tell us WHO does the action.
questions.prompt: Which one is a complete sentence?

questions.hint: Look for the one that is missing a WHAT (an action). The blue ball did what?
questions.prompt: Which one is NOT a complete sentence?

title: Capital Letters & Periods
description: Every sentence starts with a BIG letter and ends with a period! Build a punctuation display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip has two important rules for sentences! Rule 1: Start with a capital (big) letter. Rule 2: End with a period (a tiny dot). Let's practice spotting which sentences follow the rules!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">the cat sat</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">40</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Fix It!", 40, 5)

sentences = [
    ("the cat sat", "The cat sat."),
    ("i like dogs", "I like dogs."),
    ("she can run", "She can run."),
    ("we are here", "We are here.")
]

for wrong, right in sentences:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xEF4444, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(wrong, 5, 40)
    time.sleep(1.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(right, 5, 65)
    Lcd.drawString("Capital + .", 15, 90)
    Speaker.tone(770, 200)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Punctuation!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Show a sentence without a capital letter or period in red.
Wait for the reader to spot the mistakes.
Show the fixed sentence in green with capital letter and period.
Remind: every sentence needs a Capital letter at the start and a . at the end.
Practice fixing 4 different sentences!

questions.hint: A correct sentence starts with a capital letter AND ends with a period.
questions.prompt: Which sentence is written correctly?

questions.hint: The word I is always a capital letter, and the sentence needs a period at the end.
questions.prompt: Which sentence is written correctly?

questions.hint: Look at the beginning -- is the first letter big? Look at the end -- is there a period?
questions.prompt: What is MISSING from this sentence? "she has a red bike"

options.text: A capital letter at the start
Both a capital letter and a period

questions.hint: Only the FIRST word gets a capital letter. And we need a period at the end!
questions.prompt: Which sentence is written correctly?

questions.hint: Capitalize only the first word, and add a period at the very end.
questions.prompt: Fix this sentence: "my dog is happy" What should it look like?

title: Naming Words (Nouns)
description: Nouns are naming words! Build a noun display on your M5Stick that shows people, places, animals, and things.
story_text: Chip has a fun word game for you! Some words are special because they NAME things. A dog is a noun. A park is a noun. Your name is a noun! These naming words are called nouns. Let's find them!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">cat = noun!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Nouns!", 40, 5)

categories = [
    ("Person", ["mom", "teacher", "boy"]),
    ("Place", ["school", "park", "home"]),
    ("Animal", ["cat", "dog", "bird"]),
    ("Thing", ["book", "ball", "cup"])
]
colors = [0xEF4444, 0x3B82F6, 0x22C55E, 0xF97316]

for i, (cat, words) in enumerate(categories):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(colors[i], 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(cat + ":", 10, 30)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    y = 50
    for word in words:
        Lcd.drawString(word, 20, y)
        Speaker.tone(500, 80)
        time.sleep(0.4)
        y += 18
    time.sleep(1.2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Noun expert!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Show a category like Person in a bright color.
List nouns that fit that category: mom, teacher, boy.
Nouns name people, places, animals, and things.
Show each word one at a time with a beep.
Use different colors for each noun category!

activities.hint: Is it a person, a place, an animal, or a thing? Think about what each word names!
activities.prompt: Match each noun to what kind of noun it is!

questions.hint: A noun names a person, place, animal, or thing. Which word is a thing?
questions.prompt: Which word is a NOUN (a naming word)?

questions.hint: Which word names a thing you can hold in your hands?
questions.prompt: Which word is a NOUN?

questions.hint: Which word names a living creature in the sentence?
questions.prompt: Find the noun in this sentence: The bird sat on a branch.

questions.hint: Which word names a yummy treat you can eat?
questions.prompt: Find the noun in this sentence: She ate a cookie.

questions.hint: One of these words describes something instead of naming something. Which one?
questions.prompt: Which of these is NOT a noun?

title: Action Words (Verbs)
description: Verbs are action words! Build an action word display on your M5Stick that shows what people and animals DO.
story_text: Last time we learned nouns -- naming words. Now Chip wants to teach you VERBS! Verbs are action words. Run, jump, eat, sing -- those are all verbs! Let's find the action in every sentence!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">run = verb!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Verbs!", 40, 5)

verbs = ["run", "jump", "eat", "sing", "read", "swim", "fly", "play"]
colors = [0xEF4444, 0xF97316, 0xEAB308, 0xA855F7, 0x3B82F6, 0x14B8A6, 0xEC4899, 0x22C55E]

for i, verb in enumerate(verbs):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(colors[i], 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(verb, 40, 50)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("= action!", 30, 75)
    Speaker.tone(400 + i * 40, 150)
    time.sleep(1.2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Verb master!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Show each verb in a different bright color.
Verbs are doing words: run, jump, eat, sing.
Display = action! below each verb to reinforce the concept.
Use a loop to show 8 different verbs.
Play a different tone for each verb!

activities.hint: A verb tells what you DO. Match the action word to what it looks like!

right.text: Looking at words in a book

activities.prompt: Match each action word to its picture!

questions.hint: A verb is something you can DO. Can you book? Can you jump? Can you red?
questions.prompt: Which word is a VERB (an action word)?

questions.hint: Which word is something you can DO in a pool?
questions.prompt: Which word is a VERB?

questions.prompt: Find the verb in this sentence: The girl runs to school.

questions.hint: What is he DOING to the banana? That word is the verb!
questions.prompt: Find the verb in this sentence: He eats a banana.

title: What Makes Sound?
description: Sound is made by vibrations! When something vibrates fast, it makes a high sound. Vibrate slow, low sound. Explore vibrations with your M5Stick buzzer!
story_text: Hey there, explorer! Chip here! Have you ever wondered why a guitar goes TWANG or a drum goes BOOM? It's all because of vibrations -- tiny shakes that travel through the air to your ears! Let's find out what makes different sounds!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Vibrations!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">500</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("What Makes Sound?", 0, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Slow vibration:", 5, 30)
Speaker.tone(200, 500)
time.sleep(0.7)
Lcd.drawString("= LOW sound!", 15, 48)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Fast vibration:", 5, 75)
Speaker.tone(800, 500)
time.sleep(0.7)
Lcd.drawString("= HIGH sound!", 15, 93)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sound = shaking!", 5, 125)
Lcd.drawString("(vibrations)", 15, 145)

hints.text: Sound happens when something VIBRATES (shakes back and forth really fast). The buzzer inside your M5Stick vibrates to make sound!
Play a LOW tone (200 Hz). Low frequency = slow vibrations = deep low sound.
Play a HIGH tone (800 Hz). High frequency = fast vibrations = high squeaky sound.
Label each sound on the display. The number (frequency) tells us HOW FAST it vibrates!
ALL sound is vibrations! Your voice vibrates your throat, a drum vibrates its skin, the buzzer vibrates metal!

activities.prompt: Match each thing to the sound it makes!

questions.hint: When something shakes back and forth very fast, it makes a sound!
questions.prompt: What do all sounds come from?

questions.hint: Put your hand on your throat and hum. Feel that buzzing? Those are your vocal cords!
questions.prompt: What vibrates when you talk?

options.text: Your vocal cords in your throat

questions.hint: A plucked rubber band shakes back and forth -- that shaking is a vibration!
questions.prompt: If you pluck a rubber band, what happens?

options.text: It vibrates and makes a sound

questions.hint: A rock just sitting there is not shaking, so it is not making any sound.
questions.prompt: Which of these does NOT make a sound by vibrating?

options.text: A rock sitting on the ground

title: Loud and Quiet Sounds
description: Some sounds are LOUD (like thunder) and some are QUIET (like a whisper). Measure loudness with your M5Stick! Scientists call loudness VOLUME!
story_text: Shhh... can you hear that? Some sounds are very quiet, like a whisper. And some sounds are SUPER LOUD, like a fire truck siren! Chip wants to figure out what makes sounds loud or quiet. Ready to investigate?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Volume!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">100</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Loud & Quiet!", 10, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Whisper:", 5, 30)
Speaker.tone(440, 80)
Lcd.fillRect(75, 33, 10, 8, 0x00FF00)
time.sleep(0.8)
Lcd.drawString("Talk:", 5, 55)
Speaker.tone(440, 250)
Lcd.fillRect(75, 58, 30, 8, 0xFFFF00)
time.sleep(0.8)
Lcd.drawString("Shout:", 5, 80)
Speaker.tone(440, 500)
Lcd.fillRect(75, 83, 55, 8, 0xFF0000)
time.sleep(0.8)
Lcd.drawString("Volume = loudness", 2, 115)
Lcd.drawString("Short=quiet", 5, 135)
Lcd.drawString("Long=loud!", 5, 150)

hints.text: VOLUME in science means how LOUD or QUIET a sound is. A whisper has low volume, a shout has high volume!
Play a quiet sound (short duration 80ms). Draw a tiny green bar to show low volume.
Play a medium sound (250ms). Draw a medium yellow bar. Getting louder!
Play a loud sound (500ms). Draw a big red bar. Thats loud volume!
The bars show volume visually -- like a volume meter on a speaker! Scientists measure sound in decibels.

questions.hint: Fire trucks need to be heard far away so cars will move out of the way!
questions.prompt: Which sound is the LOUDEST?

questions.hint: Mice are tiny and make very soft, small sounds!
questions.prompt: Which sound is the QUIETEST?

questions.hint: Hitting harder makes bigger vibrations, and bigger vibrations make louder sounds!
questions.prompt: What happens when you bang a drum HARDER?

questions.hint: Thick strings vibrate slower and make deeper, lower sounds!
questions.prompt: If you pluck a thick guitar string and a thin guitar string, which one sounds lower?

questions.hint: Whispering uses very gentle vibrations, which is why it is so quiet!
questions.prompt: When you whisper, your vocal cords vibrate...

activities.prompt: Match each sound to whether it is loud or quiet!

title: Light All Around
description: Light is everywhere! Some things MAKE light (like the Sun) and some REFLECT light (like the Moon). Show light sources on your M5Stick!
story_text: Look around you -- what do you see? You can see because of LIGHT! Some things make their own light, like the sun and a lamp. Chip is curious: where does all this light come from? Let's find out together!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">67</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Light Sources!", 10, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Makes light:", 5, 25)
Lcd.fillCircle(25, 55, 12, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.drawString("Sun", 42, 50)
Lcd.fillCircle(25, 85, 8, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.drawString("Light bulb", 40, 80)
Lcd.drawString("Reflects light:", 5, 110)
Lcd.fillCircle(25, 135, 12, 0xCCCCCC)
Lcd.drawString("Moon", 42, 130)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Moon bounces", 5, 160)
Lcd.drawString("Sun light!", 15, 175)

hints.text: LIGHT SOURCES make their own light! The Sun, light bulbs, and fire are all light sources.
Draw a bright yellow circle for the Sun. It makes its own light! Its a star!
Add a smaller circle for a light bulb. It also makes light using electricity.
The MOON does NOT make light! It REFLECTS (bounces back) light from the Sun like a mirror!
Draw the Moon in gray. Label that it reflects light. Many things we see are reflecting light, not making it!

cards.back: The sun is our biggest light source. It lights up the whole Earth during the day!
A lamp uses electricity to make light so we can see indoors at night.
A candle makes light by burning. The flame glows and gives off warm light.
A flashlight uses a battery to make a bright beam of light you can point.
Fireflies are bugs that make their own light! Their tummies glow in the dark.
Stars are like faraway suns. They make their own light, but they look tiny because they are so far away.

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about things that make light!

questions.hint: The sun is a giant ball of hot gas that glows and makes light all by itself!
questions.prompt: Which of these makes its OWN light?

questions.hint: The moon looks bright, but it is actually reflecting light from the sun!
questions.prompt: Which of these does NOT make its own light?

questions.hint: Light hits objects and bounces into our eyes -- that is how we see!
questions.prompt: Why can we see things?

options.text: Because light bounces off things and reaches our eyes
Because our eyes shoot out beams
Because everything glows on its own

questions.hint: Without any light, there is nothing to bounce into our eyes, so we cannot see!
questions.prompt: In a very dark room with no light at all, what can you see?

options.text: Everything, if you wait long enough

title: Shadow Play!
description: Make shadow puppets with science! The closer an object is to light, the BIGGER its shadow. Show how shadow size changes!
story_text: Whoa, look at the ground on a sunny day -- do you see your shadow following you? Shadows happen when something blocks the light. Chip loves making silly shadow shapes! Let's learn all about how shadows work!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Shadow Play!", 15, 5)
Lcd.fillCircle(15, 60, 10, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.fillRect(50, 50, 10, 20, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillRect(70, 42, 18, 36, 0x333333)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Close=big shadow", 2, 90)
time.sleep(1.5)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 110, 135, 90, 0x000000)
Lcd.fillCircle(15, 140, 10, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.fillRect(80, 130, 10, 20, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillRect(100, 133, 12, 14, 0x333333)
Lcd.drawString("Far=small shadow", 2, 165)

hints.text: Shadow size depends on how CLOSE the object is to the light! Close to light = BIG shadow. Far from light = small shadow.
Draw a light source (yellow circle) and an object (red rectangle) CLOSE to the light.
Draw a BIG gray shadow behind the object. Close to light makes big shadows!
Now show the object FAR from the light. The shadow is SMALLER!
Try it with a real flashlight and your hand! Move your hand closer to make shadow puppets bigger!

questions.hint: A shadow forms when an object blocks light from passing through!
questions.prompt: What do you need to make a shadow?

options.text: Light and something to block it

questions.hint: No light means no shadow! You need a light source to create shadows.
questions.prompt: Can you see your shadow in a completely dark room?

options.text: No, you need light to make a shadow
Only if you stand very still

questions.hint: When the sun is low in the sky, it makes long, stretched-out shadows!
questions.prompt: When is your shadow the LONGEST outside?

options.text: At noon when the sun is high

questions.hint: Cardboard blocks all the light, so it makes the darkest shadow!
questions.prompt: Which material would make the DARKEST shadow?

questions.hint: Moving closer to the light makes your shadow grow bigger on the wall!
questions.prompt: What happens to your shadow when you move closer to a light?

activities.prompt: Match each object to the kind of shadow it makes!

title: Why Do We Need Light?
description: We need light to see! Without light, everything is dark. Show what happens when there IS light vs when there is NO light!
story_text: Imagine you are in a cave with no flashlight. Could you read a book? Nope! We need light to see things. And guess what -- people use both light and sound to send messages! Chip is going to show you how. Let's go!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Why Light?</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Why Light?", 20, 5)
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x333333, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("NO LIGHT:", 5, 30)
Lcd.drawString("Cant see!", 5, 48)
Lcd.drawString("Everything dark", 5, 66)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.fillScreen(0xFFFFCC)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x000000, 0xFFFFCC)
Lcd.drawString("WITH LIGHT:", 5, 30)
Lcd.drawString("We can see!", 5, 48)
Lcd.fillCircle(40, 90, 15, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillRect(70, 80, 30, 20, 0x0000FF)
Lcd.drawString("Objects visible!", 5, 120)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF0000, 0xFFFFCC)
Lcd.drawString("Light helps us", 5, 150)
Lcd.drawString("SEE the world!", 5, 168)

hints.text: Without LIGHT, we cannot see anything! Our eyes need light to detect objects and colors.
Show NO LIGHT: black screen with very faint text. You can barely see anything! Everything is dark.
Show WITH LIGHT: bright screen with colorful shapes. Now you can see everything clearly!
Light bounces off objects into our eyes. Thats how we see colors and shapes!
Plants need light to grow, animals need it to find food. Light is essential for life on Earth!

questions.hint: Without light, our eyes cannot see what is around us!
questions.prompt: Why do people turn on lights at night?

questions.hint: Red means stop, yellow means slow down, green means go -- all using light!
questions.prompt: How does a traffic light communicate with drivers?

options.text: It uses colored lights: red, yellow, and green

questions.hint: Ambulances use both light (flashing) and sound (siren) to communicate!
questions.prompt: How does an ambulance tell other cars to move?

options.text: It uses flashing lights AND a loud siren

questions.hint: Ships can see the lighthouse beam from far away in the dark!
questions.prompt: A lighthouse helps ships at sea by using...

questions.hint: When someone pushes the button, you hear a ding-dong -- that is sound!
questions.prompt: A doorbell communicates using...

activities.prompt: Match each thing to how it communicates: with light or with sound!

title: Parts of a Plant
description: Plants have parts -- roots, stem, leaves, and flower! Draw a plant on your M5Stick and label each part to learn what they do!
story_text: Chip found a garden full of beautiful plants! Every plant has special parts that help it live and grow -- roots, a stem, leaves, and flowers. Let's explore each part and find out what job it does!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_rect"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">63</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">80</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">8</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Plant Parts!", 15, 5)
Lcd.fillCircle(67, 45, 20, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Flower", 85, 40)
Lcd.fillCircle(45, 75, 12, 0x00FF00)
Lcd.fillCircle(89, 75, 12, 0x00FF00)
Lcd.drawString("Leaves", 95, 70)
Lcd.fillRect(63, 50, 8, 80, 0x00FF00)
Lcd.drawString("Stem", 75, 95)
Lcd.fillRect(55, 130, 25, 3, 0x8B4513)

Lcd.fillRect(70, 130, 4, 20, 0x8B4513)

hints.text: Plants have 4 main parts: ROOTS (underground), STEM (trunk), LEAVES, and FLOWER. Each has a job!
Draw the STEM: a green rectangle going up from the bottom. Thats the plants highway for water!
Add LEAVES: green circles on the sides. Leaves make food from sunlight! Thats called photosynthesis.
Add a FLOWER on top: a big colored circle. Flowers help make seeds for new plants!
Add ROOTS at the bottom: brown lines going down. Roots drink water from the soil. Label everything!

cards.back: Roots grow underground. They drink up water and hold the plant in the soil.
The stem is like a straw! It carries water from the roots up to the leaves.
Leaves catch sunlight and use it to make food for the plant. Amazing!
Flowers are pretty AND important! They help the plant make seeds for new baby plants.
Seeds are like tiny plant babies. When they get water and sun, they grow into new plants!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn what this plant part does!

right.text: Soak up water from the soil
Carry water up to the leaves

activities.prompt: Match each plant part to its job!

title: What Do Plants Need?
description: Plants need 4 things to grow: water, sunlight, air, and soil! Display each need as an icon and fact on your M5Stick!
story_text: Chip tried to grow a plant in a dark closet with no water. Guess what happened? It did not grow at all! Plants need special things to be happy and healthy. Let's figure out what every plant needs!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Plants Need:</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Plants Need:", 10, 5)

Lcd.fillCircle(20, 68, 8, 0xFFFF00)

hints.text: Plants are living things that need 4 things to survive and grow! Without ANY one, they will not make it.
WATER (blue): Plants drink water through their roots. Show a blue circle for water.
SUNLIGHT (yellow): Plants use light to make food. AMAZING! Show a yellow circle.
AIR (gray): Plants breathe in carbon dioxide from the air. Show a gray circle.
SOIL (brown): Soil gives plants minerals and a place to anchor roots. Show a brown square. All 4 are needed!

questions.hint: Plants drink water through their roots, just like you drink from a cup!
questions.prompt: Which of these does a plant need to grow?

questions.hint: Leaves are like tiny solar panels -- they catch sunlight to make food!
questions.prompt: What do leaves use to make food for the plant?

questions.hint: Roots dig down into the soil to find water and nutrients!
questions.prompt: Where do most plants grow their roots?

questions.hint: Plants breathe in a gas from the air to help make their food!
questions.prompt: Plants also need this invisible thing from the air. What is it?

questions.hint: Just like you get thirsty, plants need water to stay alive!
questions.prompt: What would happen if a plant got NO water at all?

options.text: It would dry up and die

questions.hint: Plants need water, sunlight, air, and soil -- the four big things!
questions.prompt: Which group shows what ALL plants need?

title: Amazing Animal Body Parts
description: Animals have special body parts that help them survive! Wings for flying, fins for swimming, claws for climbing. Display animal adaptations!
story_text: Animals are amazing! A bird has wings to fly, a fish has fins to swim, and a rabbit has big ears to hear danger. Chip wants to match animals with their special body parts. Can you help?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Body Parts!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Animal Parts!", 10, 5)
parts = [("Wings -> Fly!", 0xFF0000), ("Fins -> Swim!", 0x0000FF), ("Legs -> Run!", 0x00FF00), ("Claws -> Climb!", 0xFF9800)]
y = 30
for text, color in parts:
    Lcd.fillCircle(15, y + 5, 8, color)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(text, 28, y)
    Speaker.tone(400, 100)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 25
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Each part has", 5, 135)
Lcd.drawString("a JOB!", 35, 155)

hints.text: Animals have special body parts called ADAPTATIONS that help them survive in their habitat!
WINGS help birds FLY through the air. Show a red icon for flight.
FINS help fish SWIM in water. LEGS help animals RUN on land.
CLAWS help animals CLIMB trees or dig. Each body part has a specific PURPOSE!
These adaptations evolved over millions of years. Animals without them would not survive!

activities.prompt: Match each animal body part to what it helps the animal do!

questions.hint: Birds use their beaks like we use forks and spoons -- to eat!
questions.prompt: Why does a bird have a beak?

options.text: To pick up and eat food

questions.hint: Both the shell and the quills keep other animals from hurting them!
questions.prompt: What do a turtle's hard shell and a porcupine's sharp quills have in common?

options.text: They protect the animal from danger

questions.hint: White fur hides them in the snow, and thick fur keeps them toasty warm!
questions.prompt: Why do polar bears have thick white fur?

options.text: To stay warm in the cold and blend in with the snow

questions.hint: Giraffes eat leaves from the tops of trees that other animals cannot reach!
questions.prompt: A giraffe has a very long neck. This helps it...

options.text: Reach leaves high up in tall trees

title: Where Do Animals Live?
description: Different animals live in different places! Forest, desert, ocean, arctic. Match animals to their habitats on your M5Stick!
story_text: Every animal has a special place it calls home! Fish live in water, birds build nests in trees, and bears find cozy caves. Chip wants to be a habitat detective -- let's match animals to their homes!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Habitats!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Where Do They", 5, 5)
Lcd.drawString("Live?", 45, 20)
habitats = [("Forest: Bear", 0x00FF00), ("Desert: Lizard", 0xFFFF00), ("Ocean: Fish", 0x0000FF), ("Arctic: Penguin", 0xFFFFFF)]
y = 40
for text, color in habitats:
    Lcd.fillRect(5, y, 12, 12, color)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(text, 22, y + 1)
    Speaker.tone(400, 80)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 23
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Right home for", 5, 140)
Lcd.drawString("each animal!", 10, 158)

hints.text: A HABITAT is the natural home for an animal. Each habitat has the food, water, and shelter the animal needs!
FOREST: Bears live here among trees. Green color for forests full of plants.
DESERT: Lizards love hot, dry deserts. Yellow for sand. OCEAN: Fish live in water. Blue for the sea!
ARCTIC: Penguins live in the freezing cold. White for ice and snow.
Each animal is adapted to its home. A polar bear would be too hot in the desert! Matching matters!

activities.prompt: Match each animal to the place it lives!

questions.hint: Animals need food to eat, water to drink, and a safe place to rest!
questions.prompt: What do ALL animals need from their homes?

questions.hint: High nests keep baby birds safe from cats, foxes, and other predators!
questions.prompt: Why do birds build nests high up in trees?

options.text: To be safe from animals on the ground

questions.hint: The hump stores fat that the camel can use when there is no food or water around!
questions.prompt: A camel lives in the desert. What special thing does it have?

options.text: A hump that stores fat for energy

questions.hint: Polar bears are built for the cold! They would overheat in a hot jungle.
questions.prompt: Could a polar bear live in a hot jungle?

options.text: No, it would be too hot with all that thick fur

questions.hint: A habitat is the place where an animal finds everything it needs to survive!
questions.prompt: What is a habitat?

options.text: The natural home where an animal lives

title: Baby Animals Look Like Their Parents!
description: Baby animals inherit features from their parents! They have the same color, shape, and features but are smaller. Show parent-baby pairs!
story_text: Have you ever seen a baby kitten? It looks a lot like its mom and dad! Baby animals look like their parents -- they share the same colors, shapes, and features. Let's match baby animals to their parents!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Parents!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">25</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Baby = Parent!", 5, 5)
pairs = [("Cat", "Kitten", 0xFF9800), ("Duck", "Duckling", 0xFFFF00), ("Horse", "Foal", 0x8B4513)]
y = 28
for parent, baby, color in pairs:
    Lcd.fillCircle(20, y + 5, 12, color)
    Lcd.fillCircle(50, y + 8, 7, color)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(parent + "->" + baby, 65, y + 2)
    Speaker.tone(400, 100)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 32
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Same color!", 5, 130)
Lcd.drawString("Same shape!", 5, 148)
Lcd.drawString("Just smaller!", 5, 166)

hints.text: Baby animals look like SMALLER versions of their parents! Same fur color, same body shape.
Show a big orange circle (parent cat) next to a small orange circle (kitten). Same color!
Show parent duck and duckling (yellow), horse and foal (brown). Babies match their parents!
The big circle = parent, small circle = baby. Same color but different SIZE.
This is HEREDITY -- parents pass their traits (features) to babies. Living things look like their families!

activities.prompt: Match each baby animal to its parent!

questions.hint: Baby animals get their features from their parents!
questions.prompt: A baby cat (kitten) looks like its parent cat because...

options.text: It has fur, whiskers, and a tail just like mom and dad

questions.hint: Puppies from the same family are similar, but each one is a little bit different!
questions.prompt: Do all puppies in the same family look exactly alike?

options.text: No, they can have different fur colors and spots
Only the girls look the same

questions.hint: A tadpole lives in water and has a tail. It changes a LOT to become a frog!
questions.prompt: Which baby animal looks VERY different from its parent?

questions.hint: Baby birds get their feather colors from their parents!
questions.prompt: If a mother bird has blue feathers, her baby bird will probably...

questions.hint: Traits like fur color, body shape, and size are passed from parents to babies!
questions.prompt: Why do baby animals look like their parents?

options.text: Parents pass on their traits to their babies
They copy each other on purpose

title: Our Amazing Sun!
description: The Sun gives us light and warmth! Program your M5Stick to show the Sun rising with a bright yellow circle and display fun Sun facts!
story_text: Look up at the sky on a sunny day -- do you see that big, bright ball? That is the SUN! It gives us light, it keeps us warm, and it helps plants grow. Chip thinks the sun is the coolest star ever. Let's learn all about it!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">67</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">70</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("The Sun!", 30, 5)
for r in range(5, 35, 5):
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 80, r, 0xFFFF00)
    time.sleep(0.3)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sun Facts:", 20, 125)
Lcd.drawString("Its a STAR!", 15, 145)
Lcd.drawString("It gives light!", 10, 165)
Speaker.tone(523, 300)

hints.text: The Sun is actually a STAR -- the closest star to Earth! It gives us light and heat. Lets show it on screen!
Draw a bright yellow filled circle for the Sun. Make it big in the center of the screen!
Make it grow! Draw circles of increasing size to animate the Sun rising.
Display fun facts: The Sun is a star! It gives us light and warmth!
The Sun is so important -- without it, Earth would be cold and dark. Add a victory sound for our amazing Sun!

cards.back: The sun is a star! It is a giant ball of hot, glowing gas. It is the closest star to Earth.
The sun is SO big that a million Earths could fit inside it!
The sun gives us light and heat. Without it, Earth would be dark and freezing cold!
cards.front: What does the sun give us?

cards.back: Plants use sunlight to make their food. No sun means no food for plants!
cards.front: Why do plants need the sun?

cards.back: NEVER look directly at the sun! It is so bright it can hurt your eyes. Always wear sunglasses on sunny days.
cards.front: Should you look at the sun?

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn a fun fact about the sun!

questions.hint: The sun is actually a star -- the closest one to Earth!
questions.prompt: What is the sun?

questions.hint: Feel how warm a sunny day is? The sun gives us warmth (heat) and brightness (light)!
questions.prompt: What two things does the sun give to Earth?

questions.hint: Earth is always spinning! When our side turns away from the sun, we get night.
questions.prompt: Where does the sun appear to go at night?

options.text: It turns off like a lamp
It goes below the horizon as Earth spins

questions.hint: The sun is our nearest star. The others are so far away they look like tiny dots!
questions.prompt: Is the sun closer or farther than the other stars we see at night?

options.text: MUCH closer -- that is why it looks so big and bright

title: Day and Night
description: Earth spins to give us day and night! When our side faces the Sun, its day. When it faces away, its night. Show both on your M5Stick!
story_text: Every day the sun comes up and the sky gets bright. Then at night, the sun goes away and we see the moon and stars. But why? Chip discovered that the Earth is spinning like a top! Let's explore the cycle of day and night!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Day & Night!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0xFFFF00)
Lcd.fillCircle(100, 30, 18, 0xFF9800)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x000000, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.drawString("DAYTIME!", 25, 40)
Lcd.drawString("Sun is up!", 20, 60)
Lcd.drawString("Light & warm", 10, 80)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000022)
Lcd.fillCircle(100, 30, 15, 0xCCCCCC)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000022)
Lcd.drawString("NIGHTTIME!", 20, 50)
Lcd.drawString("Moon & stars", 15, 70)
Lcd.fillCircle(20, 20, 2, 0xFFFFFF)
Lcd.fillCircle(50, 15, 2, 0xFFFFFF)
Lcd.fillCircle(80, 25, 2, 0xFFFFFF)
Lcd.drawString("Dark & cool", 15, 90)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000022)
Lcd.drawString("Earth spins!", 15, 120)

hints.text: The Earth SPINS like a top! The side facing the Sun has DAYTIME, the other side has NIGHTTIME.
Show DAYTIME: bright yellow background, orange sun, text saying its light and warm!
Then show NIGHTTIME: dark blue background, gray moon, white star dots. Its dark and cool!
Earth takes 24 hours to spin once. Thats why we have day and night every single day!
Add a message about Earth spinning. The Sun doesnt move -- WE spin!

questions.hint: Think about your day from when you wake up to when you go to sleep!

items.text: Morning -- time for breakfast and school
Noon -- the sun is high in the sky
Afternoon -- the sun starts going down
Sunset -- the sky turns orange and pink
Night -- the moon and stars come out

questions.prompt: Put the parts of a day in the right order, starting from morning!

questions.hint: Earth keeps spinning! When your side faces the sun, it is day. When it faces away, it is night.
questions.prompt: Why do we have day and night?

options.text: Someone turns the sun on and off
The Earth spins, so different sides face the sun
The sun gets tired and takes a nap

questions.hint: The other side of Earth is facing away from the sun, so it is dark there!
questions.prompt: When it is daytime where you live, what is it on the other side of Earth?

title: The Moon and Stars
description: The night sky is full of wonders! The Moon changes shape through the month and stars twinkle. Display the night sky on your M5Stick!
story_text: When the sun goes to sleep, the night sky puts on an amazing show! The moon glows bright and thousands of stars twinkle. Chip loves stargazing! Did you know the moon changes shape? Let's find out why!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">67</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">25</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">white</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000022)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000022)
Lcd.drawString("Night Sky!", 25, 5)
Lcd.fillCircle(67, 60, 20, 0xCCCCCC)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000022)
Lcd.drawString("Moon", 52, 85)
stars = [(15,20),(30,35),(100,25),(110,15),(45,12),(85,40),(120,50)]
for x, y in stars:
    Lcd.fillCircle(x, y, 2, 0xFFFFFF)
Lcd.drawString("Stars twinkle!", 10, 110)
Lcd.drawString("Moon reflects", 10, 130)
Lcd.drawString("sunlight!", 25, 148)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000022)
Lcd.drawString("Look up tonight!", 2, 175)

hints.text: The NIGHT SKY has the Moon and millions of stars! The Moon is the closest thing to Earth in space.
Use a dark blue background for the night sky. Draw the Moon as a big gray circle.
Add STARS: tiny white dots scattered around. Real stars are actually huge -- they just look tiny because they are SO far away!
The Moon doesnt make its own light -- it REFLECTS sunlight! Like a mirror in space.
Encourage looking at the real sky! The Moon changes shape over the month. Can you spot it tonight?

cards.back: No! The moon reflects light from the sun. It is like a big mirror in the sky.
cards.front: Does the moon make its own light?

cards.back: As the moon moves around Earth, we see different amounts of the sunny side. These are called moon phases!
cards.front: Why does the moon look different each night?

cards.back: A full moon looks like a big, round, bright circle. We can see the whole sunny side!
A crescent moon looks like a curved sliver or banana shape. We only see a small piece of the sunny side.
Stars are giant balls of hot, glowing gas -- just like our sun, but very far away!
Starlight travels through Earth's air, which bends and wiggles the light. That makes stars look like they are twinkling!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about the moon and stars!

questions.hint: The moon does not glow on its own. Sunlight bounces off it and reaches our eyes!
questions.prompt: The moon gets its light from...

options.text: The sun -- moonlight is reflected sunlight

questions.hint: When we see the whole lit-up side of the moon, it is a perfect circle!
questions.prompt: What shape is a full moon?

questions.hint: Every star is a sun! They just look small because they are so far away.
questions.prompt: Stars are really...

options.text: Tiny lights stuck to the sky
Giant balls of hot gas, like faraway suns

questions.hint: A clear, dark sky is perfect for seeing the moon and stars!
questions.prompt: We can see the moon and stars best when...

options.text: It is nighttime and the sky is clear

title: Weather Watch!
description: Build a weather station! Use the tilt sensor on your M5Stick as a pretend thermometer. Tilt it to show different temperatures!
story_text: Is it sunny today? Rainy? Snowy? Chip checks the weather every morning to decide what to wear. Rain means an umbrella, snow means a warm coat! Let's become weather watchers and learn about all kinds of weather!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Weather!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">25</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Weather Station!", 2, 5)
Lcd.drawRect(60, 30, 15, 120, 0xFFFFFF)
Lcd.fillCircle(67, 155, 10, 0xFF0000)
tilt = Imu.getAccel()[1]
temp = int(50 + tilt * 30)
h = max(5, min(110, temp + 20))
Lcd.fillRect(62, 150 - h, 11, h, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString(str(temp) + " F", 80, 80)
if temp > 70:
    Lcd.drawString("HOT!", 85, 100)
    Lcd.drawString("Sunny day!", 5, 170)
elif temp > 40:
    Lcd.drawString("Nice!", 85, 100)
    Lcd.drawString("Perfect day!", 5, 170)
else:
    Lcd.drawString("Cold!", 85, 100)
    Lcd.drawString("Brrr! Bundle up!", 2, 170)

hints.text: Weather scientists use THERMOMETERS to measure temperature! Lets build one on screen.
Draw a thermometer: a tall rectangle outline with a red circle at the bottom (the bulb).
The red bar inside shows the temperature. Higher bar = hotter! Use the tilt sensor to control it.
Display the temperature number. If its high, show "HOT!" if medium, "Nice!" if low, "Cold!"
Real weather scientists measure temperature every day. Tilt your device to see the thermometer change!

activities.prompt: Match each type of weather to the right clothing!

questions.hint: Clouds are made of tiny water drops. When the drops get big enough, they fall as rain!
questions.prompt: Where does rain come from?

options.text: Someone pours water from the sky
Clouds -- water drops in clouds get heavy and fall down
The ocean jumps up into the air

questions.hint: When it rains, water collects on the ground and makes puddles!
questions.prompt: What kind of weather makes puddles on the ground?

questions.hint: When it is cold enough, water drops in clouds freeze into beautiful ice crystals -- snowflakes!
questions.prompt: Snow is really...

options.text: Tiny ice crystals that fall from clouds when it is very cold
White sand falling from the sky

questions.hint: Knowing the weather helps us wear the right clothes and stay safe!
questions.prompt: Why is it important to know the weather?

options.text: So we can dress right and plan our day
So we know what to eat for lunch

title: The Four Seasons
description: Earth has 4 seasons: Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter. Each has different weather and nature. Show all 4 seasons on your M5Stick!
story_text: Did you know the year has four big parts called seasons? Spring is when flowers bloom, summer is hot and sunny, fall has crunchy leaves, and winter brings snow! Chip loves every season. Let's explore them all!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">4 Seasons!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("4 Seasons!", 20, 5)
seasons = [("Spring", 0x00FF00, "Flowers bloom!"), ("Summer", 0xFFFF00, "Hot & sunny!"), ("Fall", 0xFF9800, "Leaves change!"), ("Winter", 0xFFFFFF, "Cold & snowy!")]
y = 28
for name, color, desc in seasons:
    Lcd.fillCircle(18, y + 5, 8, color)
    Lcd.setTextColor(color, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 32, y)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(desc, 32, y + 14)
    Speaker.tone(350 + seasons.index((name, color, desc)) * 50, 150)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 32

hints.text: Earth tilts as it orbits the Sun, giving us 4 SEASONS! Each one brings different weather and changes in nature.
SPRING (green): Flowers bloom, baby animals are born. Everything grows!
SUMMER (yellow): Hot and sunny, long days. FALL (orange): Leaves change color and fall.
WINTER (white): Cold, short days, maybe snow! Then spring comes again -- its a CYCLE!
Show each season with its color and a fun fact. The seasons go in order and repeat every year!

questions.hint: Think about the year: spring, then it gets hot, then leaves fall, then it gets cold!

items.text: Spring -- flowers bloom, baby animals are born
Summer -- hot and sunny, time for swimming
Fall -- leaves change color and drop from trees
Winter -- cold and snowy, time for warm cocoa

questions.prompt: Put the four seasons in order, starting with spring!

right.text: Leaves turn red, orange, and yellow
Snow falls and days are short

activities.prompt: Match each season to what happens during it!

questions.hint: Earth is tilted like a leaning top! As it travels around the sun, different parts get more or less sunlight.
questions.prompt: Why do we have different seasons?

options.text: Because Earth is tilted and moves around the sun
Because someone changes the weather machine

questions.hint: Summer days are long! The sun rises early and sets late, giving us lots of daylight.
questions.prompt: In which season do we have the MOST hours of daylight?

questions.hint: In fall, leaves change to beautiful reds, oranges, and yellows before dropping off!
questions.prompt: What happens to many trees in fall?

options.text: Their leaves turn colors and fall off

questions.hint: Spring is when nature wakes up! Flowers bloom and baby animals are born.
questions.prompt: In which season do many baby animals get born?

title: Feel the Beat!
description: Discover what a beat is! A beat is a steady pulse in music -- like a heartbeat. Build a program that makes your M5Stick play a drum beat pattern!
story_text: Hey there, little musician! Chip here! Did you know that music has a heartbeat? It's called a BEAT! Just like your heart goes thump-thump-thump, music has a steady beat too. Let's clap along and feel the beat together!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Feel the Beat!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">150</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Feel the Beat!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Listen...", 30, 60)
for i in range(4):
    Speaker.tone(200, 150)
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 140, 30, 0xFF9800)
    Lcd.drawString("BOOM!", 42, 130)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 140, 30, 0x000000)
    time.sleep(0.5)

hints.text: A beat is a steady pulse -- like your heartbeat! Click the orange Sound tab on the left to find the beat-making blocks.
Drag the "play tone" block into your program. Set the frequency to 200 for a deep drum sound, and the duration to 150 for a quick hit!
One beat is lonely! Click Loops and drag a "repeat" block. Put your tone block INSIDE the loop. Try repeating 4 times -- that is one measure of music!
Beats need SPACE between them! Add a "wait" block (from the Wait tab) AFTER your tone. Set it to 0.5 seconds. That is the rest between beats.
Make it visual! Add a Display block to show "BOOM!" each time the beat plays. Now click Run to hear your beat pattern!

questions.hint: Think about your heartbeat -- it keeps going over and over!
questions.prompt: What is a beat in music?

options.text: A steady pulse that keeps going

questions.hint: Put your hand on your chest. What do you feel?
questions.prompt: What part of your body has its own beat?

questions.hint: Try clapping your hands one... two... three... four!
questions.prompt: How can you show a beat with your hands?

options.text: Clap them together over and over

questions.hint: A steady beat is something that repeats the same way over and over.
questions.prompt: A clock goes tick-tock, tick-tock. Is that a steady beat?

options.text: Yes! It repeats the same way each time.
Only if it's a music clock.

questions.hint: A steady beat happens at the same speed every time.
questions.prompt: Which sound has a steady beat?

options.text: A dog barking whenever it wants
A drum hit at the same speed: boom, boom, boom

title: Fast and Slow
description: Tempo means how fast or slow music plays! Fast music makes you want to run, slow music is calm. Program your M5Stick to play the same beat at different speeds!
story_text: Chip loves to dance! Sometimes Chip dances really fast like a bunny hopping, and sometimes Chip moves slow like a sleepy turtle. That's called TEMPO! Let's find out which things are fast and which are slow!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Fast and Slow!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Fast and Slow!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("SLOW tempo:", 10, 40)
for i in range(4):
    Speaker.tone(440, 200)
    time.sleep(1)
Lcd.drawString("FAST tempo:", 10, 80)
for i in range(8):
    Speaker.tone(440, 100)
    time.sleep(0.25)

hints.text: Tempo is the SPEED of music. Slow tempo = relaxed (like walking). Fast tempo = exciting (like running)! Lets try both!
Start with a SLOW beat: play tone at 440 for 200ms, then wait 1 whole second. Repeat 4 times with a loop.
Now add a FAST section below: same tone but wait only 0.25 seconds between beats. Repeat 8 times!
Can you hear the difference? The SLOW part has big gaps between beats, the FAST part has tiny gaps!
Add display text to label "SLOW" and "FAST" sections. Tempo is just the speed between beats!

activities.prompt: Match each thing to how fast it goes!

questions.hint: It starts with the letter T!
questions.prompt: What is the word for how fast or slow music plays?

questions.hint: If the music is slow, the tempo is...
