questions.hint: Think about how a lullaby makes you feel. Calm and sleepy!
questions.prompt: A lullaby at bedtime is usually...

title: Rhythm Patterns
description: Create your own rhythm! A rhythm is a pattern of beats -- some long, some short, some with silence. Build a cool rhythm pattern on your M5Stick!
story_text: Chip found something cool! In music, sounds make PATTERNS that repeat over and over! Like clap-clap-stomp, clap-clap-stomp! Your job is to figure out the pattern and keep it going. Ready?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_buzzer_tone" x="20" y="20"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">300</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">150</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_wait"><value name="SECONDS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">0.2</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF00FF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("My Rhythm!", 20, 10)
for i in range(3):
    Speaker.tone(300, 150)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    Speaker.tone(300, 150)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    Speaker.tone(300, 400)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    time.sleep(0.3)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("da-da-DAAA!", 20, 60)

hints.text: A rhythm combines short hits, long hits, and rests (silence). Think of it like a drum pattern!
Start with two SHORT beats: tone 300 for 150ms each, with tiny 0.2 second waits.
Then add one LONG beat: tone 300 for 400ms. Short-short-LONG makes a fun rhythm!
Add a longer wait (0.3 seconds) at the end before the pattern repeats. Thats a REST in music!
Wrap it all in a repeat loop (3 times). Display "da-da-DAAA!" to show your rhythm!

questions.prompt: Put these sounds in order to make the pattern: CLAP - TAP - CLAP - TAP
Put these sounds in order: BOOM - BOOM - SNAP - BOOM - BOOM - SNAP
Put these in order: CLAP - CLAP - STOMP - REST

questions.hint: The pattern starts over! After TAP comes...
questions.prompt: The pattern is: CLAP - TAP - CLAP - TAP. What comes next?

questions.hint: Patterns repeat the same sounds over and over.
questions.prompt: What is a rhythm pattern?

options.text: Sounds that repeat in the same order

title: Move to the Beat!
description: Music makes you want to move! Different beats inspire different movements. Program a dance beat with a strong downbeat that makes you want to clap and stomp!
story_text: Time to get moving! Chip wants to dance, and Chip needs YOUR help to pick the right moves! When music is fast, we move fast. When music is slow, we move slow. Let's match our moves to the music!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Dance!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">35</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">purple</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">250</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF00FF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Move to Beat!", 10, 10)
for i in range(4):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 40, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF0000, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("STOMP!", 35, 55)
    Speaker.tone(250, 200)
    time.sleep(0.3)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 40, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("clap!", 40, 55)
    Speaker.tone(500, 100)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 40, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x0000FF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("clap!", 40, 55)
    Speaker.tone(500, 100)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    time.sleep(0.3)

hints.text: Dance music has a pattern: a STRONG beat (stomp!) followed by lighter beats (clap clap). Lets build that pattern!
The strong beat (STOMP) uses a LOW tone: 250 for 200ms. Low sounds feel powerful like stomping your feet!
The lighter beats (clap clap) use a HIGHER tone: 500 for 100ms each. Quick and light like clapping!
Pattern: STOMP-clap-clap, rest. Put it in a loop that repeats 4 times for a full dance phrase!
Add color-coded display text: red STOMP, green clap. Now try actually stomping and clapping along!

questions.hint: Fast music means fast movements!

questions.hint: Slow music means slow, calm movements.

questions.hint: Marching means stepping with a strong, steady beat!
questions.prompt: A marching song plays. How would you move?

options.text: March with big steps: LEFT, RIGHT, LEFT, RIGHT

questions.hint: When the music speeds up, your body speeds up too!

options.text: Speed up your movements to match!

title: Long and Short Sounds
description: Notes can be long or short! A whole note lasts a long time, a quarter note is quick. Program your M5Stick to play notes of different lengths!
story_text: Chip just learned something amazing! Some sounds last a loooong time, like a church bell ringing. And some sounds are super quick, like a woodpecker tapping. Let's sort sounds into long and short!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Note Lengths!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">1000</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Note Lengths!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Whole note:", 10, 35)
Speaker.tone(440, 1000)
time.sleep(1.2)
Lcd.drawString("Half note:", 10, 55)
Speaker.tone(440, 500)
time.sleep(0.7)
Lcd.drawString("Quarter note:", 10, 75)
Speaker.tone(440, 250)
time.sleep(0.4)
Lcd.drawString("Eighth notes:", 10, 95)
for i in range(4):
    Speaker.tone(440, 125)
    time.sleep(0.15)
Lcd.drawString("Shorter = faster!", 5, 120)

hints.text: Notes have different lengths! A whole note is looong, a half note is half that, a quarter note is quick, and eighth notes are super fast!
Start with a whole note: play tone 440 for 1000ms. Thats a full one-second note! Nice and long.
Now a half note: 500ms. Then a quarter note: 250ms. Each is half as long as the last!
For eighth notes, play 125ms tones quickly in a row with tiny waits. They sound like rapid beats!
Label each one on the display. See the pattern? 1000, 500, 250, 125 -- each divides by 2! Math in music!

activities.prompt: Match each sound to whether it is LONG or SHORT!

questions.hint: A siren keeps going and going -- that's a long sound!
questions.prompt: Which of these is a LONG sound?

options.text: A fire truck siren going WHEEEE
A quick knock on a door

questions.hint: One quick bark is over really fast!
questions.prompt: Which of these is a SHORT sound?

title: High and Low Sounds
description: Explore pitch! High sounds have fast vibrations and low sounds have slow vibrations. Program your M5Stick to play high-pitched and low-pitched tones so you can hear the difference!
story_text: Hey friend! Chip here! Did you know sounds can be HIGH like a tiny bird tweeting, or LOW like a big bear growling? Let's explore high and low sounds and see if you can tell them apart!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">High and Low!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">880</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">500</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("High and Low!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("High note:", 10, 40)
Speaker.tone(880, 500)
time.sleep(0.6)
Lcd.drawString("Low note:", 10, 70)
Speaker.tone(220, 500)
time.sleep(0.6)
Lcd.drawString("High again:", 10, 100)
Speaker.tone(880, 500)
time.sleep(0.6)
Lcd.drawString("Low again:", 10, 130)
Speaker.tone(220, 500)

hints.text: Pitch means how HIGH or LOW a sound is. A bird chirps HIGH, a bear growls LOW! Open the Sound tab to find tone blocks.
Drag a "play tone" block in. Set frequency to 880 -- that is a HIGH sound! Press Run to hear it.
Now add ANOTHER tone block below. Set this one to 220 -- that is LOW! The bigger the number, the higher the pitch.
Add a "wait" block between your two tones (0.5 seconds) so you can hear each note separately.
Add Display blocks to label each sound -- write "High!" and "Low!" so you can see AND hear the difference!

left.text: A tiny bird singing: tweet tweet!

activities.prompt: Match each sound to HIGH or LOW!

questions.hint: Mice are tiny and make tiny, high squeaky sounds!

questions.hint: Big instruments usually make lower sounds.

title: Loud and Soft
description: Dynamics in music means how LOUD or SOFT you play! Program your M5Stick to play sounds at different volumes -- from a whisper to a shout!
story_text: Shhh... can you hear that? Some sounds are very soft, like a whisper. And some sounds are VERY LOUD, like a drum! Musicians call this DYNAMICS. Let's learn when music is loud and when it's soft!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Volume!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">300</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Loud and Soft!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Soft... (piano)", 10, 40)
Speaker.tone(440, 100)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Medium (mezzo)", 10, 65)
Speaker.tone(440, 300)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("LOUD! (forte)", 10, 90)
Speaker.tone(440, 600)
time.sleep(0.8)
Lcd.drawString("Soft again...", 10, 115)
Speaker.tone(440, 100)
time.sleep(0.3)

hints.text: In music, soft is called PIANO and loud is called FORTE. Musicians use these Italian words! Lets make soft and loud sounds.
A soft sound is short and gentle. Play tone 440 for only 100ms -- thats like a whisper!
A medium sound lasts longer: 300ms. And a LOUD sound is even longer: 600ms. Try all three!
Put them in order: soft, medium, loud. Thats called a CRESCENDO -- getting louder!
End with a soft note again. Label each one on the display: piano, mezzo, forte. Now you know dynamics!

left.text: A butterfly landing on a flower
A secret whisper to a friend
A big drum: BANG BANG BANG!
Rain drops gently tapping a window

activities.prompt: Match each sound to LOUD or SOFT!

questions.hint: It starts with the letter D!
questions.prompt: What is the musical word for how loud or soft music is?

questions.hint: Libraries are quiet places!
questions.prompt: You are in a library. Should you play music loud or soft?

options.text: Very loud so everyone can hear!
Very soft so you don't disturb others

questions.hint: Parties are exciting and full of energy!
questions.prompt: At a birthday party, the music is usually...

title: Sounds Around Us
description: Sounds are everywhere! Birds chirp high, thunder rumbles low. Use your M5Stick to create sound effects that imitate things you hear every day!
story_text: Chip loves listening! The world is full of sounds -- birds singing, cars honking, leaves rustling! Every sound is different. Some are high, some are low, some are loud, and some are soft. Let's explore the sounds around us!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Sound Effects!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">800</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sound Effects!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Bird:", 10, 35)
Speaker.tone(800, 200)
time.sleep(0.1)
Speaker.tone(1000, 150)
time.sleep(0.1)
Speaker.tone(800, 200)
time.sleep(0.8)
Lcd.drawString("Doorbell:", 10, 55)
Speaker.tone(523, 400)
time.sleep(0.1)
Speaker.tone(392, 600)
time.sleep(0.8)
Lcd.drawString("Alarm:", 10, 75)
for i in range(4):
    Speaker.tone(880, 200)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    Speaker.tone(660, 200)
    time.sleep(0.2)

hints.text: Listen to sounds around you! A bird chirps HIGH and quick. Thunder is LOW and long. Lets make sound effects!
For a bird chirp: use HIGH frequencies (800, 1000) with SHORT durations (200ms, 150ms). Quick and high!
For a doorbell: two notes -- ding (523) dong (392). The first note is higher, the second is lower and longer.
For an alarm: alternate between two tones (880 and 660) rapidly! Use a loop to repeat it.
Label each sound effect on the display. What other sounds can you create with different frequencies?

cards.back: Birds make HIGH and SOFT sounds. They tweet and chirp to talk to each other!
Thunder makes a LOW and LOUD rumbling sound. It happens when lightning heats the air super fast!
Rain makes a SOFT tapping sound. It can be fast or slow depending on how hard it rains!
A car horn is LOUD and HIGH! Drivers honk to say 'watch out!' It gets your attention fast.
Wind can be SOFT like a gentle breeze or LOUD like a big storm. It makes a whooshing sound!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about everyday sounds!
Match each everyday sound to where you hear it!

title: Do Re Mi
description: Sing and play Do Re Mi! This famous musical scale is used everywhere. Program your M5Stick to play the full Do Re Mi scale with colorful display!
story_text: Chip is so excited! Today we're learning our very first musical NOTES! Notes have special names. The first three are Do, Re, and Mi. They go from low to high, like climbing up stairs. Let's meet them!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Do Re Mi!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">262</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">500</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Do Re Mi!", 25, 10)
colors = [0xFF0000, 0xFF9800, 0xFFFF00, 0x00FF00, 0x0000FF, 0xFF00FF, 0xFFFFFF]
notes = [(262,"Do"),(294,"Re"),(330,"Mi"),(349,"Fa"),(392,"Sol"),(440,"La"),(494,"Ti")]
for i in range(len(notes)):
    freq, name = notes[i]
    Lcd.setTextColor(colors[i], 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 50, 35 + i * 17)
    Speaker.tone(freq, 500)
    time.sleep(0.55)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Do!", 50, 35 + 7 * 17)
Speaker.tone(523, 700)

hints.text: Do Re Mi Fa Sol La Ti Do! These are the solfege notes that musicians all over the world use to learn music.
Start with Do = 262. Then Re = 294, Mi = 330. Each goes a step higher on the musical staircase!
Continue: Fa = 349, Sol = 392, La = 440, Ti = 494. Almost at the top!
End with high Do = 523 (double of 262!). Make this last note longer to show you reached the top!
Give each note a different color on the display -- like a rainbow scale! Do Re Mi is colorful!

cards.back: Do is the LOWEST of the three notes. It's like the bottom step of a staircase. Sing it low: Dooooo!
Re is the MIDDLE note. It's one step higher than Do. Sing it a little higher: Reeeee!
Mi is the HIGHEST of the three. It's one step higher than Re. Sing it up high: Miiiiii!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to meet the first three musical notes!

questions.hint: It's at the bottom of the musical stairs!
questions.prompt: Which note is the LOWEST?

questions.hint: It's at the top of the musical stairs!
questions.prompt: Which note is the HIGHEST of the three?

questions.hint: Start at the bottom and climb up: Do... Re... Mi!
questions.prompt: What order do the first three notes go in, from low to high?

questions.hint: Each note goes a little higher, like climbing stairs!
questions.prompt: The notes Do, Re, Mi are like...

title: Musical Patterns
description: Patterns in music are like patterns in math! AABB, ABAB, ABBA -- each makes a different musical feeling. Program different musical patterns!
story_text: Chip LOVES patterns! In music, patterns are everywhere! A pattern is when something repeats: like Do-Mi-Do-Mi or Clap-Clap-Stomp, Clap-Clap-Stomp. Let's find and finish some musical patterns!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_buzzer_tone" x="20" y="20"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">300</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">330</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">300</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Music Patterns!", 5, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("ABAB pattern:", 5, 35)
for i in range(4):
    Speaker.tone(440, 300)
    time.sleep(0.35)
    Speaker.tone(330, 300)
    time.sleep(0.35)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("AABB pattern:", 5, 60)
for i in range(2):
    Speaker.tone(440, 300)
    time.sleep(0.35)
    Speaker.tone(440, 300)
    time.sleep(0.35)
    Speaker.tone(330, 300)
    time.sleep(0.35)
    Speaker.tone(330, 300)
    time.sleep(0.35)

hints.text: Music patterns are like color patterns! A and B are two different notes. ABAB means they take turns.
Choose note A (440) and note B (330). For ABAB: play A, B, A, B in order!
Now try AABB: play A, A, B, B. Same notes but a different pattern -- does it sound different?
Put each pattern in a repeat loop so it plays a few times. Label each one on the display.
Can you hear how ABAB sounds bouncy but AABB sounds more steady? Patterns change the feel of music!

questions.prompt: Put the notes in order: Do - Mi - Do - Mi
Put the rhythm in order: CLAP - CLAP - REST - CLAP - CLAP - REST
Put the notes in order: Do - Re - Mi - Do - Re - Mi

questions.hint: The pattern goes Do, Mi, Do, then what?
questions.sentence: The pattern is: Do - Mi - Do - ___

questions.hint: The pattern repeats: CLAP, CLAP, then what?
questions.sentence: The pattern is: CLAP - CLAP - STOMP - CLAP - CLAP - ___

questions.hint: After A always comes...
questions.sentence: An AB pattern goes: A, B, A, B, A, ___

title: Rainbow Colors!
description: Explore the rainbow! Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple -- paint each color of the rainbow on your M5Stick screen as colorful stripes!
story_text: Wow, look at that! Chip sees a rainbow! Rainbows have special colors that always go in the same order. Let's learn all the beautiful colors of the rainbow together!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_rect"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">0</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">0</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">135</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">red</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 0, 135, 30, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 30, 0xFF9800)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 60, 135, 30, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 90, 135, 30, 0x00FF00)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 120, 135, 30, 0x0000FF)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 150, 135, 30, 0xFF00FF)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Rainbow!", 30, 190)

hints.text: A rainbow has bands of color! Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple. Lets paint each one as a stripe on screen!
Use the "draw rectangle" block with FILL checked. Make it full width (135) and about 30 pixels tall. Start with RED!
Add more rectangles below, each at a different Y position: 0, 30, 60, 90, 120, 150. Change the color each time!
The color order for a rainbow is: Red, Orange (use yellow block), Yellow, Green, Blue, Purple.
Add a label at the bottom. You just painted a rainbow with code! Art + coding = pixel art!

back.text: Red is bright and bold! Find it on fire trucks 🚒, apples 🍎, and ladybugs 🐞
Orange is warm and happy! Find it on oranges 🍊, pumpkins 🎃, and goldfish 🐠
Yellow is sunny and cheerful! Find it on the sun ☀️, bananas 🍌, and school buses 🚌
Green is the color of nature! Find it on grass 🌿, leaves 🍃, and frogs 🐸
Blue is cool and calm! Find it in the sky 🌤️, the ocean 🌊, and blueberries 🫐
Purple is magical and special! Find it on grapes 🍇, eggplants 🍆, and butterflies 🦋

activities.prompt: Flip each card to see a rainbow color and where to find it!
Match each color to something that is that color!

title: Shapes Everywhere!
description: Shapes are the building blocks of art! Circles, rectangles, squares -- combine them to make pictures. Draw a house using only shapes!
story_text: Chip sees shapes EVERYWHERE! The wheels on a bus are circles, a window is a rectangle, and a slice of pizza is a triangle! Let's learn all about shapes and find them in the world!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_rect"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">80</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">70</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">red</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Shape House!", 15, 5)
Lcd.fillRect(30, 80, 70, 60, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillRect(52, 55, 26, 30, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillRect(50, 100, 20, 25, 0x0000FF)
Lcd.fillRect(75, 90, 15, 15, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.fillCircle(65, 40, 15, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("House!", 40, 150)

hints.text: Every picture can be made from SHAPES! A house is rectangles and a circle. Lets build one!
Start with a big red rectangle for the house walls: (30, 80) width 70 height 60.
Add a smaller rectangle on top for the roof peak. Then a blue rectangle for the door.
Add a small yellow square for the window. And a yellow circle above the house for the sun!
Label it! You built a house using only shapes. Artists start with simple shapes too, then add details!

cards.back: A circle is perfectly round with no corners at all! Think of a ball, a cookie, or the moon.
A square has 4 equal sides and 4 corners! Think of a window, a cracker, or a block.
A triangle has 3 sides and 3 corners! Think of a slice of pizza, a roof, or a yield sign.
A rectangle has 4 sides and 4 corners, but two sides are longer! Think of a door, a book, or a phone.

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about a basic shape!
Match each shape to something that looks like it!

story_text: Pop quiz time! Chip wants to see how many colors you know. Don't worry -- this is going to be super fun! Let's see if you can spot the right colors. Ready? Let's go!

solution_code:

Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("My Fave Colors!", 5, 10)
Lcd.fillRect(10, 35, 115, 35, 0x0000FF)

hints.text: Everyone has favorite colors! Pick your top 3 and lets display them as big colorful bars on screen!
Clear the screen and add a title. Then draw a big filled rectangle for your #1 favorite color!
Below it, draw another rectangle in your #2 color. Then a third for #3. Stack them down the screen!
Write the color name INSIDE each rectangle. Use white text so it shows up on any background!
You made a personal color chart! Art is about expressing yourself -- your color choices are unique to YOU!

title: Shapes in Our World
description: Look around -- shapes are everywhere! A window is a rectangle, a wheel is a circle, a slice of pizza is a triangle. Draw things from the real world using shapes!
story_text: Guess what, friend? Shapes are HIDING everywhere! A pizza box is a square, a coin is a circle, and a roof is a triangle. Chip is going on a shape hunt -- come along and see how many shapes you can spot!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">67</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">25</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Real Shapes!", 15, 5)
Lcd.fillCircle(35, 50, 18, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)

Lcd.fillRect(80, 35, 35, 35, 0x0000FF)
Lcd.drawString("Window", 80, 75)

Lcd.fillRect(32, 120, 6, 25, 0xFF0000)

Lcd.fillRect(85, 105, 20, 30, 0xFFFF00)

hints.text: Shapes are everywhere in real life! A clock face is a circle, a window is a square. Lets draw real things with shapes!
Draw a yellow circle -- thats a clock! Add a label below it.
Draw a blue square -- thats a window! Put it next to the clock.
Try a lollipop: circle on top of a thin rectangle stick! And a TV: rectangle with a smaller one inside.
You found shapes in the real world and recreated them with code! Artists see shapes in everything!

title: Types of Lines
description: Lines are everywhere in art! Horizontal, vertical, diagonal, thick, thin. Draw different types of lines on your M5Stick using rectangles!
story_text: Did you know there are different kinds of lines? Chip learned about straight lines, curvy lines, zigzag lines, and wavy lines! They're all around us. Let's learn about each one!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_rect"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">40</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">115</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">3</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">red</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Types of Lines!", 5, 10)
Lcd.fillRect(10, 40, 115, 3, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Horizontal", 30, 48)
Lcd.fillRect(67, 65, 3, 80, 0x00FF00)
Lcd.drawString("Vertical", 75, 100)
Lcd.fillRect(10, 155, 60, 8, 0x0000FF)
Lcd.drawString("Thick", 15, 168)
Lcd.fillRect(80, 155, 50, 2, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.drawString("Thin", 85, 162)

hints.text: Artists use many types of lines! A horizontal line goes left-right, a vertical line goes up-down. Lets draw them!
A horizontal line is a WIDE but SHORT rectangle: width=115, height=3. Thats a thin horizontal line!
A vertical line is a TALL but NARROW rectangle: width=3, height=80. Same idea, just rotated!
Try different thicknesses: height=2 for thin, height=8 for thick. Lines can be any thickness!
Label each line type on the display. Artists choose line types to create different feelings in their art!

cards.back: A straight line goes in one direction without bending. Think of a ruler, a pencil, or a table edge!
A curved line bends smoothly, like a smile, a rainbow, or the letter C!
A zigzag line goes back and forth with sharp points, like a lightning bolt or mountain tops!
A wavy line goes up and down smoothly, like ocean waves or a slithering snake!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about a type of line!

questions.hint: A rainbow bends smoothly across the sky!
questions.prompt: A rainbow in the sky is what kind of line?

questions.hint: Lightning goes back and forth with sharp, pointy turns!
questions.prompt: A lightning bolt looks like what kind of line?

questions.hint: Waves go up and down smoothly -- not with sharp points.
questions.prompt: Ocean waves look like what kind of line?

questions.hint: Rulers help you draw lines that don't bend!
questions.prompt: The edge of a ruler is what kind of line?

title: Patterns All Around
description: Patterns are everywhere in nature and art! Stripes, polka dots, checks. Create a polka dot pattern on your M5Stick using loops and circles!
story_text: Chip found a secret about art: PATTERNS make everything look awesome! A pattern is when something repeats -- like red-blue-red-blue or circle-star-circle-star. Let's make some patterns!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="controls_repeat_ext"><value name="TIMES"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">4</field></shadow></value><statement name="DO"><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">8</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">white</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></statement></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x0000FF)
colors = [0xFF0000, 0xFFFF00, 0x00FF00, 0xFFFFFF]
y = 25
for row in range(5):
    for col in range(4):
        x = 20 + col * 30
        c = colors[(row + col) % 4]
        Lcd.fillCircle(x, y, 8, c)
    y = y + 30
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Polka Dots!", 20, 180)

hints.text: A polka dot pattern is circles repeated in rows! Each row has dots spaced evenly apart.
Fill the screen with a blue background first. Then draw white filled circles with radius 8.
Space dots 30 pixels apart horizontally. Use a loop to draw 4 dots in a row.
Then move down 30 pixels and draw another row. Use different colors for variety!
5 rows of 4 dots = 20 dots total! But with loops you only wrote the code once. Patterns love loops!

title: Color Patterns
description: Color patterns follow rules! Red-Blue-Red-Blue or Red-Red-Blue-Red-Red-Blue. Create a color pattern display that shows the rule and repeats it!
story_text: Wow, you did great with patterns! Now Chip has a trickier challenge. Some colors are MISSING from the pattern! Can you figure out which color goes in the blank spot? Think about what repeats!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_rect"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">40</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">red</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Color Pattern!", 10, 10)
pattern = [0xFF0000, 0x00FF00, 0x0000FF]
for rep in range(2):
    for i in range(3):
        x = 5 + (rep * 3 + i) * 21
        Lcd.fillRect(x, 40, 18, 18, pattern[i])
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("R-G-B R-G-B", 10, 70)
Lcd.drawString("Next is...", 20, 95)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.fillRect(5, 115, 18, 18, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("RED!", 30, 118)
Speaker.tone(523, 300)

hints.text: A color pattern has a RULE that repeats! Red-Green-Blue, Red-Green-Blue... what comes next?

Display the pattern name below: R-G-B R-G-B. This helps you SEE the repeating unit.
Ask "Whats next?" then reveal the answer after a pause -- RED! Because the pattern starts over.
Play a success sound! Recognizing the REPEATING UNIT is the key to solving any pattern!

title: Shape Patterns
description: Create shape patterns! Circle-Triangle-Circle-Triangle or Circle-Circle-Square. Draw the pattern and predict what comes next!
story_text: Chip loves shapes! Circles, squares, triangles -- they can all make patterns! Sometimes shapes take turns just like colors do. Can you put these shapes in the right order to finish the pattern?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">12</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">blue</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Shape Pattern!", 10, 10)
for i in range(5):
    x = 13 + i * 25
    if i % 2 == 0:
        Lcd.fillCircle(x, 50, 10, 0x0000FF)
    else:
        Lcd.fillRect(x - 10, 40, 20, 20, 0xFF0000)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("O [] O [] O", 10, 75)
Lcd.drawString("Next: ?", 30, 95)
time.sleep(1.5)
Lcd.fillRect(13 + 5 * 25 - 10, 40, 20, 20, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("SQUARE!", 35, 118)
Speaker.tone(523, 300)

hints.text: Patterns can use shapes! Blue circle, red square, blue circle, red square... whats next?
Draw alternating circles and squares in a row. Blue circles, red squares.
After 5 shapes, pause and ask "What comes next?" The viewer needs to spot the rule!
The answer depends on the pattern! If the last was a circle, next is a square!
Patterns help us PREDICT. Scientists, artists, and programmers all use pattern recognition!

solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)

Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)

Lcd.drawString("3. Ate the cake", 5, 48)
Lcd.drawString("1. Mixed batter", 5, 63)
Lcd.drawString("2. Baked it", 5, 78)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 95, 135, 100, 0x000000)

hints.text: Stories go in ORDER! You cant eat a cake before baking it. Lets fix a mixed-up story!
Show the mixed-up events first: eating, mixing, baking -- all in wrong order!
Wait a moment, then show the CORRECT order: First mix, then bake, then eat!
Play a sound as each correct step appears. Higher pitch = closer to the end!
Sequencing (putting things in order) is a key problem-solving skill! It helps in coding too!

title: What Happens Next?
description: Predict outcomes! If you push a ball off a table, it falls. What happens next in different scenarios? Display cause-effect chains!
story_text: Here's a fun question: What happens if you drop an ice cream cone? It falls on the ground! That's called cause and effect. One thing CAUSES another thing to happen. Let's figure out what happens next!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">If... Then...</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Predict!", 35, 10)
pairs = [("IF it rains...", "THEN use umbrella!"), ("IF you study...", "THEN you learn!"), ("IF you press A...", "THEN beep!")]
y = 35
for cause, effect in pairs:
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(cause, 5, y)
    time.sleep(1)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(effect, 5, y + 16)
    Speaker.tone(400, 150)
    time.sleep(1)
    y = y + 42
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF00FF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Think ahead!", 15, 175)

hints.text: Predicting means thinking about WHAT HAPPENS NEXT. IF something happens, THEN something else follows!
Display an IF statement: "IF it rains..." then wait and show the THEN: "THEN use umbrella!"
Show another pair: IF you study, THEN you learn! Each cause has an effect.
Show a coding one: IF you press button A, THEN the device beeps! Same logic!
Predicting outcomes is how we make good decisions AND write good code. Think ahead!

title: Following Steps
description: Follow instructions carefully! Your M5Stick gives you steps to follow. Each step builds on the last one. Missing a step means trouble!
story_text: Chip is learning to follow directions! When someone tells you to do something, the ORDER matters. You can't frost a cake before you bake it! Help Chip put these instructions in the right order.
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Follow Along!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Follow Steps!", 10, 10)
steps = ["Step 1: Look!", "Step 2: Listen!", "Step 3: Think!", "Step 4: Do it!"]
actions = [(0xFF0000, 300), (0x00FF00, 400), (0x0000FF, 500), (0xFFFF00, 600)]
for i in range(4):
    Lcd.setTextColor(actions[i][0], 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(steps[i], 5, 35 + i * 22)
    Speaker.tone(actions[i][1], 200)
    if i == 0:
        Power.setLed(255)
    elif i == 3:
        Power.setLed(0)
    time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("4 steps done!", 10, 130)

hints.text: Following steps in ORDER is super important! Step 1 comes first, then Step 2. You cant skip ahead!
Display Step 1 in red with a sound. Turn on the LED to show you are paying attention!
Each step gets a different color and pitch. Step 2: green. Step 3: blue. Step 4: yellow.
The tones go UP in pitch (300, 400, 500, 600) as the steps progress. Building up to the end!
Show that all 4 steps are done. Following instructions carefully is how recipes, science experiments, and programs work!

title: Same and Different
description: Compare two things -- how are they the SAME and how are they DIFFERENT? Display two shapes and list their similarities and differences!
story_text: Time to be a super detective! Chip wants to know: how are things the SAME and how are they DIFFERENT? A cat and a dog are both animals (same!) but one barks and one meows (different!). Let's compare!

hints.text: COMPARING means finding what is SAME and what is DIFFERENT between two things. Lets compare two circles!
Draw a red circle on the left and a blue circle on the right. Same shape, different colors!
List what is SAME: both are circles, same size. Display this in green.
List what is DIFFERENT: different colors (red vs blue), different positions (left vs right).
Comparing helps us understand things better. Scientists compare, writers compare, coders compare data!

title: Simple Puzzles
description: Solve simple puzzles! What number is missing? Which shape completes the pattern? Display puzzles and reveal the answers!
story_text: You're a super problem solver now! Let's try some real puzzles. Chip will give you clues, and you figure out the answer. Think carefully about each clue -- they all help you find the answer!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Puzzle Time!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Puzzle Time!", 20, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("1 + ? = 4", 25, 30)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("? = 3!", 40, 48)
Speaker.tone(400, 200)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("A,B,C,?,E", 25, 75)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("? = D!", 40, 93)
Speaker.tone(500, 200)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("10,20,?,40", 20, 120)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("? = 30!", 35, 138)
Speaker.tone(600, 300)
Lcd.drawString("3 puzzles!", 25, 165)

hints.text: Puzzles are fun to solve! Each one has a question mark (?) that you need to figure out.
Puzzle 1: 1 + ? = 4. What number plus 1 equals 4? Think... the answer is 3!
Puzzle 2: A, B, C, ?, E. What letter comes after C? D! Its alphabetical order.
Puzzle 3: 10, 20, ?, 40. The pattern adds 10 each time. The answer is 30!
Each puzzle gets a higher-pitched sound. Numbers, letters, patterns -- puzzles come in all types!

title: What is a Computer?
description: Discover what a computer is! A computer follows instructions, just like your M5Stick. Program it to say hello and show that it does exactly what you tell it!
story_text: Hi friend! Chip here -- and guess what? I AM a computer! Well, a tiny one. Computers are everywhere -- in phones, cars, even refrigerators! Let's learn about what makes a computer a computer. Flip the cards to find out!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">I am a computer!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("I am a computer!", 5, 30)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("I follow your", 10, 60)
Lcd.drawString("instructions!", 10, 80)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Tell me what", 15, 40)
Lcd.drawString("to do!", 35, 65)
Power.setLed(255)
time.sleep(1)
Power.setLed(0)

hints.text: A computer is a machine that follows instructions! Your M5Stick is a tiny computer. Lets give it its first instruction!
Drag a "clear display" block first -- that cleans the screen so we start fresh. Like erasing a chalkboard!
Now add a "display text" block. Type "I am a computer!" -- you just told the computer what to show!
Add more display blocks below to write more messages. The computer reads your instructions from TOP to BOTTOM.
Try adding an LED block at the end! The computer does EVERYTHING you tell it, in ORDER. That is programming!

cards.back: Input is how you TELL a computer what to do. You use a keyboard, mouse, or touchscreen!
Output is how a computer SHOWS you things. The screen, speakers, and printer are all output!
The processor is the computer's BRAIN. It thinks really fast and follows instructions!
Memory is where a computer REMEMBERS things. Like your brain remembers your name!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about computers!

title: Giving Instructions
description: Computers need VERY specific instructions! Learn to give step-by-step commands by programming your M5Stick to draw a smiley face one step at a time!
story_text: Did you know that coding is just giving instructions? When you tell someone how to make a peanut butter sandwich, you're kind of coding! But here's the tricky part: computers need VERY clear instructions. Let's practice!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle" x="20" y="20"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">67</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">80</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.fillCircle(67, 80, 50, 0xFFFF00)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.fillCircle(50, 70, 6, 0x000000)
Lcd.fillCircle(84, 70, 6, 0x000000)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.fillRect(47, 95, 40, 5, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("I gave steps!", 10, 150)

hints.text: To draw a smiley, you need STEPS: 1) Draw face, 2) Add eyes, 3) Add mouth. Each step is one instruction!
Step 1: Clear the screen, then draw a big yellow circle for the face. Center it at x=67, y=80, radius=50.
Step 2: Draw two small BLACK circles for eyes. Left eye at (50, 70) and right eye at (84, 70), radius 6.
Step 3: Draw a rectangle for the mouth at (47, 95) width 40 height 5. Use black color!
Add a wait between steps to see it being drawn step by step. ORDER matters -- face first, then eyes, then mouth!

title: Be a Robot!
description: Be a robot that follows commands! Program a sequence of actions -- display messages, flash the LED, play sounds -- and watch your M5Stick follow them in order!
story_text: Beep boop! Chip wants you to pretend you are a ROBOT! Robots only move when you tell them how. Up, down, left, right -- those are your commands. Can you put the robot moves in the right order to reach the goal?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Robot Mode!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field><next><block type="m5_led_set"><field name="COLOR">on</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Robot Mode!", 15, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Step 1: LED on", 5, 35)
Power.setLed(255)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.drawString("Step 2: Beep!", 5, 55)
Speaker.tone(440, 300)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Step 3: LED off", 5, 75)
Power.setLed(0)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Step 4: Low beep", 5, 95)
Speaker.tone(220, 300)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Done! 4 steps!", 5, 120)

hints.text: A robot follows a LIST of commands in order. Lets make a list of things for your M5Stick robot to do!
Step 1: Turn the LED on! Drag an "LED set on" block. Your robot does what you say!
Step 2: Make a sound! Add a "play tone" block (440, 300ms). The robot beeps on command!
Step 3: LED off. Step 4: Another sound. Add waits between so you can see each step happen.
Display each step on screen as it happens. Your robot followed 4 instructions perfectly -- thats a program!

questions.prompt: The robot needs to go RIGHT then UP to reach the star. Put the moves in order!
The robot needs to go UP, UP, then RIGHT to get the cookie. Put the moves in order!
The robot needs to go RIGHT, RIGHT, DOWN to get the treasure. Put the moves in order!
The robot needs to go DOWN, LEFT, LEFT, UP to get home. Put the moves in order!

title: Bugs! (Finding Mistakes)
description: A bug is a mistake in your code! Programmers find and fix bugs every day. Build a program with a bug in it, then fix it to make it work correctly!
story_text: Uh oh! Chip found some BUGS! Not real bugs -- in coding, a "bug" is a mistake in your instructions. Even the best programmers make bugs! The important thing is learning to FIND them. Can you spot the bug?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Bug Hunt!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">red</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Fix the code!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF0000, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Bug Hunt!", 20, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Step 1: Clear", 5, 35)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Step 2: Title", 5, 55)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Step 3: Message", 5, 75)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Step 4: Sound", 5, 95)
Speaker.tone(440, 300)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Bug Fixed!", 20, 125)

hints.text: A BUG is when your program does something wrong. Maybe words appear in the wrong spot, or steps are in the wrong ORDER!
Look at the starter code. The display text appears but the screen was never cleared first! Add a "clear display" block at the TOP.
Order matters! If you put Step 2 before Step 1, things look wrong. Check that your blocks go in the RIGHT order.
Add a sound at the end. If no sound plays, the block might not be connected! Make sure blocks snap together.
When everything works in order: thats debugging! Every programmer finds and fixes bugs. You are a real coder now!

questions.hint: Can you eat the toast BEFORE it is toasted? Something is out of order!
questions.prompt: To make toast: 1) Put bread in toaster, 2) Eat the toast, 3) Push the lever down. Which step is in the wrong place?

options.text: You should put on socks BEFORE shoes

questions.hint: Compare the plan and what happened: Right, Right, Up vs Right, LEFT, Up. Which one changed?
questions.prompt: The robot was told: Move Right, Move Right, Move Up. But it went RIGHT, LEFT, UP! Which instruction had a bug?

options.text: The second Move Right (it went Left instead)

questions.hint: Steps 1, 2, and 3 all make sense. But step 4... do you eat flowers? That's the bug!
questions.prompt: To water a flower: 1) Fill the watering can with water, 2) Carry the can to the flower, 3) Pour water on the flower, 4) Eat the flower. What is the bug?

options.text: Step 4 is wrong -- you don't eat flowers!

questions.hint: It is the name of a tiny crawly creature. Programmers call mistakes...
questions.prompt: What do programmers call a mistake in code?

title: Everyday Algorithms
description: An algorithm is a step-by-step plan! Making a sandwich, brushing teeth -- these are all algorithms. Write an algorithm for your M5Stick to follow!
story_text: Big word alert: ALGORITHM! Don't worry, it just means a set of steps you follow to do something. Brushing your teeth is an algorithm! Getting dressed is an algorithm! You already know lots of algorithms. Let's practice!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Step 1</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">white</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Morning Algorithm", 2, 10)
steps = ["1. Wake up!", "2. Brush teeth", "3. Get dressed", "4. Eat breakfast", "5. Go to school!"]
y = 35
for step in steps:
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(step, 5, y)
    Speaker.tone(500, 100)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 20
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Algorithm done!", 5, y + 5)

hints.text: An ALGORITHM is just a list of steps in order. Your morning routine is an algorithm: wake up, brush teeth, get dressed, eat breakfast!
Start by clearing the screen and adding a title. Then display "Step 1" with a display text block.
Add more display text blocks for each step. Put them at different Y positions so they stack down the screen.
Add a wait and a beep between each step. This shows the computer doing one step at a time!
End with "Algorithm done!" Your M5Stick just followed your algorithm -- a step-by-step plan!

title: Step by Step
description: Break big tasks into small steps! Learn sequencing by programming your M5Stick to count from 1 to 5, showing each number one at a time with sounds!
story_text: Now you know what an algorithm is! Let's practice putting instructions in order -- just like a real programmer writes code. Each step does one small thing. Together, the steps do something awesome!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_number"><value name="NUMBER"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">1</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">55</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">white</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)

for i in range(1, 6):
    Lcd.fillRect(30, 50, 80, 50, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)

hints.text: Sequencing means doing things in ORDER: step 1, step 2, step 3... Lets count from 1 to 5 step by step!
Clear the screen first. Then use a "display number" block to show the number 1.
Add a wait (0.8 seconds) and a beep after each number. Then show 2, 3, 4, and 5.
Make each beep a slightly different pitch to hear the counting go up! Add 50 to the frequency each time.
Clear just the number area before showing the next one. Now your program steps through 1 to 5 in sequence!

title: Repeat After Me (Loops)
description: Loops save time! Instead of writing the same block 10 times, a loop repeats it for you. Use a loop to make your M5Stick blink the LED and beep 10 times!
story_text: Chip has a SUPER power: loops! A loop means doing the same thing again and again. Instead of saying "jump, jump, jump, jump, jump," you can say "jump 5 times!" That's a loop! Let's learn about this amazing shortcut!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="controls_repeat_ext" x="20" y="20"><value name="TIMES"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><statement name="DO"><block type="m5_led_set"><field name="COLOR">on</field><next><block type="m5_wait"><value name="SECONDS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">0.3</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></statement></block></xml>

Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)

for i in range(10):
    Power.setLed(255)
    Speaker.tone(440, 100)
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 80, 20, 0xFF0000)
    time.sleep(0.3)
    Power.setLed(0)
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 80, 20, 0x000000)
    time.sleep(0.3)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("10 blinks!", 25, 120)

hints.text: A LOOP repeats blocks over and over! Instead of adding 10 LED blocks, you add 1 and loop it 10 times. So much easier!

Put "LED on" inside the loop, then a wait (0.3 seconds), then "LED off", then another wait.
Change the repeat count to 10. Now it blinks 10 times! Add a beep inside the loop too.
Try changing the number to 20 or 3. The loop does ALL the repetition for you -- thats the power of loops!

title: Pattern Machine
description: Use loops to create patterns! A loop that draws shapes or plays sounds in sequence creates amazing patterns. Build a pattern machine!
story_text: Remember patterns from Puzzle Lab? Patterns and loops are BEST FRIENDS! When you see a pattern like clap-stomp-clap-stomp, the loop is "repeat clap-stomp." Let's find the loop inside each pattern!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="controls_repeat_ext"><value name="TIMES"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">4</field></shadow></value><statement name="DO"><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value></block></statement></block></next></block></xml>

    Speaker.tone(300 + i * 100, 200)

time.sleep(0.3)

Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Pattern!", 35, 120)

hints.text: A pattern machine uses loops to create repeating designs! Each time the loop runs, it can draw something in a new position.

Add a tone inside the loop too -- each rectangle gets a different pitch. Visual AND sound pattern!
Add a second loop below for circles. Same pattern, different shapes! Your pattern machine creates art!

title: If This, Then That
description: Conditionals let your program make decisions! IF the button is pressed, THEN do something. Program your M5Stick to respond differently to each button!
story_text: You make choices every day! IF it is raining, THEN take an umbrella. IF you are hungry, THEN eat a snack. Computers make choices too! They use something called IF-THEN. Let's practice thinking like a computer!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Press a button!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="controls_if"><value name="IF0"><block type="m5_button_pressed"><field name="BUTTON">A</field></block></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("If This Then That", 2, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Press A or B!", 10, 35)
for i in range(20):
    if BtnA.isPressed():
        Lcd.fillRect(0, 60, 135, 50, 0x000000)
        Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
        Lcd.drawString("Button A!", 25, 70)
        Speaker.tone(523, 200)
        Power.setLed(255)
    elif BtnB.isPressed():
        Lcd.fillRect(0, 60, 135, 50, 0x000000)
        Lcd.setTextColor(0x0000FF, 0x000000)
        Lcd.drawString("Button B!", 25, 70)
        Speaker.tone(330, 200)
        Power.setLed(0)
    time.sleep(0.2)

hints.text: IF-THEN is how computers make choices! IF it is raining, THEN bring an umbrella. IF button A is pressed, THEN play a sound!
Find the Logic tab and drag an "if" block. Find the Buttons tab and put "button A pressed?" in the IF slot.
INSIDE the if block, add what happens when A is pressed: display "Button A!" and play a tone!
Click the gear on the if block to add an ELSE IF. Check for button B and do something different!
Wrap everything in a loop so it keeps checking. Now your program DECIDES what to do based on your input!

title: Code a Dance!
description: Combine everything you learned to code a dance routine! Use loops, sounds, LED blinks, and display messages to create an awesome dance performance!
story_text: Time to DANCE! Chip loves dancing -- and guess what? A dance is just an algorithm with music! You put moves in order, and some moves REPEAT (that's a loop!). Let's code a dance routine together!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Dance Time!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">purple</field><next><block type="controls_repeat_ext"><value name="TIMES"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">4</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:

colors = [0xFF0000, 0x00FF00, 0x0000FF, 0xFFFF00]
for i in range(4):
    for j in range(2):
        Lcd.fillRect(0, 40, 135, 60, 0x000000)
        Lcd.setTextColor(colors[i], 0x000000)
        Lcd.drawString(moves[i], 35, 60)
        Speaker.tone(300 + i * 80, 200)
        Power.setLed(255)
        time.sleep(0.3)
        Lcd.fillRect(0, 40, 135, 60, 0x000000)
        Power.setLed(0)
        time.sleep(0.2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Great dance!", 15, 120)

title: Counting Machine
description: Count to 50 with your M5Stick as a counting partner! Build a program that beeps and displays each number as it counts up.
story_text: Chip is SO excited! "I just learned something amazing," he says, bouncing up and down. "Numbers are EVERYWHERE! There are numbers on clocks, on houses, on scoreboards... but I keep losing track when I count. Can you help me build a counting machine? We will count together -- I will show each number on my screen and beep along with you! Ready? Let us start from 1!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Counting!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Counting!", 15, 10)

for i in range(1, 51):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 50, 135, 40, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(str(i), 50, 60)
    Speaker.tone(440, 100)
    time.sleep(0.4)

Lcd.drawString("Done! 50!", 25, 100)
Speaker.tone(880, 500)

hints.text: Start with Display Text to show Counting! as your title.
Add a Loop block that repeats 50 times.
Inside the loop, use Display Number to show each number.
Add a Buzzer Tone for each count so you can hear the counting!
After the loop, display a celebration message like Done! 50!

title: Number Neighbors
description: Explore number lines and find out which numbers live next to each other! Build a number line display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip is looking at a row of houses on his screen. "Every house has a number," he says, "but some of the numbers fell off! House 5 is right between House 4 and House 6. They are number neighbors! Can you help me figure out what numbers are missing from the other houses? If I know a number, I can always find its neighbors!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_number" x="20" y="20"><value name="NUMBER"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">55</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)

for round in range(5):
    num = random.randint(2, 18)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 90, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(str(num - 1), 10, 60)
    Lcd.drawString("- " + str(num) + " -", 45, 60)
    Lcd.drawString(str(num + 1), 105, 60)
    Lcd.drawRect(35, 48, 65, 30, 0x3B82F6)
    Speaker.tone(550, 150)
    time.sleep(2.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 90, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("You did it!", 25, 60)

Show the number in the center of the screen with Display Number.

Use a Rectangle block to highlight the middle number!

title: Addition Adventure
description: Discover addition by combining groups! Build a visual addition quiz on your M5Stick that shows two numbers adding together.

I have 5! That is called ADDING! 3 plus 2 equals 5! I wonder what other numbers I can add together. Shake me to get a new addition puzzle!"

Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Math star!", 25, 60)

title: Number Line Adventure!
description: Put the numbers in the right order from smallest to biggest! Build a number line display that lights up each number in sequence.
story_text: Chip is building a number train! But the number cars got all mixed up. Can you help put them back in order?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Number Line</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><next><block type="controls_repeat_ext"><value name="TIMES"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Number Line", 10, 5)

Lcd.drawRect(5, 55, 125, 3, 0xFFFFFF)

for i in range(1, 11):
    x = 5 + (i - 1) * 12
    Lcd.fillRect(x, 50, 10, 12, 0x3B82F6)

title: Subtraction Safari
description: Learn subtraction by watching objects disappear! Build a visual subtraction display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip is at the safari park! "I can see 8 flamingos standing by the pond," he whispers. "Oh no, 3 of them just flew away! How many are still here? Let me count... 1, 2, 3, 4, 5! There are 5 left! When things go away, that is called SUBTRACTING. 8 take away 3 is 5. Let us go find more animals and practice taking away!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Subtract!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">8</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Subtract!", 30, 5)

for round in range(5):
    total = random.randint(4, 8)
    take = random.randint(1, total - 1)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 90, 0x000000)
    for i in range(total):
        Lcd.fillCircle(12 + i * 15, 45, 6, 0x22C55E)
    Lcd.drawString(str(total) + " total", 30, 65)
    time.sleep(1)
    for i in range(take):
        Lcd.fillCircle(12 + (total-1-i) * 15, 45, 6, 0x000000)
        Speaker.tone(300, 80)
        time.sleep(0.3)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 60, 135, 30, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(str(total) + "-" + str(take) + "=" + str(total-take), 15, 70)
    Speaker.tone(660, 200)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 90, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Safari done!", 15, 60)

title: Tens and Ones Tower
description: Build towers with tens and ones blocks to understand place value! Display visual blocks on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip is stacking blocks! "I have all these little blocks," he says, "and I figured out something cool -- when I stack 10 of them together, they turn into ONE tall tower! So the number 14 is really 1 tall tower and 4 little blocks. The 1 means one TEN and the 4 means four ONES. Let us build some number towers together!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_rect" x="20" y="20"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Tens & Ones!", 10, 5)

for round in range(4):
    tens = random.randint(1, 5)
    ones = random.randint(0, 9)
    num = tens * 10 + ones
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    for t in range(tens):
        Lcd.fillRect(8 + t * 14, 35, 10, 40, 0x3B82F6)
    for o in range(ones):
        Lcd.fillRect(8 + o * 10, 80, 8, 8, 0xF97316)
    Lcd.drawString(str(tens) + " tens " + str(ones) + " ones", 5, 100)
    Lcd.drawString("= " + str(num), 80, 100)
    Speaker.tone(550, 200)
    time.sleep(2.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Place value!", 15, 60)

title: Making 10 to Add!
description: Use two ten frames to add numbers bigger than 10. Fill one frame, then overflow into the next on your M5Stick!
story_text: Here is a super math trick! When you add two numbers and they go past 10, fill up the first ten frame ALL the way, then put the extra in a second frame. Like 8 + 5: fill 8, then add 2 more to make 10, and put the leftover 3 in the next frame. 10 + 3 = 13!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_rect" x="20" y="20"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Make 10 Add!", 10, 5)

a = random.randint(6, 9)
b = random.randint(3, 8)
total = a + b
Lcd.drawString(str(a) + " + " + str(b), 30, 22)

for row in range(2):
    for col in range(5):
        Lcd.drawRect(5 + col*13, 38 + row*13, 12, 12, 0x444444)
        Lcd.drawRect(70 + col*13, 38 + row*13, 12, 12, 0x444444)

count = 0
for row in range(2):
    for col in range(5):
        if count < a:
            Lcd.fillRect(6 + col*13, 39 + row*13, 10, 10, 0x3B82F6)
            Speaker.tone(500, 50)
            time.sleep(0.15)
        count += 1

count = 0
for row in range(2):
    for col in range(5):
        if count < b:
            Lcd.fillRect(71 + col*13, 39 + row*13, 10, 10, 0x22C55E)
            Speaker.tone(600, 50)
            time.sleep(0.15)
        count += 1

time.sleep(1)
Lcd.drawString("= " + str(total) + "!", 45, 75)
Speaker.tone(880, 400)

title: Frog Hops Forward!
description: Help the frog hop along the number line to add numbers together! Build a visual number line with a hopping frog on your M5Stick.
story_text: Ribbit! This little frog loves to hop! When we add numbers, the frog hops FORWARD on the number line. If we start at 4 and add 3, the frog makes 3 hops forward and lands on... 7! Let's practice!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Hop!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_display_rect"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">70</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">125</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">3</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Frog Hops!", 20, 5)

start = random.randint(1, 5)
hops = random.randint(2, 5)
Lcd.drawString(str(start) + " + " + str(hops), 25, 22)

Lcd.drawRect(5, 70, 125, 3, 0xFFFFFF)
for i in range(11):
    x = 5 + i * 12
    Lcd.drawRect(x, 68, 1, 7, 0xFFFFFF)
    Lcd.drawString(str(i), x - 2, 80)

pos = start
Lcd.fillCircle(5 + pos * 12, 60, 5, 0x22C55E)
time.sleep(1)

for h in range(hops):
    Lcd.fillCircle(5 + pos * 12, 60, 5, 0x000000)
    pos += 1
    Lcd.fillCircle(5 + pos * 12, 60, 5, 0x22C55E)
    Speaker.tone(500 + h * 50, 150)
    time.sleep(0.6)

Lcd.drawString("= " + str(start + hops), 45, 100)
Speaker.tone(880, 300)

hints.text: Draw a number line at the bottom with marks for 0 through 10.
Place a green circle (the frog) at the starting number.
Move the frog forward by erasing it and drawing it at the next position.
Play a hop sound each time the frog moves!
When the frog stops, show where it landed as the answer.

questions.hint: Hop forward 4 times from 3!
questions.prompt: Start at 3 and add 4. Where does the frog land?

questions.hint: From 5, hop forward 6 times!
questions.prompt: Start at 5 and add 6. Where does the frog land?

questions.hint: 8 plus 5 — hop past 10!
questions.prompt: Start at 8 and add 5. Where does the frog land?

questions.hint: 2 + 7 = ? Hop forward 7 times!
questions.prompt: Start at 2 and add 7. Where does the frog land?

title: Frog Hops Backward!
description: Help the frog hop backward on the number line to subtract! Build a visual subtraction tool on your M5Stick.
story_text: Ribbit ribbit! Now the frog needs to hop BACKWARD! When we subtract, we move to the left on the number line. If we start at 9 and subtract 3, the frog hops back 3 times and lands on 6!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Hop Back!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">25</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Hop Back!", 25, 5)

start = random.randint(5, 10)
hops = random.randint(2, start - 1)
Lcd.drawString(str(start) + " - " + str(hops), 25, 22)

Lcd.drawRect(5, 70, 125, 3, 0xFFFFFF)
for i in range(11):
    x = 5 + i * 12
    Lcd.drawRect(x, 68, 1, 7, 0xFFFFFF)
    Lcd.drawString(str(i), x - 2, 80)

pos = start
Lcd.fillCircle(5 + pos * 12, 60, 5, 0x22C55E)
time.sleep(1)

for h in range(hops):
    Lcd.fillCircle(5 + pos * 12, 60, 5, 0x000000)
    pos -= 1
    Lcd.fillCircle(5 + pos * 12, 60, 5, 0x22C55E)
    Speaker.tone(500 - h * 30, 150)
    time.sleep(0.6)

Lcd.drawString("= " + str(start - hops), 45, 100)
Speaker.tone(660, 300)

hints.text: Draw a number line from 0 to 10 at the bottom of the screen.
Place the frog (green circle) at the starting number.
Move the frog backward one hop at a time by subtracting 1.
Play descending tones as the frog hops back!
Show where the frog lands as the subtraction answer.

questions.hint: From 12, hop backward 5 times!
questions.prompt: Start at 12 and subtract 5. Where does the frog land?

questions.hint: 10 minus 4 — hop backward from 10!
questions.prompt: Start at 10 and subtract 4. Where does the frog land?

title: Bead Addition!
description: Add numbers together using the counting rack! Build a visual bead addition display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Now let's use the rekenrek to ADD numbers! Show the first number on the top row, then add more beads on the bottom row. Count all the beads together to find the answer!
