solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Bead Addition!", 5, 5)

a = random.randint(2, 8)
b = random.randint(1, 10 - a)
Lcd.drawString(str(a) + " + " + str(b), 35, 22)

Lcd.drawRect(5, 42, 125, 22, 0xFFFFFF)
for i in range(a):
    Lcd.fillCircle(12 + i * 12, 53, 5, 0xEF4444)
    Speaker.tone(500, 50)
    time.sleep(0.2)

time.sleep(0.5)
for i in range(b):
    Lcd.fillCircle(12 + (a + i) * 12, 53, 5, 0x3B82F6)
    Speaker.tone(600, 50)
    time.sleep(0.2)

time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("= " + str(a + b) + " beads!", 20, 75)
Speaker.tone(880, 300)

title: Clock Builder
description: Learn to tell time to the hour! Build an analog clock display on your M5Stick that shows different times.
story_text: Chip is confused. "There is this round thing on the wall with two sticks that keep moving," he says. "Someone told me it tells TIME, but how? The short stick points to the HOUR and the long stick points to the minutes. When the long stick points straight up at 12, it is something-o'clock! Right now the short hand points to 3 and the long hand at 12, so it is... 3 o'clock! Let us practice!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_circle" x="20" y="20"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">67</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">65</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">35</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
import math

Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Clock Time!", 20, 5)

for hour in range(1, 13):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 22, 135, 98, 0x000000)
    cx, cy, r = 67, 65, 32
    Lcd.drawCircle(cx, cy, r, 0xFFFFFF)
    Lcd.fillCircle(cx, cy, 2, 0xFFFFFF)
    for h in range(12):
        angle = math.radians(h * 30 - 90)
        tx = int(cx + (r - 5) * math.cos(angle))
        ty = int(cy + (r - 5) * math.sin(angle))
        Lcd.fillCircle(tx, ty, 1, 0xFFFFFF)
    angle = math.radians(hour * 30 - 90)
    hx = int(cx + 18 * math.cos(angle))
    hy = int(cy + 18 * math.sin(angle))
    Lcd.drawRect(cx, cy, hx - cx, hy - cy, 0x3B82F6)
    Lcd.drawRect(cx, cy, 0, -25, 0xEF4444)
    Lcd.drawString(str(hour) + ":00", 40, 105)
    Speaker.tone(440 + hour * 30, 100)
    time.sleep(1.2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 22, 135, 98, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Time pro!", 30, 60)

hints.text: Draw a circle for the clock face using Display Circle.

Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)

score = 0

# Round 1: Addition

Lcd.drawString(str(a)+"-"+str(b)+"=?", 20, 45)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.drawString("= "+str(a-b), 50, 70)

score += 1
time.sleep(1.5)

# Round 3: Shape

Lcd.fillCircle(67, 70, 15, 0x22C55E)

solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Shape Find!", 20, 5)

shapes = [
    ("Circle", 0xEF4444),
    ("Square", 0x3B82F6),
    ("Rectangle", 0x22C55E),
    ("Oval", 0xA855F7)
]

for name, color in shapes:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    if name == "Circle":
        Lcd.fillCircle(67, 55, 18, color)
    elif name == "Square":
        Lcd.fillRect(47, 37, 36, 36, color)
    elif name == "Rectangle":
        Lcd.fillRect(27, 42, 80, 26, color)
    else:
        Lcd.fillCircle(67, 55, 22, color)
    Lcd.drawString("?", 60, 85)
    time.sleep(1.5)
    Lcd.fillRect(40, 80, 60, 20, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name + "!", 30, 85)
    Speaker.tone(660, 200)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Shape expert!", 10, 60)

solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Compare Size!", 10, 5)

for round in range(5):
    a = random.randint(20, 100)
    b = random.randint(20, 100)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.fillRect(10, 40, a, 12, 0x3B82F6)
    Lcd.drawString("A: " + str(a), 10, 30)
    Lcd.fillRect(10, 70, b, 12, 0xEF4444)
    Lcd.drawString("B: " + str(b), 10, 60)
    time.sleep(1)
    if a > b:
        Lcd.drawString("A is longer!", 15, 95)
    elif b > a:
        Lcd.drawString("B is longer!", 15, 95)
    else:
        Lcd.drawString("Same length!", 15, 95)
    Speaker.tone(660, 200)
    time.sleep(1.8)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Measured!", 30, 60)

title: Skip Counting by 2s
description: Count by twos and discover the pattern! Build a skip counting display on your M5Stick that shows 2, 4, 6, 8...
story_text: Hey there, superstar! Chip here! Did you know you can count SUPER fast by skipping every other number? It's like hopping over one number each time: 2, 4, 6, 8... Let's practice skip counting by 2s together!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="controls_repeat_ext" x="20" y="20"><value name="TIMES"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Count by 2s!", 15, 5)

for i in range(1, 11):
    num = i * 2
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 40, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(str(num), 50, 45)
    bar_w = num * 6

title: Even and Odd Numbers
description: Discover even and odd numbers! Build a number checker on your M5Stick that shows if numbers can be split into two equal groups.
story_text: Chip has a riddle for you! Some numbers can be split perfectly into two equal teams, and some always have one left over. The ones that split evenly are called EVEN, and the ones with a leftover are called ODD. Let's figure out which is which!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_random_number" x="20" y="20"><value name="MIN"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">1</field></shadow></value><value name="MAX"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Even or Odd?", 10, 5)

for round in range(8):
    num = random.randint(1, 20)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(str(num), 55, 35)
    time.sleep(1)
    half = num // 2
    for i in range(half):
        Lcd.fillCircle(10 + i * 13, 60, 5, 0x3B82F6)
        Lcd.fillCircle(10 + i * 13, 78, 5, 0x3B82F6)
    if num % 2 == 1:
        Lcd.fillCircle(10 + half * 13, 60, 5, 0xEF4444)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    if num % 2 == 0:
        Lcd.drawString("EVEN!", 45, 100)
        Speaker.tone(660, 200)
    else:
        Lcd.drawString("ODD!", 50, 100)
        Speaker.tone(440, 200)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Number brain!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Pick a random number from 1 to 20.
Try splitting it into two equal groups of circles.
If all circles pair up, it is EVEN!
If one circle is left over (shown in red), it is ODD!
Play different sounds for even vs odd numbers.

questions.hint: Try splitting 7 into two equal groups. Can you? There is 1 left over!
questions.prompt: Is 7 even or odd? 🤔

questions.hint: 12 can be split into two groups of 6. No leftovers!
questions.prompt: Is 12 even or odd? 🤔

questions.hint: 15 split into two groups gives 7 and 8 -- not equal! It is odd.
questions.prompt: Is 15 even or odd? 🤔

questions.hint: 20 splits into two groups of 10. Even!
questions.prompt: Is 20 even or odd? 🤔

questions.hint: Odd numbers end in 1, 3, 5, 7, or 9!
questions.prompt: Which of these numbers is ODD? 🌟

questions.hint: 6 is even! Put 3 beads on each row to prove it.
questions.prompt: Show 6 on the rekenrek. Can you split it into two equal rows?

questions.hint: 9 is odd! You get 4 and 5 -- they are not equal.
questions.prompt: Show 9 on the rekenrek. Can you split it evenly?

questions.hint: 8 is even! 4 beads on each row makes two equal groups.
questions.prompt: Show 8 on the rekenrek. Is 8 even or odd?

title: Meet the Coins!
description: Learn the names and values of pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters! Build a coin gallery on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip just found a treasure chest full of coins! But Chip doesn't know their names or how much they are worth. Can you help? There are 4 types of coins: pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters. Let's learn about each one!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_circle" x="20" y="20"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">67</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">55</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Penny = 1c</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">90</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Meet Coins!", 20, 5)

coins = [
    ("Penny", 1, 0xCD7F32, 12),
    ("Nickel", 5, 0xC0C0C0, 14),
    ("Dime", 10, 0xC0C0C0, 10),
    ("Quarter", 25, 0xC0C0C0, 16)
]

for name, value, color, radius in coins:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 55, radius, color)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x000000, color)
    Lcd.drawString(str(value), 62, 52)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 35, 80)
    Lcd.drawString("= " + str(value) + " cents", 25, 100)
    Speaker.tone(500 + value * 10, 200)
    time.sleep(2.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Coin expert!", 15, 60)

title: Counting Coins
description: Add up groups of coins to find the total! Build a coin counting display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip is saving up to buy a new battery! Can you help count the coins in Chip's piggy bank? Remember: start with the biggest coins first, then add the smaller ones. Let's see how much money Chip has!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">25 + 10 + 5</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Count Coins!", 15, 5)

coin_vals = [1, 5, 10, 25]
coin_names = ["1c", "5c", "10c", "25c"]
coin_colors = [0xCD7F32, 0xC0C0C0, 0xC0C0C0, 0xC0C0C0]

for round in range(4):
    num_coins = random.randint(2, 4)
    total = 0
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    for c in range(num_coins):
        idx = random.randint(0, 3)
        val = coin_vals[idx]
        total += val
        Lcd.fillCircle(20 + c * 28, 50, 10, coin_colors[idx])
        Lcd.drawString(coin_names[idx], 10 + c * 28, 65)
        Speaker.tone(400, 80)
        time.sleep(0.5)
    time.sleep(1)
    Lcd.drawString("= " + str(total) + " cents!", 15, 90)
    Speaker.tone(770, 200)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Money smart!", 15, 60)

title: Big Number Addition
description: Use the number line to add bigger numbers! Build a visual addition tool on your M5Stick with hopping animations.
story_text: Chip is ready for a BIG challenge! Now that you know how to skip count by 10s, we can use that skill to add big numbers. Start at a number, then hop forward by 10 or 20 to find the answer. You are going to do great!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">15 + 8 = ?</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">40</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Big Addition!", 10, 5)

for round in range(4):
    a = random.randint(10, 30)
    b = random.randint(5, 20)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(str(a) + " + " + str(b) + " = ?", 15, 35)
    Lcd.drawRect(5, 60, 125, 3, 0xFFFFFF)
    Lcd.fillCircle(10, 55, 4, 0x22C55E)
    time.sleep(1)
    for hop in range(min(b, 8)):
        pos = 10 + int((hop + 1) * 120 / 10)
        Lcd.fillCircle(pos, 55, 4, 0x22C55E)
        Speaker.tone(400 + hop * 30, 80)
        time.sleep(0.2)
    Lcd.drawString("= " + str(a + b) + "!", 40, 80)
    Speaker.tone(770, 250)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Big brain!", 25, 60)

hints.text: Pick two numbers to add -- one big, one smaller.
Display the addition problem on screen.
Draw a number line and show hops for the second number.
Each hop moves forward by 1 from the starting number.
Show where you land as the answer!

questions.hint: When you add 10, only the tens digit changes! 23 + 10 = 33.
questions.prompt: Start at 23 and add 10. Where do you land?

questions.hint: Adding 20 is like hopping forward by 10 two times! 45 + 10 = 55, then 55 + 10 = 65.
questions.prompt: Start at 45 and add 20. Where do you land?

questions.hint: 37 + 10 = 47. The ones digit stays the same!
questions.prompt: Start at 37 and add 10. Where do you land?

questions.hint: Adding 30 means 3 hops of 10! 58, 68, 78, 88!
questions.prompt: Start at 58 and add 30. Where do you land?
Start at 14 and add 20. Where do you land?

title: Hundreds, Tens, and Ones
description: Break apart 3-digit numbers into hundreds, tens, and ones! Every digit has a special place.
story_text: Hey there, superstar! Chip here with a BIG idea! Numbers have a secret code. In the number 347, the 3 means three HUNDREDS (that's like 3 big boxes of 100), the 4 means four TENS (like 4 bags of 10), and the 7 means seven ONES (7 loose ones). Let's crack the code together!

questions.hint: The 3 in 347 is in the hundreds place. 3 hundreds = 300!
questions.prompt: Break apart 347 into hundreds, tens, and ones! What is the hundreds part?

questions.hint: The 5 in 582 is in the hundreds place. 5 hundreds = 500!
questions.prompt: Break apart 582! What is the hundreds part?

questions.hint: 215 minus 200 equals 15. The tens and ones part is 15!
questions.prompt: Break apart 215! The tens and ones part together is ___.

questions.hint: The 7 in 736 is in the hundreds place. 7 hundreds = 700!
questions.prompt: Break apart 736! What is the hundreds part?

questions.hint: The first digit (on the left) in a 3-digit number is the hundreds place!
questions.prompt: In the number 463, the digit 4 is in the ___ place.

questions.hint: The middle digit in a 3-digit number is the tens place!
questions.prompt: In the number 829, the digit 2 is in the ___ place.

questions.hint: The last digit (on the right) in a 3-digit number is the ones place!
questions.prompt: In the number 156, the digit 6 is in the ___ place.

questions.hint: The first digit tells you how many hundreds. What is the first digit of 503?
questions.prompt: The number 503 has ___ hundreds, 0 tens, and 3 ones.

questions.hint: The middle digit tells you how many tens. What is the middle digit of 271?
questions.prompt: The number 271 has 2 hundreds, ___ tens, and 1 one.

title: Expanded Form
description: Stretch numbers out into expanded form! Write 347 as 300 + 40 + 7.
story_text: Chip learned a cool trick called EXPANDED FORM! You take a number and stretch it out to show each part. It's like taking apart a Lego tower to see all the pieces: 347 becomes 300 + 40 + 7. Each piece is a different size -- hundreds, tens, and ones. Let's stretch some numbers!

questions.hint: The 4 is in the tens place. 4 tens = 40!
questions.prompt: Write 347 in expanded form: 300 + ___ + 7

questions.hint: The 6 is in the hundreds place. 6 hundreds = 600!
questions.prompt: Write 629 in expanded form: ___ + 20 + 9

questions.hint: The 5 is in the ones place. 5 ones = 5!
questions.prompt: Write 185 in expanded form: 100 + 80 + ___

questions.hint: Put the pieces back together! 400 + 50 + 3 = ?
questions.prompt: What number is 400 + 50 + 3? ___

questions.hint: 7 hundreds, 1 ten, 8 ones. Write them all together!
questions.prompt: What number is 700 + 10 + 8? ___

activities.prompt: Match each number to its expanded form! 🧩

title: Reading Big Numbers
description: Learn to read and write number words for 3-digit numbers! Say them like a pro.
story_text: Chip wants to read numbers like a champion! Did you know you can write 256 as "two hundred fifty-six"? It's like telling a story about the number -- first you say the hundreds, then the tens, then the ones. Let's practice reading big numbers out loud!

cards.back: Two hundred fifty-six. Say the hundreds first (two hundred), then the rest (fifty-six)!
Four hundred ten. When there are 0 ones, you just skip the ones part!
Eight hundred three. When there are 0 tens, you skip right from hundreds to ones!
Nine hundred ninety-nine. This is the biggest 3-digit number! One more and you reach 1,000!
One hundred. The very first 3-digit number! It has 1 hundred, 0 tens, and 0 ones.

activities.prompt: Flip each card to see how to read the number!

questions.hint: Three hundred = 300, twenty = 20, four = 4. Put them together: 324!
questions.prompt: Write the number for: three hundred twenty-four = ___

questions.hint: Five hundred = 500, sixty = 60, one = 1. Together: 561!
questions.prompt: Write the number for: five hundred sixty-one = ___

questions.hint: Seven hundred = 700, eight = 8. No tens! So it is 708.
questions.prompt: Write the number for: seven hundred eight = ___

questions.hint: Nine hundred = 900, forty = 40. No ones! So it is 940.
questions.prompt: Write the number for: nine hundred forty = ___

questions.hint: 999 + 1 = ? You need a 4-digit number now!
questions.prompt: What number comes right AFTER 999? ___

title: Comparing 3-Digit Numbers
description: Which number is bigger? Use >, =, and < to compare 3-digit numbers!
story_text: Time for a game, superstar! Chip calls it the Alligator Game! The alligator is ALWAYS hungry and its mouth opens toward the BIGGER number. 451 > 415 means 451 is greater. 415 < 451 means 415 is less. And if two numbers are the same, we use the equals sign: 300 = 300. Let's play!

questions.hint: Both start with 4 hundreds. Compare the tens: 5 tens vs 1 ten. 5 tens is more, so 451 is greater!
questions.prompt: Which number is GREATER: 451 or 415? 🐊

questions.hint: Compare hundreds first: 6 hundreds vs 7 hundreds. 6 is less than 7, so 672 is less!
questions.prompt: Which number is LESS: 672 or 762? 🐊

questions.hint: Both have 3 hundreds. Compare tens: 8 tens vs 9 tens. 8 is less than 9, so 389 < 398!
questions.prompt: Which symbol goes between 389 ___ 398?

questions.hint: The numbers are exactly the same! When two numbers are the same, we use the equals sign.
questions.prompt: Which symbol goes between 500 ___ 500?

questions.hint: Compare hundreds: 2 hundreds vs 3 hundreds. 3 hundreds is more, so 300 > 299!
questions.prompt: Which is greater: 299 or 300? 🤔

questions.hint: Same hundreds (6). Compare tens: 3 tens vs 4 tens. 3 < 4, so 634 < 643!
questions.prompt: Fill in the symbol: 634 ___ 643 (type >, <, or =)

questions.hint: Same hundreds (8). Compare tens: 5 tens vs 1 ten. 5 > 1, so 851 > 815!
questions.prompt: Fill in the symbol: 851 ___ 815 (type >, <, or =)

questions.hint: Both numbers are exactly the same! 777 equals 777.
questions.prompt: Fill in the symbol: 777 ___ 777 (type >, <, or =)

questions.hint: Compare hundreds: 1 hundred vs 2 hundreds. 1 < 2, so 199 < 200!
questions.prompt: Fill in the symbol: 199 ___ 200 (type >, <, or =)

questions.hint: Compare hundreds: 9 hundreds vs 8 hundreds. 9 > 8, so 905 > 895!
questions.prompt: Fill in the symbol: 905 ___ 895 (type >, <, or =)

title: Ordering Numbers to 1000
description: Put 3-digit numbers in order from least to greatest! Organize the number parade.
story_text: Chip is organizing a Number Parade! But the numbers are all mixed up and need to march in order from SMALLEST to BIGGEST. Can you help line them up? Remember: compare the hundreds first, then tens, then ones. Let's get this parade started!

questions.hint: Compare the hundreds first! 253 has 2 hundreds, 325 has 3 hundreds, 532 has 5 hundreds.
questions.prompt: Put these numbers in order from LEAST to GREATEST!

questions.hint: All start differently or same hundreds? 478 and 487 both have 4 hundreds. Compare their tens: 7 tens vs 8 tens!
questions.prompt: Put these numbers in order from LEAST to GREATEST!

questions.hint: Compare hundreds first! 109 and 190 have 1 hundred. 901 and 910 have 9 hundreds. Then compare tens within each group!
questions.prompt: Put these numbers in order from LEAST to GREATEST!

questions.hint: They all have 5 hundreds! Compare tens: 500 has 0 tens, 505 has 0 tens, 550 has 5 tens, 555 has 5 tens. Then check ones!
questions.prompt: Number Parade! Line up from LEAST to GREATEST!

questions.hint: Put the smallest digit in the hundreds place! The smallest digit is 2.
questions.prompt: What is the SMALLEST 3-digit number you can make with 4, 7, and 2? ___

questions.hint: Put the biggest digit in the hundreds place! The biggest digit is 7.
questions.prompt: What is the BIGGEST 3-digit number you can make with 4, 7, and 2? ___
Which number comes BETWEEN 399 and 401? ___
Which number comes BETWEEN 549 and 551? ___

title: Mental Math +10, +100
description: Use place value to add and subtract 10 or 100 in your head! Chip's mental math superpower.
story_text: Chip has a SUPERPOWER and wants to share it with you! When you add 10, only the TENS digit changes. When you add 100, only the HUNDREDS digit changes. The other digits stay the same! It's like magic, but it's really place value. Let's train your brain!

questions.hint: When you add 10, only the tens digit changes! 356 + 10 = 366.
questions.prompt: Start at 356. Add 10! Where do you land? 🧠

questions.hint: When you add 100, only the hundreds digit changes! 356 + 100 = 456.
questions.prompt: Start at 356. Add 100! Where do you land? 🚀

questions.hint: Subtracting 10 makes the tens digit go down by 1. 482 - 10 = 472.
questions.prompt: Start at 482. Subtract 10! Where do you land?

questions.hint: Subtracting 100 makes the hundreds digit go down by 1. 715 - 100 = 615.
questions.prompt: Start at 715. Subtract 100! Where do you land?

questions.hint: 290 + 10 = 300! The tens digit rolls over from 9 to 0 and the hundreds go up by 1.
questions.prompt: Start at 290. Add 10! Where do you land? 🌟

questions.hint: Adding 10 changes only the tens digit. 2 tens becomes 3 tens!
Adding 100 changes only the hundreds digit. 4 hundreds becomes 5 hundreds!
Subtracting 10 changes only the tens digit. 7 tens becomes 6 tens!
Subtracting 100 changes only the hundreds digit. 6 hundreds becomes 5 hundreds!
590 + 10 = 600! The tens roll over from 9 to 0 and the hundreds go up by 1.
900 + 100 = 1000! You just reached one thousand!

title: Addition Facts to 20 (Fluency)
description: Practice your addition facts to 20 until they are super fast! Flip, solve, repeat!
story_text: Welcome to Chip's Speed Math Challenge! The fastest robots in Robot Land know their addition facts by heart. Can YOU answer these as fast as Chip? Flip each card to check your answer, then try the fill-in-the-blank round. Ready, set, GO!

cards.back: 15! Seven plus eight equals fifteen.
15! Nine plus six equals fifteen.
13! Eight plus five equals thirteen.
13! Six plus seven equals thirteen.
18! Nine plus nine equals eighteen.
14! Eight plus six equals fourteen.

activities.prompt: Flip each card to check your addition answer! Try to answer BEFORE you flip!

title: Making 10 to Add
description: Learn the making-10 trick! Break numbers apart so you can make a 10 first, then add the rest.
story_text: Chip has the BEST math trick ever! When you need to add two numbers, you can break one apart to MAKE A 10 first. Like 8 + 5: take 2 from the 5 to make 8 into 10, then you have 3 left. 10 + 3 = 13! Easy peasy! Let's practice this awesome trick!

questions.hint: Fill 8 in the first frame, then add 2 more to make 10. The leftover 3 go in the second frame. 10 + 3 = 13!
questions.prompt: Show 8 + 5 by making 10 first!

questions.hint: 9 just needs 1 more to make 10! Take 1 from the 6, leaving 5. 10 + 5 = 15!
questions.prompt: Show 9 + 6 by making 10 first!

questions.hint: 7 needs 3 more to make 10. Take 3 from the 6, leaving 3. 10 + 3 = 13!
questions.prompt: Show 7 + 6 by making 10 first!

questions.hint: 8 needs 2 more to make 10. Take 2 from the other 8, leaving 6. 10 + 6 = 16!
questions.prompt: Show 8 + 8 by making 10 first!
Break apart 5 to make 10 with 8! What goes with 8 to make 10?
Break apart 7 to make 10 with 6! How much does 6 need to become 10?

questions.hint: 9 + ___ = 10. Just 1 more!
questions.prompt: Break apart 4 to make 10 with 9! What does 9 need?
Break apart 8 to make 10 with 7! What does 7 need?

questions.hint: 13 minus 10 equals 3. The answer is 3!
questions.prompt: 8 + 5 = 13. Show the bond: 13 is 10 and ___!

title: Two-Digit Addition (No Regrouping)
description: Add two-digit numbers the easy way -- no carrying needed! Add tens, then add ones.
story_text: Chip is learning to add BIG numbers! When you add numbers like 23 + 15, you can add the tens first (20 + 10 = 30), then add the ones (3 + 5 = 8), and put them together: 30 + 8 = 38! Let's try it on the rekenrek and then do some on our own!

questions.hint: Slide 20 beads (all of both rows on the first rack), then 3 more. That is 23!
questions.prompt: Show 23 on the rekenrek!

questions.hint: 38 means 3 full rows of 10 (30 beads) plus 8 more. Slide them over!
questions.prompt: Now show 38 -- that is 23 + 15!

questions.hint: 4 rows of 10 (40 beads) plus 2 more. You got this!
questions.prompt: Show 42 on the rekenrek!

questions.hint: 7 rows of 10 (70 beads) plus 3 more. Awesome!
questions.prompt: Now show 73 -- that is 42 + 31!

questions.hint: Add tens: 30 + 20 = 50. Add ones: 4 + 5 = 9. Together: 50 + 9 = 59!
Add tens: 40 + 30 = 70. Add ones: 2 + 1 = 3. Together: 70 + 3 = 73!
Add tens: 50 + 20 = 70. Add ones: 1 + 6 = 7. Together: 70 + 7 = 77!
Add tens: 10 + 20 = 30. Add ones: 3 + 4 = 7. Together: 30 + 7 = 37!
Add tens: 60 + 10 = 70. Add ones: 1 + 8 = 9. Together: 70 + 9 = 79!

title: Two-Digit Addition (With Regrouping)
description: When the ones add up past 10, you need to regroup! Learn how to carry the one like a pro.
story_text: Uh oh -- what happens when the ones add up to MORE than 9? Time to REGROUP! Chip calls it "carrying the one." For 47 + 35, you add the ones: 7 + 5 = 12. That is 1 ten and 2 ones. Carry that extra ten over! 40 + 30 + 10 = 80, plus 2 = 82. Let's practice!

questions.hint: 47 has 4 tens (40) and how many ones? 47 - 40 = 7!
questions.prompt: Break 47 into tens and ones! 47 = 40 + ___

questions.hint: 35 has 3 tens (30) and how many ones? 35 - 30 = 5!
questions.prompt: Break 35 into tens and ones! 35 = 30 + ___

questions.hint: 12 is 1 ten and 2 ones. The extra ten gets carried!
questions.prompt: Add the ones: 7 + 5 = 12. Break 12 into a ten and ones! 12 = 10 + ___

questions.hint: 40 + 30 = 70, then 70 + 10 = 80! That is the tens part.
questions.prompt: Now add ALL the tens: 40 + 30 + 10 = ___. What is the total tens part?

questions.hint: 56 has 5 tens (50) and 6 ones!
questions.prompt: Break 56 into tens and ones! 56 = 50 + ___

questions.hint: Ones: 7 + 5 = 12 (write 2, carry 1). Tens: 4 + 3 + 1 = 8. Answer: 82!
Ones: 6 + 7 = 13 (write 3, carry 1). Tens: 5 + 2 + 1 = 8. Answer: 83!
Ones: 8 + 4 = 12 (write 2, carry 1). Tens: 3 + 4 + 1 = 8. Answer: 82!
Ones: 9 + 3 = 12 (write 2, carry 1). Tens: 2 + 5 + 1 = 8. Answer: 82!
Ones: 5 + 8 = 13 (write 3, carry 1). Tens: 6 + 1 + 1 = 8. Answer: 83!

title: Two-Digit Subtraction
description: Subtract two-digit numbers by hopping backward on the number line! Count back like a pro.
story_text: Chip is counting backward like a champion! When you subtract big numbers, the number line is your best friend. Start at the bigger number and HOP BACK to find the answer. For 63 - 27, start at 63 and jump back 27 spaces. Let's do it together!

questions.hint: Start at 63. Hop back 20 to reach 43, then hop back 7 more to reach 36!
questions.prompt: Solve: 63 - 27 = ? Hop backward from 63!

questions.hint: Start at 52. Hop back 10 to reach 42, then hop back 8 more to reach 34!
questions.prompt: Solve: 52 - 18 = ? Hop backward from 52!

questions.hint: Hop back 3 tens from 75! 75, 65, 55, 45. You land on 45!
questions.prompt: Solve: 75 - 30 = ? Hop backward from 75!

questions.hint: Start at 81. Hop back 40 to reach 41, then hop back 5 more to reach 36!
questions.prompt: Solve: 81 - 45 = ? Hop backward from 81!

questions.hint: Start at 90. Hop back 30 to reach 60, then hop back 4 more to reach 56!
questions.prompt: Solve: 90 - 34 = ? Hop backward from 90!

questions.hint: Start at 63 and count back 27. Or subtract tens first: 63 - 20 = 43, then 43 - 7 = 36!
54 - 30 = 24, but we subtracted 1 too many, so add 1 back: 24 + 1 = 25!
72 - 40 = 32, but we subtracted 2 too many, so add 2 back: 32 + 2 = 34!
Ones: 5 - 6? Regroup! Borrow a ten. 15 - 6 = 9. Tens: 7 - 4 = 3. Answer: 39!

title: Adding Three or Four Numbers
description: Add more than two numbers at once! Look for pairs that make 10 to speed things up.
story_text: Chip went shopping and needs to add up LOTS of prices! When you add 3 or 4 numbers, look for friends of 10 first. If you see 7 and 3, grab them -- that is 10! Then add the rest. Let's help Chip add up the shopping list!

questions.hint: 50 is 5 full rows of 10 beads. Slide them all over!
questions.prompt: Show 12 + 23 + 15 = 50 on the rekenrek! Start with 50.

questions.hint: 30 is 3 full rows of 10 beads. That is 10 + 10 + 10!
questions.prompt: Show 10 + 8 + 12 = 30 on the rekenrek!

questions.hint: 7 + 3 = 10, then add 5 more. 10 + 5 = 15 beads!
questions.prompt: Show 7 + 3 + 5 = 15 on the rekenrek!
Show 6 + 4 + 8 + 2 = 20 on the rekenrek!

questions.hint: Look for friends of 10! 7 + 3 = 10, then 10 + 8 = 18!
Find TWO pairs of 10! 6 + 4 = 10, and 8 + 2 = 10. Then 10 + 10 = 20!
Add tens: 10 + 20 + 10 = 40. Add ones: 2 + 3 + 5 = 10. Together: 40 + 10 = 50!
Add tens: 10 + 10 + 0 = 20. Add ones: 1 + 4 + 9 = 14. Together: 20 + 14 = 34!

title: Meet the Coins
description: Open a piggy bank and learn about pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters!
story_text: Hey there, superstar! Chip just cracked open a giant piggy bank and coins went EVERYWHERE! There are four different kinds of coins in here, and each one is worth a different amount. Let's figure out what each coin is called and how much it is worth!

cards.back: A penny is worth 1 cent (1¢). It is copper-colored (brownish) and has Abraham Lincoln on the front. It is the smallest value coin!
A nickel is worth 5 cents (5¢). It is silver-colored and bigger than a penny. Thomas Jefferson is on the front. 5 pennies = 1 nickel!
A dime is worth 10 cents (10¢). It is the smallest and thinnest coin! Franklin Roosevelt is on the front. 10 pennies = 1 dime!
A quarter is worth 25 cents (25¢). It is the biggest coin! George Washington is on the front. 4 quarters make a whole dollar!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about the four U.S. coins!
Match each coin to its value! 🪙

questions.hint: A quarter is 25 cents -- that is the biggest value of the four coins!
questions.prompt: Which coin is worth the MOST? 🪙

questions.hint: The dime is tiny but worth 10 cents. Don't judge a coin by its size!
questions.prompt: Which coin is the SMALLEST in size but worth MORE than a penny?

questions.hint: A nickel is worth 5 cents, and each penny is 1 cent. 5 pennies = 1 nickel!
questions.prompt: How many pennies make a nickel? 🪵

questions.hint: Only one coin looks different from the rest -- it is brownish instead of silver!
questions.prompt: Which coin has a copper (brownish) color?

title: Counting Coins
description: Add up groups of mixed coins to find out how much money you have!
story_text: Chip was cleaning the couch and found coins hidden in ALL the cushions! There are dimes, nickels, quarters, and pennies mixed up together. Can you help Chip count them? Remember the trick: start with the biggest coins first, then add the smaller ones!

questions.hint: Each dime is 10 cents. Count by 10s: 10, 20, 30. That is 30¢!
questions.prompt: Count the dimes! Each dime is worth 10¢.

questions.hint: Each nickel is 5 cents. Count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20. That is 20¢!
questions.prompt: Count the nickels! Each nickel is worth 5¢.

questions.hint: Each quarter is 25 cents. 25 + 25 = 50¢. That is half a dollar!
questions.prompt: Count the quarters! Each quarter is worth 25¢.

questions.hint: Each penny is 1 cent. Count: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. That is 5¢!
questions.prompt: Count all the pennies! Each penny is worth 1¢.
3 dimes + 2 nickels = ___ cents

questions.hint: 25 + 10 + 5 = 40¢! Start with the biggest coin.
questions.prompt: 1 quarter + 1 dime + 1 nickel = ___ cents

questions.hint: 2 quarters = 50¢. Plus 3 pennies = 3¢. 50 + 3 = 53¢!
questions.prompt: 2 quarters + 3 pennies = ___ cents

questions.hint: 4 dimes = 40¢. 1 nickel = 5¢. 2 pennies = 2¢. 40 + 5 + 2 = 47¢!
questions.prompt: 4 dimes + 1 nickel + 2 pennies = ___ cents

questions.hint: 25 + 20 + 5 + 3 = 53¢! Go from biggest to smallest.
questions.prompt: 1 quarter + 2 dimes + 1 nickel + 3 pennies = ___ cents

title: Making Change
description: Figure out how much change you get back when you buy something!
story_text: Welcome to Chip's Pretend Store! Chip has toys, stickers, and snacks for sale. But when you pay with coins, sometimes you give MORE money than the price. The extra money you get back is called CHANGE. Let's practice making change!

questions.hint: You paid 50¢ for something that costs 35¢. 50 - 35 = 15¢ change!
questions.prompt: A sticker costs 35¢. You pay with 2 quarters (50¢). How much change do you get back? 🌟

questions.hint: 25 - 20 = 5. You get 5 cents back -- that is one nickel!
questions.prompt: A pencil costs 20¢. You pay with a quarter (25¢). How much change? ✏️

questions.hint: 50¢ minus 40¢ = 10¢ change! That is one dime.
questions.prompt: A toy car costs 40¢. You pay with 2 quarters (50¢). How much change? 🚗

questions.hint: 20¢ minus 15¢ = 5¢ change. That is one nickel!
questions.prompt: An eraser costs 15¢. You pay with 2 dimes (20¢). How much change?

questions.hint: 75¢ minus 60¢ = 15¢. You get 15 cents back!
questions.prompt: A candy bar costs 60¢. You pay with 3 quarters (75¢). How much change? 🍫

questions.hint: 2 quarters = 50¢. The bookmark costs 30¢. 50 - 30 = 20¢!
questions.prompt: A bookmark costs 30¢. You pay with 2 quarters. Your change is ___ cents.

questions.hint: 1 quarter = 25¢. Minus 10¢ for the gumball. 25 - 10 = 15¢!
questions.prompt: A gumball costs 10¢. You pay with a quarter. Your change is ___ cents.

questions.hint: 2 quarters = 50¢. 50 - 45 = 5¢. You get a nickel back!
questions.prompt: A ring pop costs 45¢. You pay with 2 quarters. Your change is ___ cents.

questions.hint: 3 quarters = 75¢. 75 - 55 = 20¢. Two dimes back!
questions.prompt: A bouncy ball costs 55¢. You pay with 3 quarters. Your change is ___ cents.

title: Telling Time to 5 Minutes
description: Learn to read clocks and tell time to the nearest 5 minutes!
story_text: Tick-tock, tick-tock! Chip wants to learn how to read a clock. A clock has two hands: the SHORT hand tells the HOUR, and the LONG hand tells the MINUTES. The long hand points to numbers, and each number means 5 minutes. Let's practice reading clocks together!

pairs.left: Hour hand on 3, minute hand on 12
Hour hand on 7, minute hand on 6
Hour hand past 2, minute hand on 3
Hour hand past 10, minute hand on 9
Hour hand past 5, minute hand on 2
Hour hand past 8, minute hand on 7

activities.prompt: Match the clock description to the correct digital time! ⏰

questions.hint: Each number on the clock means 5 minutes. The 3 means: 5, 10, 15 minutes!
questions.prompt: The minute hand points to the 3. That means ___ minutes past the hour.

questions.hint: Count by 5s to the 6: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 minutes! That is also called half past.
questions.prompt: The minute hand points to the 6. That means ___ minutes past the hour.

questions.hint: When the minute hand is on 12, it means zero minutes -- it is exactly on the hour!
questions.prompt: The minute hand points to the 12. That means ___ minutes past the hour.

questions.hint: Count by 5s to the 9: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45 minutes!
questions.prompt: The hour hand is on 4 and the minute hand is on 9. The time is 4:___.

questions.hint: Count by 5s to the 4: 5, 10, 15, 20 minutes past 11!
questions.prompt: The hour hand is past 11 and the minute hand is on 4. The time is 11:___.
It is 6:___. The minute hand is on the 11.

title: A.M. and P.M.
description: Learn the difference between A.M. (morning) and P.M. (afternoon/night) and order daily activities by time!
story_text: Good morning! Or is it good afternoon? Chip gets confused sometimes! A.M. means the time from midnight to noon (morning time), and P.M. means the time from noon to midnight (afternoon and night). Let's follow Chip through a whole day and figure out which times are A.M. and which are P.M.!

questions.hint: Breakfast is in the morning. Morning times use A.M.!
questions.prompt: You eat breakfast at 7:00. Is that A.M. or P.M.? 🥞

questions.hint: Dinner is in the evening. Evening times use P.M.!
questions.prompt: You eat dinner at 6:00. Is that A.M. or P.M.? 🍝

questions.hint: You go to school in the morning. A.M. means morning!
questions.prompt: School starts at 8:30. Is that A.M. or P.M.? 🏫

questions.hint: Bedtime is at night. Night times use P.M.!
questions.prompt: You go to bed at 8:00. Is that A.M. or P.M.? 💤

questions.hint: Sunrise happens in the early morning. That is A.M.!
questions.prompt: The sun rises at 6:30. Is that A.M. or P.M.? 🌅

questions.hint: P.M. is afternoon and night. Brushing teeth before bed happens at night!
questions.prompt: Which of these happens in the P.M.? 🌙

options.text: Riding the bus to school at 7:45

questions.hint: Start with the earliest morning activity (7:00 A.M.) and end with bedtime (8:00 P.M.)!
questions.prompt: Put Chip's daily activities in order from EARLIEST to LATEST! 📅

questions.hint: A.M. comes first (morning), then P.M. (afternoon and night). 6:00 A.M. is the earliest!
questions.prompt: Put these times in order from EARLIEST to LATEST! ⏰

title: Equal Parts: Halves
description: Learn what it means to split something into two equal parts! Each part is one half.
story_text: Hey there, superstar! Chip here! Imagine you have a yummy pizza and you want to share it with your best friend. If you cut it RIGHT down the middle so both pieces are the SAME size, each piece is ONE HALF! But watch out -- if the pieces are NOT the same size, those are NOT halves. Let's figure out which shapes are split into real halves!

questions.hint: Halves must be EQUAL sizes! If one piece is bigger, they are not halves.
questions.prompt: A circle is split into 2 pieces. One piece is BIG and the other is small. Is this cut into halves? 🍕

options.text: No, the pieces are not equal

questions.hint: Two pieces that are the SAME size means each one is one half! Great job!
questions.prompt: A sandwich is cut straight down the middle into 2 pieces that are the SAME size. Is this cut into halves? 🥪

questions.hint: When you split something into 2 EQUAL parts, each part is called one half!
questions.prompt: A rectangle is split into 2 equal parts. What do we call each part?

questions.hint: A line in the exact middle makes 2 EQUAL parts -- those are halves!
questions.prompt: Which of these shows a shape cut into halves? 🤔

options.text: A square with a line in the exact middle
A square with a line near the edge
A square with no line at all

questions.hint: If you put both halves back together, you get the whole thing! 2 halves = 1 whole.
questions.prompt: How many halves make a whole? 🌟

left.text: Rectangle: one big piece and one tiny piece
Square split right down the middle

activities.prompt: Match each shape description to "Halves" or "Not Halves"! ✂️

title: Thirds and Fourths
description: Split shapes into 3 equal parts (thirds) and 4 equal parts (fourths)!
story_text: Chip is having a party and baked some brownies! If Chip shares a brownie with 2 friends, that is 3 people total -- so we cut it into 3 EQUAL pieces called THIRDS. If Chip invites one MORE friend, that is 4 people -- so we cut it into 4 EQUAL pieces called FOURTHS. The more friends you share with, the smaller each piece gets!

questions.hint: 3 equal pieces means each piece is 1 out of 3 -- that is one third!
questions.prompt: A brownie is cut into 3 EQUAL pieces. What do we call each piece? 🍫

questions.hint: 4 equal parts means each part is 1 out of 4 -- that is one fourth!
questions.prompt: A rectangle is split into 4 EQUAL parts. What do we call each part? 🟨

questions.hint: Fewer pieces means each piece is bigger! 3 pieces are bigger than 4 pieces of the same thing.
questions.prompt: Which is BIGGER: one third (1/3) or one fourth (1/4)? 🤔

questions.hint: You started with 4 fourths and ate 1. 4 - 1 = 3 fourths left!
questions.prompt: A pie is cut into 4 equal slices. You eat 1 slice. How many fourths are LEFT? 🥧

questions.hint: If you split something into thirds and put all 3 pieces back, you get the whole thing!
questions.prompt: How many thirds make a whole? 🌟

questions.hint: If a shape is split into 4 equal parts, there are 4 fourths total!
questions.prompt: A circle split into 4 equal parts has ___ fourths.

questions.hint: 3 equal parts means 3 thirds. Each piece is one third!
questions.prompt: A rectangle split into 3 equal parts has ___ thirds.

questions.hint: A whole pizza has 4 fourths. You ate 2, so 4 - 2 = 2 fourths left!
questions.prompt: You eat 2 fourths of a pizza. There are ___ fourths left.

questions.hint: 3 thirds minus 1 third equals 2 thirds!
questions.prompt: A cake is cut into thirds. Chip eats 1 third. There are ___ thirds left. 🎂

title: Arrays: Rows and Columns
description: Arrange objects in rows and columns to make arrays! Count the total in each array.
story_text: Chip loves being organized! Today Chip is lining up toy soldiers in neat ROWS and COLUMNS. A row goes side to side like this: ➡️. A column goes up and down like this: ⬇️. When objects are arranged in rows and columns, we call it an ARRAY. Let's count how many are in each array!

questions.hint: Count row by row: 3 stars in the first row, 3 stars in the second row. 3 + 3 = 6!
questions.prompt: Count all the stars in this array! (2 rows, 3 in each row)

questions.hint: 3 rows with 4 apples each: 4 + 4 + 4 = 12 apples!
questions.prompt: Count all the apples! (3 rows, 4 in each row)

questions.hint: 4 rows with 3 flowers each: 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 12 flowers!
questions.prompt: Count all the flowers! (4 rows, 3 in each row)

questions.hint: 5 rows with 2 hearts each: 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 10 hearts!
questions.prompt: Count all the hearts! (5 rows, 2 in each row)

questions.hint: 3 rows with 5 rockets each: 5 + 5 + 5 = 15 rockets!
questions.prompt: Count all the rockets! (3 rows, 5 in each row)

questions.hint: 2 rows of 4 means 4 + 4 = 8 total!
questions.prompt: An array has 2 rows and 4 columns. ___ rows of ___ = ___ total. (Enter the total.)

questions.hint: 3 rows of 3: that is 3 + 3 + 3 = 9!
questions.prompt: An array has 3 rows and 3 columns. How many objects in total? ___

questions.hint: 4 rows of 2: that is 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 8!
questions.prompt: An array has 4 rows and 2 columns. How many objects in total? ___

questions.hint: 5 rows of 3: that is 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 15!
questions.prompt: An array has 5 rows and 3 columns. How many objects in total? ___

title: Repeated Addition with Arrays
description: Discover that adding equal groups over and over is a shortcut for counting! Arrays make it easy.
story_text: Chip just discovered something AMAZING! Instead of counting every single object one by one, you can ADD equal groups together. If you have 4 rows of 3 stars, you do not need to count all 12 -- just add 3 + 3 + 3 + 3! Adding the SAME number over and over is called REPEATED ADDITION. It's like a superpower for counting fast!

questions.hint: You are adding 3 four times! 3, 6, 9, 12!
Three groups of 5! Count by 5s: 5, 10, 15!
Five groups of 2! Count by 2s: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10!
Three groups of 4! 4, 8, 12!
3 groups of 2 cupcakes: 2, 4, 6! That is 6 cupcakes total!
questions.prompt: An array has 3 rows of 2 cupcakes 🧁. Write the repeated addition answer: 2 + 2 + 2 = ___

questions.hint: 4 rows of 5 means you add 5 four times: 5 + 5 + 5 + 5 = 20!
questions.prompt: Which repeated addition equation matches an array with 4 rows of 5? 🧩

questions.hint: 3 rows of 4 means you add 4 three times: 4 + 4 + 4 = 12!
questions.prompt: Which repeated addition equation matches an array with 3 rows of 4? 🌟

questions.hint: 5 bags of 3 cookies means add 3 five times: 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 15 cookies!
questions.prompt: Chip has 5 bags with 3 cookies in each bag 🍪. Which equation finds the total?

questions.hint: You are adding 2 three times, which is 3 rows of 2!
questions.prompt: 2 + 2 + 2 = 6. Which array matches this equation? 🤖

questions.hint: 4 groups of 2 means add 2 four times: 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 8!
questions.prompt: Which is the SAME as counting 4 groups of 2? 💡

title: One-Step Word Problems
description: Solve word problems with one step! Read the story, find the math, and get the answer.
story_text: Welcome to Chip's Word Problem Workshop! Word problems are like little math stories. You read the story, figure out what math to do, and solve it! Today we will practice ONE-STEP problems -- that means just one addition or subtraction. You've got this!

questions.hint: Sam GIVES stickers away, so he has FEWER. Giving away means subtract!
questions.prompt: Sam has 45 stickers. He gives 18 to his friend. What should you do to find how many Sam has now? 🌟

questions.hint: Emma FINDS more crayons, so she has MORE. Finding more means add!
questions.prompt: Emma has 23 crayons. She finds 15 more in her desk. What should you do? 🖍️

questions.hint: Kids go AWAY, so there are fewer left. Subtract the ones who left from the total!
questions.prompt: There are 32 kids on the playground. 14 go inside. How do you find how many are left? 🎢

questions.hint: More people GET ON the bus, so the number goes UP. Add!
questions.prompt: A bus has 28 people. 19 more get on at the next stop. What should you do? 🚌

questions.hint: 45 minus 18... break it up! 45 - 10 = 35, then 35 - 8 = 27!
questions.prompt: Sam has 45 stickers. He gives 18 to his friend. How many stickers does Sam have now? 🌟 Answer: ___
Emma has 23 crayons. She finds 15 more. How many crayons does Emma have? 🖍️ Answer: ___
A baker made 50 cookies. He sold 34. How many are left? 🍪 Answer: ___
There are 37 birds in a tree. 21 more fly in. How many birds now? 🐦 Answer: ___

questions.hint: 62 minus 29... 62 - 30 = 32, then add 1 back = 33! (We subtracted 1 too many.)
questions.prompt: Mia has 62 beads. She uses 29 for a necklace. How many beads are left? 💎 Answer: ___

title: Two-Step Word Problems
description: Break big problems into two small steps! Solve one step at a time like a math detective.
story_text: Chip has a secret trick for solving BIG word problems -- break them into TWO small steps! First do one piece of math, then use that answer to do the second piece. It's like climbing stairs: one step at a time gets you to the top! Let's try it!

questions.hint: First add the apples Lily picks! 24 + 13 = 37.
questions.prompt: Lily has 24 apples 🍎. She picks 13 more, then gives 10 away. Step 1: 24 + 13 = ___

questions.hint: Now subtract the apples she gives away! 37 - 10 = 27.
questions.prompt: Lily had 37 apples after picking. She gives 10 away. Step 2: 37 - 10 = ___

questions.hint: First subtract the marbles Jake loses! 30 - 12 = 18.
questions.prompt: Jake has 30 marbles. He loses 12, then finds 8 more. Step 1: 30 - 12 = ___

questions.hint: Now add the marbles he finds! 18 + 8 = 26.
questions.prompt: Jake had 18 marbles left. He finds 8 more. Step 2: 18 + 8 = ___
A box has 42 toys. Kids take out 15, then put back 6. How many toys are in the box? 📦 Answer: ___

questions.hint: 35 minus 20 equals... the shelf has 15 books after the first step!
questions.prompt: Break this two-step problem into parts! A shelf has 35 books. 20 are taken out, 9 are put back. First step: 35 splits into 20 and what?

questions.hint: 15 plus 9 equals 24! The shelf now has 24 books.
questions.prompt: Now the second step! 15 books on the shelf, plus 9 put back. What is the new total?

questions.hint: 50 minus 22... think of it as 50 - 20 = 30, then 30 - 2 = 28!
questions.prompt: Maria has 50 coins. She spends 22, then earns 10 back. What is 50 minus 22?

questions.hint: 28 plus 10 equals 38! Maria ends up with 38 coins.
questions.prompt: Maria had 28 coins left. She earns 10 more. What is the final total?

title: Measurement Word Problems
description: Solve word problems about length, weight, and more! Use a number line to help you.
story_text: Chip is measuring things around the classroom today! How long is this ribbon? How tall is that plant? We can use math to compare measurements and find the difference. Grab your thinking cap and let's measure!

questions.hint: Find the difference! Start at 25 and count up to 48. That is 23 cm longer!
questions.prompt: Tom's ribbon is 48 cm. Jane's is 25 cm. How much LONGER is Tom's ribbon? Start at 25 and hop to 48. 🎀

questions.hint: Start at 5 and count up to 19. The difference is 14 cm!
questions.prompt: A pencil is 19 cm long. An eraser is 5 cm long. How much longer is the pencil? Start at 5 and hop to 19. ✏️

questions.hint: Hop from 65 to 90 on the number line. That is 25 cm taller!
questions.prompt: A bookshelf is 90 cm tall. A table is 65 cm tall. How much taller is the bookshelf? Start at 65 and hop to 90. 📚

questions.hint: 72 minus 18... hop back from 72! 72 - 10 = 62, then 62 - 8 = 54.
questions.prompt: A snake is 72 cm long. It sheds skin and is now 72 cm minus 18 cm. Start at 72 and hop back 18. 🐍

questions.hint: Subtract! 48 - 25 = 23 cm. Tom's ribbon is 23 cm longer!
questions.prompt: Tom's ribbon is 48 cm. Jane's ribbon is 25 cm. How much longer is Tom's ribbon? ___ cm 🎀

questions.hint: Add the growth! 34 + 17 = 51 cm tall.
questions.prompt: A plant is 34 cm tall. It grows 17 cm more. How tall is it now? ___ cm 🌱

questions.hint: Find the difference! 56 - 39 = 17 meters farther.
questions.prompt: Aiden walks 56 meters to school. Bella walks 39 meters. How much farther does Aiden walk? ___ meters 🚶

questions.hint: Cutting means subtract! 80 - 35 = 45 cm.
questions.prompt: A rope is 80 cm long. You cut off 35 cm. How long is the rope now? ___ cm 🧵

questions.hint: Find the difference! 44 - 28 = 16 cm longer.
questions.prompt: One fish is 28 cm long. Another fish is 44 cm long. How much longer is the second fish? ___ cm 🐟

title: Picture Graphs & Bar Graphs
description: Read picture graphs and bar graphs to answer questions! Data tells a story.
story_text: Chip surveyed the whole class about their favorite things! Now we have lots of data, but how do we SHOW it? With GRAPHS! A picture graph uses little pictures, and a bar graph uses bars. Let's learn to read them and answer questions like a data detective!

questions.hint: Look at the data next to the apple emoji! Apple: 8 kids.
questions.prompt: Chip's class voted for their favorite fruit! 🍎 Apple: 8 kids, 🍌 Banana: 5 kids, 🍇 Grapes: 3 kids, 🍊 Orange: 6 kids. How many kids chose Apple?

questions.hint: The most popular fruit has the BIGGEST number. 8 is the biggest!
questions.prompt: Same fruit graph! 🍎 Apple: 8, 🍌 Banana: 5, 🍇 Grapes: 3, 🍊 Orange: 6. Which fruit was the MOST popular?

questions.hint: The least popular fruit has the SMALLEST number. 3 is the smallest!
questions.prompt: Same fruit graph! 🍎 Apple: 8, 🍌 Banana: 5, 🍇 Grapes: 3, 🍊 Orange: 6. Which fruit was the LEAST popular?

questions.hint: Subtract! Apple (8) minus Grapes (3) = 5. Five more kids chose Apple!
questions.prompt: Same fruit graph! 🍎 Apple: 8, 🍌 Banana: 5, 🍇 Grapes: 3, 🍊 Orange: 6. How many MORE kids chose Apple than Grapes?

questions.hint: Add them all up! 7 + 5 + 2 + 4 = 18 kids total!
questions.prompt: Chip asked kids about their favorite pet! 🐶 Dog: 7, 🐱 Cat: 5, 🐠 Fish: 2, 🐰 Rabbit: 4. How many kids voted in TOTAL?

questions.hint: Dog (7) minus Cat (5) = 2. Two more kids chose Dog!
questions.prompt: Pet graph! 🐶 Dog: 7, 🐱 Cat: 5, 🐠 Fish: 2, 🐰 Rabbit: 4. How many MORE kids chose Dog than Cat?

left.text: How many Sunny + Rainy days total?
How many more Cloudy than Snowy?
How many days of weather data in all?

activities.prompt: Match each graph question to its answer! Use Chip's weather graph: ☀️ Sunny: 10 days, 🌧️ Rainy: 4 days, ☁️ Cloudy: 6 days, ❄️ Snowy: 2 days.

title: Letter Sounds
description: Review consonant sounds and discover what sound each letter makes! Build a letter flashcard display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip is whispering to himself. "Buh... buh... B! Sss... sss... S!" he says. "Every letter has its own special sound! B says buh, S says sss, M says mmm. But some letters are sneaky -- C can say kuh OR sss! Let us listen to every letter and learn their sounds. I will show each letter on my screen and give you a sound hint with my buzzer. Press the button when you know the sound!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">B says buh!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Consonants!", 15, 5)

consonants = "BCDFGHJKLMNPQRSTVWXYZ"
for i in range(len(consonants)):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 70, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(consonants[i], 55, 50)
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 90, 5, 0x22C55E)
    Speaker.tone(350 + i * 25, 150)
    time.sleep(0.8)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 70, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("All done!", 25, 60)
Speaker.tone(880, 400)

hints.text: Show a title at the top like Consonants! to set up the lesson.
Use a loop to show each consonant letter one at a time.
Display each letter large in the center using Display Text.
Play a unique tone for each letter using Buzzer Tone.
Add a Wait block so you can say each sound before the next letter appears.

story.title: The Sound Discovery
story.narrative: Chip realizes every letter has a secret sound. Help him learn them all!

create.title: Sound Collection
create.prompt: Find 3 things near you that start with the same letter sound. Tell Chip what you found!

explore.title: Letter Sound Cards
explore.instructions: The display shows a letter. The buzzer plays a sound hint. Press Button A to hear the sound, Button B for next letter.
explore.device_interaction: Display shows large letter. Buzzer plays tone hint for the sound. Buttons navigate and confirm.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click through letter cards with audio playback.

celebrate.title: Sound Spotter!
celebrate.summary: You know the sounds of all the consonant letters! These sounds are the building blocks of reading.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child reviewed consonant phonics with multi-sensory reinforcement (visual letter + auditory tone).

title: Vowel Valley
description: Meet the 5 special letters -- A, E, I, O, U! Build a vowel display on your M5Stick that highlights each vowel with its own color and sound.
story_text: Chip looks amazed. "There are 5 SUPER special letters," he says. "A, E, I, O, and U -- they are called VOWELS and every single word needs at least one of them! They are like the glue that holds words together. A says ah like in apple, E says eh like in egg, I says ih like in igloo. Let us visit Vowel Valley and meet them all!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">A E I O U</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Vowel Valley!", 10, 5)

vowels = ["A", "E", "I", "O", "U"]
colors = [0xEF4444, 0xF97316, 0xEAB308, 0x22C55E, 0x3B82F6]
tones = [440, 494, 523, 587, 659]

for i in range(5):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(colors[i], 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(vowels[i], 55, 50)
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 85, 10, colors[i])
    Speaker.tone(tones[i], 400)
    time.sleep(1.2)

Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("A E I O U", 15, 55)
Speaker.tone(880, 400)

hints.text: Show a title like Vowel Valley at the top of the screen.
Create a list of the 5 vowels: A, E, I, O, U.
Use a loop to display each vowel one at a time, big and centered.
Give each vowel a different color to make them easy to remember!
Play a musical tone for each vowel -- higher notes for later vowels.

story.title: Welcome to Vowel Valley
story.narrative: Five special letters live in Vowel Valley. Every word needs at least one of them!

create.title: Vowel Song
create.prompt: Make up a silly sentence where every word has the same vowel sound. Like: The fat cat sat on a mat!

explore.title: Meet the Vowels
explore.instructions: The display shows CVC word frames with the vowel highlighted. Buzzer plays the vowel sound. Press buttons to cycle vowels.
explore.device_interaction: Display shows vowel in word context. Buzzer plays vowel sound. Buttons swap vowels in CVC frame.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click to hear vowel sounds and swap them in words.

celebrate.title: Vowel Victor!
celebrate.summary: You met all 5 vowels and know their short sounds. Every word you read uses these sounds!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child practiced short vowel identification and discrimination in CVC word contexts.

title: Word Builder
description: Blend consonants and vowels to build CVC words like cat, dog, and run! Build a word builder on your M5Stick that shows words letter by letter.
story_text: Chip has letter tiles scattered everywhere. "I know the sounds," he says, "but how do I stick them together to make a WORD? If I put C... A... T together and say them fast -- cuh-ah-tuh -- CAT! I made a word! That is called BLENDING. Let us build more words! Tilt me to scroll through letters and lock them in place!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">c-a-t</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Word Builder!", 10, 5)

words = ["cat", "dog", "run", "big", "hop"]
for word in words:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    x = 20
    for letter in word:
        Lcd.drawString(letter, x, 50)
        Speaker.tone(500, 150)
        time.sleep(0.5)
        x += 30
    time.sleep(0.5)
    Lcd.drawString(word + "!", 35, 85)
    Speaker.tone(700, 300)
    time.sleep(1.2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Great words!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Show a title like Word Builder at the top of the screen.
Pick a CVC word like cat and display it letter by letter.
Use Display Text to place each letter with a space between them.
Play a beep for each letter, then a longer tone when the full word appears.
Add a Wait between letters so you can sound out each one!

story.title: Letter Factory
story.narrative: Chip has letter tiles but needs to blend them into words. Help him build his first words!

create.title: Word Chain
create.prompt: Start with any 3-letter word. Change ONE letter to make a new word. How long can your chain go?

explore.title: Blend It Together
explore.instructions: Display shows 3 letter slots. Tilt to scroll through letters for each position. Press to lock in your choice and hear the word!
explore.device_interaction: Display shows letter slots. IMU tilt scrolls letter options. Buttons lock letters and play blended word.
explore.simulator_fallback: Drag letters into slots to build words.

celebrate.title: Word Builder!
celebrate.summary: You can blend sounds together to read real words! C-A-T, D-O-G, R-U-N -- you are reading!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child practiced blending CVC words, a foundational decoding skill for early reading.

title: Letter Sound Safari!
description: Flip each card to discover what sound each letter makes! Build a letter sound flashcard game on your M5Stick.
story_text: Welcome to the Letter Sound Safari! Chip found a magical deck of cards in the jungle.
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_rect" x="20" y="20"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">75</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">?</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">55</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sound Safari!", 10, 5)

letters = list("ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ")
random.shuffle(letters)

for i in range(8):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.fillRect(30, 35, 75, 50, 0x22C55E)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x000000, 0x22C55E)
    Lcd.drawString("?", 63, 55)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    Lcd.fillRect(30, 35, 75, 50, 0xFFFFFF)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0xFFFFFF)
    Lcd.drawString(letters[i], 60, 55)
    Speaker.tone(440 + i * 40, 250)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Great safari!", 15, 60)

hints.text: Draw a card shape using Display Rect with a question mark on it.
Wait a moment, then flip the card by changing the color and showing a letter.
Use a Loop to show several letter cards one after another.
Play a sound when each card flips to reveal the letter!
Shuffle the letters so they come in a random order each time.

back.text: ah like Apple!
buh like Bear!
kuh like Cat!
duh like Dog!
eh like Elephant!
fff like Frog!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn the letter sound!

title: Rhyme Time!
description: Match words that rhyme! Build a rhyming word display on your M5Stick that shows pairs of words that sound the same at the end.
story_text: Chip loves rhyming! Words that rhyme sound alike at the end -- like cat and hat! Match the rhyming pairs!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">cat - hat</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Rhyme Time!", 15, 5)

pairs = [("cat","hat"), ("dog","log"), ("fun","run"), ("big","pig"), ("hop","pop")]
for w1, w2 in pairs:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(w1, 20, 50)
    Speaker.tone(500, 150)
    time.sleep(0.8)

    Speaker.tone(500, 150)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    Lcd.drawString("Rhyme!", 40, 85)
    Speaker.tone(800, 300)
    time.sleep(1.2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Rhyme star!", 15, 60)

hints.text: Show the title Rhyme Time! at the top of the screen.
Display the first word on the left side of the screen.
Wait a moment, then show the rhyming word on the right side.
Add the word Rhyme! below the pair when both words are shown.
Play matching tones for rhyming words -- same pitch means they rhyme!

activities.hint: Listen to the ending sounds. Cat and Hat both end in -at!
activities.prompt: Match the words that rhyme!

title: Sight Word Sprint
description: Learn to recognize common sight words instantly! Build a speed reading display on your M5Stick with timed word flashes.
story_text: Chip is reading a book but keeps getting stuck. "This word keeps showing up everywhere," he says, pointing at THE. "T-H-E... thuh? No wait, it is THE! Some words do not follow the rules -- you just have to remember what they look like. They are called SIGHT WORDS because you recognize them on sight! Let us practice with flashcards. Shake me to shuffle!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">the</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">40</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">550</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">150</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Word Sprint!", 15, 5)

words = ["the","and","is","you","said","he","she","was","for","on"]
random.shuffle(words)

for i in range(8):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 70, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(words[i], 35, 55)
    Speaker.tone(550, 100)
    time.sleep(1.5)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 70, 0x000000)
    time.sleep(0.3)

Lcd.drawString("Fast reader!", 10, 60)
Speaker.tone(880, 400)

hints.text: Create a list of 10 common sight words.
Shuffle the words so they appear in random order.
Flash each word on screen for about 1.5 seconds.
Clear the screen briefly between words.
Play a beep for each new word to keep the pace!

story.title: The Word Wall
story.narrative: Some tricky words appear everywhere but do not follow phonics rules. Learn to spot them instantly!

create.title: Sight Word Story
create.prompt: Pick 3 sight words and make up a sentence using all of them. Read it to Chip!

explore.title: Flashcard Sprint
explore.instructions: Shake the device to shuffle to a new sight word. Press Know if you recognize it, Practice if you need more time.
explore.device_interaction: Display shows sight word. IMU shake shuffles. Buttons sort into Know/Practice piles.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click through flashcards and sort them.

celebrate.title: Sight Word Superstar!
celebrate.summary: You can recognize common sight words on sight. This makes reading so much faster!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child practiced Dolch 1st grade sight words using flashcard-style recognition and repetition.

title: Rhyme Time
description: Discover rhyming words and word families! Build a rhyme matcher on your M5Stick that shows word families together.
story_text: Chip starts giggling. "Cat, hat, bat, mat -- they all sound the same at the end!" he laughs. "AT! They all end in AT! Words that sound alike at the end are called RHYMING WORDS. And they belong to the same word family! The AT family, the IG family, the UN family. Let us find rhyming words and see how big each family is!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">-at family</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">cat hat bat</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Rhyme Time!", 15, 5)

families = [
    ("-at", ["cat","hat","bat","rat"]),
    ("-ig", ["big","pig","dig","wig"]),
    ("-op", ["hop","pop","top","mop"]),
    ("-an", ["can","man","fan","ran"])
]

for ending, words in families:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xF97316, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(ending + " family", 10, 30)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    y = 50
    for word in words:
        Lcd.drawString(word, 20, y)
        Speaker.tone(500, 80)
        time.sleep(0.5)
        y += 15
    time.sleep(1)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Rhyme master!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Show a title like Rhyme Time! at the top.
Pick a word ending like -at and display it as the family name.
List words that belong to that family: cat, hat, bat, rat.
Show each word one at a time with a small beep.
Move to the next word family after a pause!

story.title: The Rhyme Family Reunion
story.narrative: Words that sound alike at the end are cousins! Help them find their rhyming families.

create.title: Silly Rhyme Poem
create.prompt: Make a silly 2-line poem where the last words rhyme! Like: I saw a cat, wearing a hat!

explore.title: Rhyme Detector
explore.instructions: The display shows a word and the buzzer plays it. Then you hear two options -- pick the one that rhymes!
explore.device_interaction: Display shows target word. Buzzer plays word and two options. Buttons choose the rhyme.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click to hear words and select the rhyme.

celebrate.title: Rhyme Rockstar!
celebrate.summary: You can hear and make rhyming words! Word families help you read lots of new words fast.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child practiced phonological awareness through rhyme recognition and production.

title: Spelling Bee
description: Practice spelling common words by building them letter by letter on your M5Stick display!
story_text: Chip shows a picture of a cat on his screen. "I know this word," he says, "it is CAT! But how do I write it? I need to think about the sounds. Cuh-ah-tuh. C... A... T! I spelled it! Now let us try more words. I will show you a picture and say the word, and you spell it letter by letter. Each correct letter gets a happy beep!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">c _ t</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Spelling Bee!", 10, 5)

words = [("c_t","cat"), ("d_g","dog"), ("r_n","run"), ("b_g","big"), ("h_p","hop")]
for blank, full in words:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 30, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(blank, 40, 45)
    time.sleep(1.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xF97316, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(full + "!", 40, 75)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    Speaker.tone(770, 200)

title: Sentence Builder
description: Learn to put words in the right order to make sentences! Build a sentence display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip has word cards all mixed up! "Dog the big ran," he reads. "That does not make sense! Words need to go in a special order to make a SENTENCE. And sentences need a capital letter at the start and a period at the end. Help me put these words in the right order: The... big... dog... ran. THE BIG DOG RAN! That makes sense! Tilt to rearrange!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">The cat sat.</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sentences!", 20, 5)

words_list = [
    (["The","cat","sat"], "The cat sat."),
    (["I","can","run"], "I can run."),
    (["She","is","big"], "She is big."),
    (["He","has","fun"], "He has fun."),
    (["We","are","happy"], "We are happy.")
]

for words, sentence in words_list:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    x = 10
    for word in words:
        Lcd.drawString(word, x, 45)
        Speaker.tone(500, 100)
        time.sleep(0.5)
        x += 35
    time.sleep(0.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xF97316, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(sentence, 10, 75)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    Speaker.tone(770, 200)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sentence pro!", 5, 60)

title: Story Sequencing
description: Every story has a beginning, middle, and end. Build a visual story timeline on your M5Stick!
story_text: Chip is telling a story but gets it all mixed up. "And then the bear went to sleep. Wait no, first the bear woke up and ate honey. Actually, first it was morning and the bear was hungry..." He sighs. "Stories have an ORDER! First is the BEGINNING, then the MIDDLE, then the END. Let us practice putting stories in the right order!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Beginning</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">35</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Story Parts!", 15, 5)

stories = [
    ("Egg hatches", "Chick grows", "Chicken!"),
    ("Get dressed", "Walk to bus", "At school!"),
    ("Get flour", "Mix and bake", "Eat bread!")
]
colors = [0x3B82F6, 0xEAB308, 0x22C55E]
labels = ["Begin:", "Middle:", "End:"]

for story in stories:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    for i in range(3):
        Lcd.setTextColor(colors[i], 0x000000)
        Lcd.drawString(labels[i], 5, 30 + i * 25)
        Lcd.drawString(story[i], 55, 30 + i * 25)
        Speaker.tone(400 + i * 100, 150)
        time.sleep(1)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sequence pro!", 5, 60)

title: Digraph Detectives
description: Learn the ch, sh, th, and wh sounds! Build a digraph display on your M5Stick that shows each special letter pair.
story_text: Hey there, detective! Chip here! Some sneaky letter pairs make brand new sounds when they team up. CH says "ch" like in cheese, SH says "sh" like in ship, TH says "th" like in think, and WH says "wh" like in whale. Let's find them all!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">ch - chip</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Digraphs!", 25, 5)

digraphs = [
    ("ch", ["chip","chat","chop"]),
    ("sh", ["ship","shop","shed"]),
    ("th", ["the","that","this"]),
    ("wh", ["when","what","why"])
]
colors = [0xEF4444, 0x3B82F6, 0x22C55E, 0xF97316]

for i, (dg, words) in enumerate(digraphs):
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(colors[i], 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(dg, 55, 30)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    for j, word in enumerate(words):
        Lcd.drawString(word, 10 + j * 40, 60)
        Speaker.tone(500, 80)
        time.sleep(0.4)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Digraph pro!", 10, 60)
