questions.prompt: I can ___ap my hands! 👏

title: Long Vowel Magic
description: Discover how silent e makes vowels say their own name! Build a magic e display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip found a magic wand -- it's the letter E! When you put a silent E at the end of a word, it makes the vowel say its OWN NAME. Watch: "cap" becomes "cape" and "hop" becomes "hope." That sneaky silent E changes everything!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">cap -> cape</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Magic E!", 30, 5)

pairs = [("cap","cape"), ("pin","pine"), ("hop","hope"), ("tub","tube"), ("kit","kite")]
for short_w, long_w in pairs:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(short_w, 20, 40)
    time.sleep(1)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xF97316, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("+ e =", 55, 40)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(long_w, 20, 65)
    Speaker.tone(770, 250)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("E is magic!", 15, 60)

hints.text: Show a short vowel word like cap on the screen.
Add + e = to show the magic e being added.
Show the new long vowel word: cape!
The vowel now says its own name (long sound).
Try 5 pairs: cap/cape, pin/pine, hop/hope, tub/tube, kit/kite.

questions.hint: Add an E to the end: c-a-p-e. Now the A says its name: "AY"! Cape!
questions.prompt: What happens when you add a silent E to "cap"? 🧢✨

questions.hint: H-O-P-E. The O says its own name now: "OH"! Hope!
questions.prompt: What happens when you add a silent E to "hop"? 🐰

questions.hint: Which word has a silent E at the end? The I says its own name: "EYE"!
questions.prompt: Which word has a LONG vowel sound? 🌟

questions.hint: T-U-B-E has a silent E! The U says "YOO" like in the word "you"!
questions.prompt: Which word has a LONG vowel sound? 💧

questions.hint: K-I-T-E. The I says its name now: "EYE"! A kite flies in the sky!
questions.prompt: Add silent E to "kit." What word do you get? 🎯

questions.hint: Which pair changes the vowel from short to long by adding a silent E?
questions.prompt: Which pair shows short vowel → long vowel with silent E? 🔄

title: Compound Word Workshop
description: Match two smaller words together to build compound words! Build a word combiner on your M5Stick.
story_text: Welcome to Chip's Word Workshop! Did you know that some BIG words are actually two small words stuck together? Like "sun" + "flower" = "sunflower"! These are called compound words. Match the word halves to build them!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">sun + flower</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">45</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Compound!", 25, 5)

compounds = [("sun","flower"), ("rain","bow"), ("cup","cake"), ("star","fish"), ("tooth","brush")]
for w1, w2 in compounds:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x3B82F6, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(w1, 10, 40)
    Speaker.tone(500, 150)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("+", 55, 40)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xF97316, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(w2, 70, 40)
    Speaker.tone(500, 150)
    time.sleep(1)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("= " + w1 + w2 + "!", 10, 70)
    Speaker.tone(770, 300)
    time.sleep(1.5)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Word builder!", 10, 60)

hints.text: Show the first word in blue on the left.
Show a + sign, then the second word in orange.
Wait a moment, then combine them into one word!
Display the compound word in green below.
sun + flower = sunflower! Two words become one.

activities.hint: Think about it: which two words combine to make a real word? Sun + flower = sunflower!
activities.prompt: Match the two words that go together to make a compound word! 🧩

title: Prefix Power!
description: Learn how un- and re- change the meaning of words! Build a prefix display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip discovered something amazing! You can add tiny word parts to the FRONT of a word to change its meaning! Adding "un-" means "not" -- so "unhappy" means "not happy." Adding "re-" means "again" -- so "redo" means "do again." Try it out!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">un + happy</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Prefixes!", 30, 5)

prefixes = [
    ("un","happy","not happy"),
    ("un","do","reverse it"),
    ("re","read","read again"),
    ("re","do","do again"),
    ("un","fair","not fair"),
    ("re","play","play again")
]

for prefix, word, meaning in prefixes:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xEF4444, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(prefix, 10, 35)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("+ " + word, 40, 35)
    time.sleep(1)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("= " + prefix + word, 10, 60)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xEAB308, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("(" + meaning + ")", 10, 82)
    Speaker.tone(660, 200)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Prefix power!", 5, 60)

hints.text: Show the prefix (un- or re-) in red.
Show the base word next to it.
Combine them into the new word below.
Show what the new word means in yellow.
un- means not or opposite. re- means again!

questions.hint: Which prefix means "not"? Starts with U!
questions.prompt: If happy means glad, ___happy means NOT glad. 😔

questions.hint: Which prefix means "again"? Starts with R!
questions.prompt: If "do" means to finish something, ___do means to do it AGAIN! 🔄

questions.hint: The shoelaces are NOT tied. Which prefix means "not"?
questions.prompt: My shoelaces came ___tied! They are NOT tied anymore. 👟

questions.hint: You want to read it AGAIN. Which prefix means "again"?
questions.prompt: I need to ___read this book because it was so good! 📖

questions.hint: The door is NOT locked. Un- means not!
questions.prompt: The door is ___locked. It is NOT locked! 🔓

questions.hint: You want to write it AGAIN, but better. Re- means again!
questions.prompt: Oops! Let me ___write my name more neatly! ✍️

title: Story Parts
description: Learn about characters, setting, problem, and solution in stories! Build a story elements display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Every great story has special parts! There are CHARACTERS (who the story is about), a SETTING (where and when it happens), a PROBLEM (something goes wrong), and a SOLUTION (how it gets fixed). Let's read a little story and find all the parts!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Who: A dog</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">35</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Story Parts!", 15, 5)

parts = [
    ("Who:", "A brave dog", 0x3B82F6),
    ("Where:", "At the park", 0x22C55E),
    ("Problem:", "Lost a ball!", 0xEF4444),
    ("Solution:", "Found it!", 0xF97316)
]

for label, text, color in parts:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(color, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(label, 10, 40)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(text, 10, 65)
    Speaker.tone(550, 200)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Story smart!", 15, 55)
Lcd.drawString("Who Where What!", 5, 75)

hints.text: Every story has 4 parts: Who, Where, Problem, Solution.
Show Who: with the main character in blue.
Show Where: with the setting in green.
Show Problem: in red -- what goes wrong?
Show Solution: in orange -- how is it fixed?

questions.hint: A character is WHO the story is about. Who was lost?
questions.prompt: 📖 Story: "Luna the cat was lost in the park. She climbed a tall tree to look around. She spotted her house and ran home!" Who is the CHARACTER in this story?

questions.hint: The setting is WHERE the story happens. Where was Luna?
questions.prompt: 📖 Same story about Luna. What is the SETTING?

questions.hint: A problem is what goes WRONG. What happened to Luna at the start?
questions.prompt: 📖 Same story. What is the PROBLEM?

questions.hint: The solution is how the problem gets FIXED. How did Luna get home?
questions.prompt: 📖 Same story. What is the SOLUTION?

options.text: She climbed a tree and found her way home

questions.hint: Who is the story mostly about? Who lost something?
questions.prompt: 📖 New story: "Ben lost his toy at school. He asked his teacher for help. She found it under a desk!" Who is the main CHARACTER?

questions.hint: Where did Ben lose his toy?
questions.prompt: 📖 Same story about Ben. What is the SETTING?

title: What's the Big Idea?
description: Find the main idea and important details in short paragraphs! Build a main idea display on your M5Stick.
story_text: Chip loves reading, and every paragraph has ONE big idea -- that's called the MAIN IDEA! The other sentences give details that support it. Think of it like a sandwich: the main idea is the bread, and the details are the yummy stuff inside. Let's practice finding the big idea!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Dogs are pets.</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">35</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Big Idea!", 30, 5)

topics = [
    ("Dogs are pets.", "They play.", "Main: Dogs"),
    ("Fish swim.", "In water.", "Main: Fish"),
    ("Birds can fly.", "They sing.", "Main: Birds")
]

for line1, line2, main in topics:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(line1, 5, 35)
    time.sleep(1)
    Lcd.drawString(line2, 5, 55)
    time.sleep(1.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xF97316, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(main, 10, 80)
    Speaker.tone(660, 200)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Big thinker!", 15, 60)

hints.text: Show a short passage of 2-3 sentences.
Wait for the reader to read all the sentences.
Ask: what is the main idea? What is it mostly about?
Reveal the main idea in orange below the passage.
The main idea is what the whole paragraph is about!

questions.hint: The main idea is the BIG message. All the other sentences tell us WHY dogs make great pets.
questions.prompt: 📖 "Dogs make great pets. They are loyal and friendly. Dogs love to play fetch. They can learn many tricks." What is the MAIN IDEA?

questions.hint: Which sentence tells you the BIGGEST idea about apples? The other sentences give more details.
questions.prompt: 📖 "Apples are a healthy snack. They come in red, green, and yellow. Apples have vitamins that help your body. They taste sweet and crunchy!" What is the MAIN IDEA?

questions.hint: A detail is a smaller fact that supports the big idea. Which one is just one thing that happens in winter?
questions.prompt: 📖 "Winter is a cold season. Snow covers the ground. People wear warm coats and hats. Kids build snowmen and have snowball fights." Which is a DETAIL, not the main idea?

questions.hint: All the sentences talk about different ocean animals. What is the BIG idea that connects them all?
questions.prompt: 📖 "The ocean is full of amazing animals. Dolphins jump through waves. Octopuses have eight arms. Sea turtles swim thousands of miles." What is the MAIN IDEA?

options.text: The ocean is full of amazing animals

questions.hint: The first sentence usually gives the main idea. The rest explain WHY.
questions.prompt: 📖 "Brushing your teeth is important. It removes food stuck in your teeth. Brushing keeps your gums healthy. It helps prevent cavities." What is the MAIN IDEA?

title: Punctuation Station
description: Choose the right ending mark: period, question mark, or exclamation point! Build a punctuation quiz on your M5Stick.
story_text: All aboard the Punctuation Station! Every sentence needs a mark at the end. A PERIOD (.) ends a regular sentence. A QUESTION MARK (?) ends a question. An EXCLAMATION POINT (!) shows excitement or strong feelings. Let's pick the right one!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">I am happy_</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Punctuation!", 10, 5)

sentences = [
    ("I am happy", ".", "telling"),
    ("Are you sad", "?", "asking"),
    ("Wow, a dog", "!", "excited"),
    ("She can run", ".", "telling"),
    ("Is it rain", "?", "asking"),
    ("Oh no", "!", "surprised")
]

for text, mark, mood in sentences:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(text + " _", 5, 40)
    time.sleep(1.5)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xF97316, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(text + mark, 5, 65)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xEAB308, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("(" + mood + ")", 25, 90)
    Speaker.tone(660, 200)
    time.sleep(2)

Lcd.fillRect(0, 25, 135, 95, 0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x22C55E, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Punctuation pro!", 1, 60)

hints.text: Show a sentence with a blank at the end: I am happy _
Wait for the reader to think about which mark fits.
Period (.) for telling, question mark (?) for asking, exclamation (!) for excitement.
Reveal the correct punctuation mark in orange.
Show the mood: telling, asking, or excited/surprised.

questions.hint: This is a regular statement -- it tells us something. Statements end with a period.
questions.prompt: "The cat sat on the mat___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 🐱

questions.hint: This sentence is asking something. Questions end with a question mark!
questions.prompt: "Where is my backpack___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 🎒

questions.hint: This is a warning with strong feeling! Exciting or urgent sentences end with !
questions.prompt: "Watch out for that puddle___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 💦

questions.hint: Someone is asking you a question about ice cream. Questions get a ?
questions.prompt: "Do you like ice cream___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 🍦

questions.hint: This tells us something in a calm way. It is a statement, so it ends with a period.
questions.prompt: "I love playing with my friends___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 👫

questions.hint: Winning is exciting! When you feel excited or surprised, use an exclamation point!
questions.prompt: "We won the game___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 🏆

title: Main Idea Detective
description: Read short passages and find the MAIN idea hiding inside!
story_text: Put on your detective hat! Chip has a magnifying glass and we're hunting for MAIN IDEAS! Every nonfiction paragraph has ONE big idea that all the other sentences support. The main idea is like the title of a chapter -- it tells you what the whole thing is about. Let's investigate!

questions.hint: The main idea is the BIG message. All the other sentences tell us cool things about dolphins. Which sentence covers them ALL?
questions.prompt: 🔍 "Dolphins are amazing swimmers. They can jump high out of the water. Dolphins use clicks and whistles to talk to each other. They live together in groups called pods." What is the MAIN idea?

options.text: Dolphins jump out of the water

questions.hint: Which sentence is the BIGGEST idea? The other sentences explain WHY bees are important.
questions.prompt: 🔍 "Bees are very important insects. They help flowers grow by carrying pollen. Bees also make honey. Without bees, many plants would not have fruit." What is the MAIN idea?

questions.hint: The first sentence introduces the BIG topic. Everything else gives details about what penguins do instead of flying.
questions.prompt: 🔍 "Penguins are birds that cannot fly. They are great swimmers instead. Penguins use their flippers to glide through cold ocean water. They catch fish to eat." What is the MAIN idea?

options.text: Penguins are birds that cannot fly

questions.hint: A detail is a smaller fact that supports the big idea. Which one is just ONE thing about elephants?
questions.prompt: 🔍 "Elephants are the largest land animals. They can weigh as much as a school bus! Elephants use their long trunks to drink water and grab food. Baby elephants stay close to their mothers." Which sentence is a DETAIL, not the main idea?

options.text: Elephants are the largest land animals
Baby elephants stay close to their mothers
All of these are the main idea

questions.hint: The main idea tells us the BIG topic. It tells us what the passage is... a-b-o-u-t!
questions.prompt: 🔍 The main idea tells us what the whole passage is ___. 📝

questions.hint: All the details tell us about how owls catch food. They are great... h-u-n-t-e-r-s!
questions.prompt: 🔍 "Owls are great hunters. They can see in the dark. Owls fly very quietly. They have sharp claws for catching mice." The main idea is: Owls are great ___. 🦉

title: Supporting Details
description: Match details to the main idea they support -- like branches on a tree!
story_text: Chip is building a DETAIL TREE! The main idea is the trunk -- big and strong. The details are the branches -- they grow OUT of the main idea and tell us more. A good reader knows which details go with which main idea. Let's match them up!

activities.hint: A supporting detail tells us MORE about the main idea. Ask yourself: does this fact help explain the big idea?

left.text: They have soft fur to keep warm
They grow from seeds in the ground
They come in many colors like red and yellow
They can learn to fetch and sit

activities.prompt: Match each DETAIL to the MAIN IDEA it supports! 🌳

questions.hint: Which detail tells us MORE about frogs being amazing jumpers? Look for the one about jumping!
questions.prompt: 🌳 Main idea: "Frogs are amazing jumpers." Which detail SUPPORTS this main idea?

options.text: Frogs can leap 20 times their body length

questions.hint: Two of these tell us about cold weather. Which one is about something totally different?
questions.prompt: 🌳 Main idea: "Winter is a cold season." Which detail does NOT support this idea?

options.text: Ice cream comes in many flavors

questions.hint: We need a detail about LIFE in the ocean. Which one talks about living creatures?
questions.prompt: 🌳 Main idea: "The ocean is full of life." Which is a supporting detail?

options.text: The ocean is made of salt water
Colorful fish swim near coral reefs

questions.hint: Which detail tells us HOW exercise helps your body?
questions.prompt: 🌳 Main idea: "Exercise keeps your body healthy." Which detail supports this?

title: Text Features Treasure Hunt
description: Discover how headings, bold words, captions, and glossaries help you read nonfiction!
story_text: Chip is exploring a nonfiction book like a treasure map! Headings are like street signs -- they tell you what's coming next. Bold words are clues -- they show important vocabulary. Captions explain pictures. And a glossary at the back is like a mini dictionary! Let's go on a treasure hunt!

activities.hint: Think about where you find each feature in a book. Headings are at the top of sections. The glossary is at the back!

right.text: Tells you what a section is about
A mini dictionary at the back of a book

activities.prompt: Match each text feature to what it DOES! 🗺️

questions.hint: Authors make words BOLD when they are important vocabulary words you should pay attention to!
questions.prompt: 🗺️ You see the word "hibernate" in BOLD in a book about bears. Why is it bold?

options.text: It is an important word to learn
It is the title of the book

questions.hint: The words below a photo explain what the picture shows. That's called a...
questions.prompt: 🗺️ You see a photo of a rainforest with words underneath it. Those words are called a ___.

questions.hint: It's like a mini dictionary at the back of the book. It starts with G!
questions.prompt: 🗺️ Where would you look to find the meaning of a tricky word in a nonfiction book?

questions.hint: Headings are like signs on a road. They help you find where you want to go in the book!
questions.prompt: 🗺️ A book about animals has these headings: "Mammals," "Reptiles," "Birds." What do the headings help you do?

options.text: Find the section you want to read

title: Author's Purpose
description: Figure out WHY the author wrote it: to inform, entertain, or persuade!
story_text: Chip is wondering: WHY did the author write this? Every author has a PURPOSE! Some authors want to INFORM you -- teach you something new. Some want to ENTERTAIN you -- make you laugh or enjoy a story. And some want to PERSUADE you -- convince you to think or do something. Let's figure out which is which!

questions.hint: Is this passage trying to teach you something? It gives facts about the sun -- that sounds like informing!
questions.prompt: 🤔 "The sun is a star. It gives Earth light and heat. Without the sun, nothing could live on Earth." Why did the author write this?

questions.hint: A silly frog singing? Fish laughing? This sounds like a funny story meant to make you smile!
questions.prompt: 🤔 "Once upon a time, a silly frog tried to sing like a bird. He croaked so loudly that all the fish laughed!" Why did the author write this?

questions.hint: The author says "everyone SHOULD" and "PLEASE start." They're trying to convince you to do something!
questions.prompt: 🤔 "Everyone should recycle! Recycling helps keep our planet clean. You can recycle paper, plastic, and glass. Please start recycling today!" Why did the author write this?

questions.hint: This passage shares facts about how ants live and work. The author wants to teach you!
questions.prompt: 🤔 "Ants live in colonies underground. Each ant has a job. Some ants find food, some protect the nest, and the queen lays eggs." Why did the author write this?

options.text: To entertain (tell a funny ant story)

questions.hint: "You SHOULD" and "pick up a book tonight!" -- the author is trying to convince you to do something!
questions.prompt: 🤔 "You should read every day! Reading makes you smarter and is so much fun. Pick up a book tonight!" Why did the author write this?

activities.hint: Facts and science = inform. Funny stories = entertain. "You should" = persuade!

left.text: A book about how volcanoes work
A poster saying "Vote for class president!"
A fairy tale about a magic dragon

activities.prompt: Match each passage to the author's purpose! 🎯

title: Connecting Ideas in Text
description: Put ideas from a passage in order and discover how they connect like puzzle pieces!
story_text: Chip loves puzzles, and today we're putting IDEAS together! In nonfiction, ideas connect to each other like puzzle pieces. One idea leads to the next. Sometimes things happen in order, sometimes one thing CAUSES another, and sometimes ideas are compared. Let's connect the dots!

questions.hint: Think about what has to happen FIRST. The bird needs a spot, then materials, then it builds, then makes it comfy, then lays eggs!

items.text: The bird finds a safe spot in a tree
It gathers twigs, grass, and leaves
It weaves the materials into a bowl shape
It lines the nest with soft feathers
The bird lays eggs in the cozy nest

questions.prompt: 🧩 "How a Bird Builds a Nest" -- Put these steps in order!

questions.hint: Start with planting! Then the wheat grows, gets harvested, becomes flour, and finally becomes bread!

items.text: The wheat grows tall in the field
The wheat is ground into flour
A baker uses the flour to make bread

questions.prompt: 🧩 "From Seed to Sandwich" -- How does wheat become bread? Put it in order!

questions.hint: One thing happens, THEN the next, THEN the next. These ideas follow a time order!
questions.prompt: 🧩 "Caterpillars eat leaves to grow big. Then they form a chrysalis. Inside, they change into butterflies." How are these ideas connected?

options.text: They happen in order (first, then, finally)

questions.hint: Rain causes rivers to overflow, which causes flooding. One event leads to another!
questions.prompt: 🧩 "When it rains a lot, rivers can overflow. This causes flooding in nearby towns." How are these ideas connected?

questions.hint: The passage talks about frogs AND fish and how they are different. That's a comparison!
questions.prompt: 🧩 "Frogs live in water and on land. Fish only live in water." How are these ideas connected?

questions.hint: The text says eating a lot helps them SURVIVE winter. One idea explains the other!
questions.prompt: 🧩 "Bears eat a lot of food in the fall. This helps them survive winter when food is hard to find." Why do bears eat so much in fall?

options.text: Because they need energy to survive winter

title: Nouns Everywhere (Collective Nouns)
description: Discover the special names for groups of animals and things!
story_text: Hey there! Chip just learned something AMAZING! Did you know that a group of birds is called a FLOCK? And a group of wolves is called a PACK? These special group names are called collective nouns, and they're super fun. Let's match each group name to the right animal!

activities.hint: A flock flies in the sky, a team plays together, a herd grazes in a field, a pack hunts together, and a school swims in the ocean!
activities.prompt: Match each collective noun to the group it describes! 🦁

questions.hint: This word is also a place where kids learn! A ___ of fish.
questions.prompt: A ___ of fish swam through the coral reef! 🐠

questions.hint: Starts with FL. Rhymes with clock! A fl-___ of birds.
questions.prompt: A ___ of birds flew across the sunset sky! 🌅

questions.hint: When you put things in a suitcase, you ___ them. Wolves travel in a ___!
questions.prompt: The ___ of wolves howled at the moon! 🌙

questions.hint: This word sounds like "heard" (what your ears do). A h-___ of cows.
questions.prompt: A ___ of cows grazed in the green field! 🌾

title: Action Verbs & Past Tense
description: Match present tense verbs to their tricky past tense forms!
story_text: Chip is talking about what happened YESTERDAY versus what happens TODAY! TODAY I run, but YESTERDAY I ran. TODAY I eat, but YESTERDAY I ate. Some past tense words are tricky -- they don't just add "-ed." Let's match them up and practice using them!

activities.hint: These are irregular verbs -- they don't follow the normal rules! Run becomes ran, sit becomes sat, eat becomes ate, go becomes went, and see becomes saw.
activities.prompt: Match each TODAY verb to its YESTERDAY (past tense) form! 🔄

questions.hint: Run changes to r-a-n. Ran!
questions.prompt: Today I run, but yesterday I ___. 🏃

questions.hint: Eat changes to a-t-e. Ate! It sounds like the number 8.
questions.prompt: Today I eat lunch, but yesterday I ___ lunch. 🍔

questions.hint: Go is a funny one! It changes completely to w-e-n-t. Went!
questions.prompt: Today I go to school, but yesterday I ___ to school. 🏫

questions.hint: See changes to s-a-w. Saw! Like the tool but a different meaning.
questions.prompt: Today I see a rainbow, but yesterday I ___ a rainbow. 🌈

questions.hint: Sit changes the I to an A. S-a-t. Sat!
questions.prompt: Today I sit on the bench, but yesterday I ___ on the bench. 🪑

title: Adjectives & Adverbs
description: Make boring sentences AMAZING by adding describing words!
story_text: Chip thinks sentences can be SO much better with describing words! Adjectives describe NOUNS (things) -- like "the FLUFFY dog" or "the BIG house." Adverbs describe VERBS (actions) -- like "she ran QUICKLY" or "he sang LOUDLY." Let's make boring sentences amazing!

questions.hint: An adjective describes what the dog looks like. Try fluffy, big, or tiny -- any describing word works!
questions.prompt: The ___ dog ran across the yard! (Pick an adjective: big, tiny, or fluffy) 🐶

questions.hint: What does the apple look, taste, or feel like? Red, juicy, and crunchy all describe the apple!
questions.prompt: I ate a ___ apple for snack! (Pick an adjective: red, juicy, or crunchy) 🍎

questions.hint: What kind of cat? A describing word that tells us about the cat goes here!
questions.prompt: The ___ cat slept on the warm pillow. (Pick an adjective: sleepy, gray, or little) 🐱

questions.hint: Adverbs often end in -LY and tell you HOW something is done. Which word ends in -ly?
questions.prompt: Which word is an ADVERB (describes how something is done)? 🤔

questions.hint: Turtles are not fast! Which -ly word describes HOW the turtle walked?
questions.prompt: "The turtle walked ___." Which adverb fits best? 🐢

questions.hint: An adjective describes a THING (noun). Which word could describe a building or a person? The ___ building.
questions.prompt: Which word is an ADJECTIVE (describes a noun)? 🌟

questions.hint: Lions roar with a big sound! Which -ly word tells HOW the lion roared?
questions.prompt: "The lion roared ___." Which adverb fits best? 🦁

title: Punctuation Party
description: Period, Question Mark, and Exclamation Point are throwing a party -- pick the right one for each sentence!
story_text: Welcome to the Punctuation Party! Chip invited three special friends: PERIOD (.) who ends calm, regular sentences. QUESTION MARK (?) who LOVES asking questions. And EXCLAMATION POINT (!) who gets super excited about everything! Let's figure out which friend belongs at the end of each sentence!

questions.hint: This sentence is ASKING something. When you ask, you need a question mark!
questions.prompt: "What time is lunch___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 🍕

questions.hint: This is a calm statement -- just telling you a fact. Statements end with a period.
questions.prompt: "I have a pet goldfish___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 🐟

questions.hint: This is a warning! The person sounds urgent and excited. That calls for an exclamation point!
questions.prompt: "Look out, the ball is coming___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? ⚽

questions.hint: Someone is asking for permission. Questions always end with a question mark!
questions.prompt: "Can I have a cookie___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 🍪

questions.hint: This is just telling you a plan in a calm way. Regular statements end with a period.
questions.prompt: "We are going to the park today___" Which punctuation mark goes at the end? 🏞️

questions.hint: This is a calm statement telling you a name. Statements end with a period.
questions.prompt: My name is Chip___ (Add the correct punctuation: . or ? or !)

questions.hint: Someone is asking a question! What mark goes at the end of a question?
questions.prompt: Where did my pencil go___ (Add the correct punctuation: . or ? or !)

questions.hint: "Wow" shows excitement! Excited sentences end with an exclamation point!
questions.prompt: Wow, that rainbow is beautiful___ (Add the correct punctuation: . or ? or !)

questions.hint: This is a calm fact about where the cat is. Regular statements end with a period.
questions.prompt: The cat is sleeping on the couch___ (Add the correct punctuation: . or ? or !)

title: Sentence Expander
description: Put words in order and grow tiny sentences into amazing ones!
story_text: Chip loves playing the sentence growing game! Start with a tiny sentence like "The cat sat." Now let's make it GROW! Add an adjective: "The fluffy cat sat." Add an adverb: "The fluffy cat sat quietly." Add more details: "The fluffy orange cat sat quietly on the soft mat." See how much better that is? Let's play!

questions.hint: Start with "The" (capital letter = beginning!), then the noun (dog), then the verb (ran), then how (fast).
questions.prompt: Put these words in order to make a sentence! 📝

questions.hint: Start with "A" (capital!), then the adjective (little), noun (bird), verb (sang), adverb (sweetly).
questions.prompt: Put these words in order to make a sentence! 🌟

questions.hint: Questions often start with "Can" or "Do" or "What." Look for the word with a capital letter and the word with a question mark!
questions.prompt: Put these words in order to make a question! ❓

questions.hint: An adjective describes the cat! What does the cat look like? Try fluffy, big, tiny, orange, or any other describing word!
questions.prompt: Make the sentence bigger! "The cat sat." → "The ___ cat sat." (Add an adjective!) 🐱

questions.hint: An adverb describes HOW the dog ran. Did it run quickly? Slowly? Happily? Try a word that ends in -ly!
questions.prompt: Make the sentence bigger! "The dog ran." → "The dog ran ___." (Add an adverb!) 🐶

questions.hint: Describe the bird! What does it look like? Try little, tiny, blue, pretty, or any describing word!
questions.prompt: Make the sentence bigger! "The bird sang." → "The ___ bird sang." (Add an adjective!) 🐦

questions.hint: An adverb tells HOW the fish swam. Did it swim slowly? Quickly? Happily? Try a word that ends in -ly!
questions.prompt: Make the sentence bigger! "The fish swam." → "The fish swam ___." (Add an adverb!) 🐟

title: Sound Safari
description: Go on a sound safari! Use your M5Stick to play different animal sounds -- a bird chirps HIGH, a bear growls LOW. Explore sound in nature!
story_text: Chip is pressing his hand against his buzzer. "WHOA! I can feel it SHAKING when it makes noise!" he says. "That shaking is called a VIBRATION. ALL sounds come from vibrations! When a drum goes BOOM, the drum skin vibrates. When you talk, your throat vibrates! Let us go on a Sound Safari and discover all the different vibrations that make sounds. Clap your hands near me and watch what happens!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Sound Safari!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">800</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sound Safari!", 10, 5)
animals = [("Bird:", 800, 200), ("Frog:", 400, 300), ("Bear:", 150, 600), ("Cricket:", 900, 100)]
y = 30
for name, freq, dur in animals:
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name + " " + str(freq) + "Hz", 5, y)
    Speaker.tone(freq, dur)
    time.sleep(dur / 1000 + 0.3)
    y = y + 25
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("High=small animal", 2, 135)
Lcd.drawString("Low=big animal!", 5, 155)

hints.text: In nature, small animals often make HIGH sounds and big animals make LOW sounds! Lets explore with a sound safari!
A bird chirps HIGH (800 Hz) and short. A frog croaks MEDIUM (400 Hz).
A bear growls LOW (150 Hz) and long. A cricket chirps very HIGH (900 Hz) and quick!
Display each animal name and its frequency. Higher number = higher pitch!
Notice the pattern: smaller animals have higher voices! Thats because smaller vocal cords vibrate faster!

story.title: Vibration Discovery
story.narrative: Chip feels his buzzer shaking and discovers that ALL sound comes from vibrations!

create.title: Sound Hunt
create.prompt: Find 5 things that make sound. What part of each thing vibrates? Tell Chip about your discoveries!

explore.title: Sound Experiments
explore.instructions: The buzzer plays tones while the display shows sound waves. Clap near the mic and see YOUR sound on screen!
explore.device_interaction: Buzzer demonstrates vibration. Mic detects claps. Display shows wave visualization.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click to play sounds and see animated wave patterns.

celebrate.title: Sound Scientist!
celebrate.summary: You discovered that ALL sounds come from vibrations. You are a real sound scientist!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child explored the concept that sound is produced by vibrating objects (NGSS 1-PS4-1).

title: Loud and Quiet
description: Measure how loud things are! Scientists measure sound in decibels. Compare whispers, normal talking, and shouting on your M5Stick meter!
story_text: Chip is experimenting with his buzzer. "When I play a LOW number, the sound is low and rumbly. When I play a HIGH number, it is squeaky and high!" he says. "And when I make it LOUDER, the vibrations are BIGGER. Quiet sounds have tiny vibrations. I wonder what happens if I change the pitch and volume at the same time? Let us find out!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Sound Meter!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">100</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sound Meter!", 15, 5)
levels = [("Whisper 20dB", 80, 0x00FF00), ("Talking 60dB", 250, 0xFFFF00), ("Music  80dB", 400, 0xFF9800), ("Siren 100dB", 600, 0xFF0000)]
y = 30
for name, dur, color in levels:
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 5, y)
    Speaker.tone(440, dur)
    Lcd.fillRect(5, y + 15, dur // 5, 8, color)
    time.sleep(dur / 1000 + 0.3)
    y = y + 30
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Protect your ears!", 2, 155)

hints.text: Scientists measure loudness in DECIBELS (dB). A whisper is about 20 dB, and a siren is about 100 dB!
Show a WHISPER (20 dB): very short sound, tiny green bar. Super quiet!
TALKING (60 dB): medium sound and yellow bar. MUSIC (80 dB): louder, orange bar.
SIREN (100 dB): long loud sound and big red bar. Too loud can hurt your ears!
Show a message about protecting your ears. Very loud sounds (over 85 dB) can damage hearing over time!

story.title: The Sound Lab
story.narrative: Chip experiments with his buzzer to discover what makes sounds high or low, loud or quiet.

create.title: My Sound Effects
create.prompt: Create 3 different sound effects: a tiny mouse squeak, a big lion roar, and a spaceship engine. Use pitch and volume!

explore.title: Pitch & Volume Controls
explore.instructions: Press buttons to change the buzzer frequency (pitch) and volume. The display shows the wave getting taller (louder) or faster (higher).
explore.device_interaction: Buzzer plays variable tones. Buttons adjust pitch and volume. Display shows wave visualization.
explore.simulator_fallback: Use sliders to control pitch and volume. See and hear the results.

celebrate.title: Sound Master!
celebrate.summary: You understand pitch and volume! You can make sounds high, low, loud, and quiet.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child explored how pitch relates to frequency and volume relates to amplitude (1-PS4-1).

title: Light and Shadow
description: Shadows happen when light is blocked! Shine a light on an object and it makes a dark shadow. Show how light, objects, and shadows work!
story_text: Chip turns off his screen. "I cannot see ANYTHING! It is pitch black!" he says. "We need LIGHT to see things. And when light hits something, it makes a SHADOW on the other side! The shadow is where the light cannot reach. Watch -- I will turn on my LED and you can see how shadows appear on the display. Move the object and the shadow moves too!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">12</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Light & Shadow!", 5, 5)
Lcd.fillCircle(20, 60, 15, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Light", 5, 80)
Lcd.fillRect(60, 45, 15, 30, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.drawString("Object", 50, 80)
Lcd.fillRect(90, 45, 30, 35, 0x333333)
Lcd.drawString("Shadow!", 88, 85)
Lcd.fillRect(20, 55, 40, 2, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Light blocked =", 5, 110)
Lcd.drawString("shadow behind!", 5, 128)

hints.text: A SHADOW forms when an object BLOCKS light. The light cant go through, so it makes a dark area behind the object!
Draw a yellow circle for the LIGHT SOURCE (like the Sun or a flashlight).
Draw a red rectangle for the OBJECT that blocks the light.
Draw a dark gray rectangle behind the object -- thats the SHADOW! Light cant get past!
Draw yellow lines from the light to the object. The shadow is always on the OPPOSITE side from the light!

story.title: Light and Dark
story.narrative: Chip discovers that without light, we cannot see. Learn how light and shadows work!

create.title: Shadow Puppet Show
create.prompt: Make a shadow puppet show! What shapes can you make with shadows? Tell a short story with your shadow characters.

explore.title: Shadow Experiments
explore.instructions: Toggle the LED on and off. Watch how objects on the display cast shadows when light shines on them. Move objects to change shadows!
explore.device_interaction: LED demonstrates light on/off. Display simulates shadows. Buttons move objects.
explore.simulator_fallback: Toggle virtual light and drag objects to see shadow changes.

celebrate.title: Light Explorer!
celebrate.summary: You understand that light helps us see and objects make shadows by blocking light!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child explored that objects need light to be seen and opaque objects block light to create shadows (1-PS4-3).

title: Plant Parts
description: Every plant part has a job! Roots absorb water, stems transport it, leaves make food, flowers make seeds. Build and label a plant on screen!
story_text: Chip is looking at a flower on his display. "Plants are amazing!" he says. "They have PARTS just like we do! The ROOTS drink water from the ground -- like a straw! The STEM holds the plant up tall -- like a backbone! The LEAVES catch sunlight and make food -- like a solar panel! And the FLOWER makes seeds for new plants! Let us explore each part and learn its job."
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_rect"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">63</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">8</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">70</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Plant Parts!", 15, 5)
Lcd.fillCircle(67, 40, 15, 0xFF00FF)
Lcd.fillCircle(67, 40, 6, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Flower", 88, 35)
Lcd.fillRect(63, 50, 8, 70, 0x00FF00)
Lcd.drawString("Stem", 75, 80)
Lcd.fillCircle(50, 70, 10, 0x00FF00)
Lcd.fillCircle(84, 70, 10, 0x00FF00)
Lcd.drawString("Leaf", 95, 65)
Lcd.fillRect(57, 120, 4, 15, 0x8B4513)
Lcd.fillRect(73, 120, 4, 15, 0x8B4513)
Lcd.drawString("Roots", 80, 125)
Lcd.fillRect(0, 118, 135, 3, 0x8B4513)
Lcd.drawString("soil", 5, 125)

hints.text: A plant is like a factory! Each part has a specific job. Lets draw and label all the parts!
Draw the STEM: a tall green rectangle. The stem is like a highway for water and nutrients!
Add LEAVES on the sides. Leaves capture sunlight and make food through photosynthesis!
Add a FLOWER on top with a center. Flowers attract bees and make seeds for new plants!
Add ROOTS below ground. Roots anchor the plant and soak up water from the soil. Label every part!

story.title: Plant Anatomy
story.narrative: Chip discovers that plants have parts with special jobs, just like our body parts!

create.title: Draw a Plant
create.prompt: Draw a plant and label all 4 parts. Can you explain what each part does to Chip?

explore.title: Explore the Plant
explore.instructions: Display shows a plant diagram. Press buttons to highlight each part and learn its function. Take a quiz to label them!
explore.device_interaction: Display shows interactive plant diagram. Buttons cycle through parts with descriptions.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click on plant parts to learn about each one.

celebrate.title: Plant Expert!
celebrate.summary: You know all the parts of a plant and what they do! Roots, stem, leaves, and flower -- they all work together.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child learned plant structure and function, identifying roots, stems, leaves, and flowers (1-LS1-1).

title: Animal Homes
description: Animals live in different homes called habitats! A bird has a nest, a bear has a cave, a fish has the ocean. Show animal homes on your M5Stick!
story_text: Chip is watching animals on his display. "Birds live in nests, fish live in water, bears live in caves," he observes. "But WHY? Because every animal needs three things: FOOD to eat, WATER to drink, and SHELTER to stay safe. A fish lives in water because that is where its food is AND it is its shelter! Let us explore different habitats and figure out why animals live where they do!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Animal Homes!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Animal Homes!", 10, 5)
homes = [("Bird -> Nest", 0x8B4513), ("Fish -> Water", 0x0000FF), ("Bear -> Cave", 0x666666), ("Bee -> Hive", 0xFFFF00)]
y = 30
for text, color in homes:
    Lcd.fillCircle(15, y + 5, 8, color)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(text, 28, y)
    Speaker.tone(400, 100)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 25
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Habitats!", 30, 140)

hints.text: A HABITAT is where an animal lives! Each animal needs a home that gives it food, water, and shelter.
A BIRD lives in a NEST made of sticks. Show a brown circle for the nest.
A FISH lives in WATER (ocean, lake, river). Show a blue circle. A BEAR lives in a CAVE.
A BEE lives in a HIVE. Show a yellow circle. Each home is perfect for its animal!
Animals pick homes that match their needs. A fish cant live in a nest! Matching animals to habitats is important!

story.title: Habitat Hunt
story.narrative: Chip wonders why animals live where they do. Discover that every animal needs food, water, and shelter!

create.title: Dream Habitat
create.prompt: If you were an animal, where would you live? Draw your perfect habitat with food, water, and shelter!

explore.title: Explore Habitats
explore.instructions: Display shows different habitats. Tilt to explore areas. Press buttons to match animals to their homes!
explore.device_interaction: Display shows habitat scenes. IMU tilt scrolls to explore. Buttons select matching animals.
explore.simulator_fallback: Scroll through habitats and click to match animals.

celebrate.title: Habitat Hero!
celebrate.summary: You know what animals need to survive and why they live where they do!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child learned that animals need food, water, and shelter and explored how habitats provide these (1-LS1-1).

title: Animal Homes!
description: Match animals to their homes! Nest, burrow, cave, web. Each animal builds or finds the perfect home. Show the matches on screen!
story_text: Chip is making a nature documentary! Help match each animal to where it lives.
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Animal Homes!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Animal Homes!", 10, 5)
matches = [("Bird -> Nest", 0x8B4513), ("Spider -> Web", 0xCCCCCC), ("Rabbit -> Burrow", 0xFF9800), ("Bat -> Cave", 0x666666)]
y = 30
for text, color in matches:
    Lcd.fillCircle(15, y + 5, 7, color)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(text, 28, y)
    Speaker.tone(400, 80)
    time.sleep(0.7)
    y = y + 24
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Each animal has", 5, 130)
Lcd.drawString("the perfect home!", 5, 148)

hints.text: Every animal has a home that is perfect for it! The home protects them from weather and predators.
A BIRD lives in a NEST made of sticks and grass, high in trees. A SPIDER makes a WEB of silk!
A RABBIT digs a BURROW underground. A BAT sleeps in a CAVE during the day!
Show each match with a colored dot and text. Each home is made from different materials!
Animals are amazing builders! Some homes are super complex, like termite mounds taller than people!

activities.hint: Think about where you would find each animal in nature!
activities.prompt: Match each animal to its home!

questions.hint: What does a caterpillar eat? What eats a caterpillar?
questions.prompt: Put the food chain in order from smallest to biggest!

title: Plant Parts!
description: Learn what each plant part DOES! Roots drink, stems carry, leaves cook, flowers attract. Build a working plant diagram!
story_text: Chip is growing a garden! Did you know every part of a plant has a special job? Match each plant part to what it does.
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Plant Jobs!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field></block></next></block></xml>

for text, color in jobs:
    Lcd.fillCircle(15, y + 5, 7, color)

    Speaker.tone(350, 100)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 24

title: Like Parent, Like Baby
description: Baby animals look like their parents! Kittens look like cats, puppies look like dogs. Show how parent and baby animals are similar!
story_text: Chip shows pictures of a cat and a kitten. "The kitten looks a LOT like the big cat!" he says. "Same pointy ears, same whiskers, same tail. Babies look like their parents because parents PASS ON their features. But the kitten is not exactly the same -- maybe it has different colored fur or different size spots. Let us match baby animals to their parents and spot the similarities and differences!"

hints.text: Baby animals look like their parents but SMALLER! A kitten looks like a small cat.
Draw a big orange circle (parent cat) and a small orange circle (kitten). Same color, different size!
Draw a big brown circle (parent dog) and a small brown circle (puppy). Like parent, like baby!
Label each pair. Babies inherit traits (features) from their parents.
This is called HEREDITY -- parents pass traits to babies. Thats why you might look like your mom or dad!

story.title: Family Album

title: Weather Watch!
description: Track weather over several days! Show sunny, cloudy, and rainy icons in a row to see weather patterns throughout the week!
story_text: Chip is planning outdoor adventures but needs to check the weather! Help identify different weather and what to wear.
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Week Weather!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Week Weather!", 10, 5)
days = ["Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri"]
weather = [0xFFFF00, 0xFFFF00, 0xCCCCCC, 0x666666, 0xFFFF00]
x = 5
for i in range(5):
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(days[i], x, 28)
    Lcd.fillCircle(x + 10, 55, 8, weather[i])
    if weather[i] == 0x666666:
        Lcd.fillRect(x + 7, 65, 2, 6, 0x0000FF)
        Lcd.fillRect(x + 13, 65, 2, 6, 0x0000FF)
    x = x + 26
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("3 sunny, 1 cloudy", 2, 80)
Lcd.drawString("1 rainy this week!", 2, 98)
Lcd.drawString("Track weather", 5, 125)
Lcd.drawString("every day!", 15, 143)

hints.text: Scientists track weather EVERY DAY to spot patterns! Lets record a whole week of weather.
Show 5 days: Mon through Fri. Under each day, draw a weather icon (circle).
Yellow = sunny, gray = cloudy, dark gray with blue lines = rainy. Pick different weather for each day!
Count the results: How many sunny days? Cloudy? Rainy? Thats collecting DATA!
Tracking weather helps us predict patterns. Scientists use data from many days to forecast weather!

questions.hint: When water falls from clouds, it is rain!
questions.prompt: It is pouring outside. What kind of weather is this?

questions.hint: When it is sunny, protect your eyes!

questions.hint: When air moves fast, we call it wind!
questions.prompt: Strong wind is blowing leaves around. What kind of day is it?

title: Butterfly Life Cycle
description: Butterflies go through 4 stages: egg, caterpillar, chrysalis, butterfly! Show each stage on your M5Stick with a display and sound!
story_text: Chip found a beautiful butterfly in the garden! But did you know butterflies weren't always butterflies? They go through an AMAZING transformation called a life cycle. First they're a tiny egg, then a hungry caterpillar, then they wrap up in a chrysalis, and FINALLY -- a butterfly! Let's explore!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Life Cycle!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Butterfly Life!", 5, 5)
stages = [("1. Egg", 0xFFFFFF, 300), ("2. Caterpillar", 0x00FF00, 350), ("3. Chrysalis", 0xFF9800, 400), ("4. Butterfly!", 0xFF00FF, 523)]
y = 30
for name, color, freq in stages:
    Lcd.setTextColor(color, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 10, y)
    Speaker.tone(freq, 200)
    time.sleep(1)
    y = y + 25
Lcd.fillCircle(67, 150, 15, 0xFF00FF)
Lcd.fillCircle(55, 140, 10, 0xFF00FF)
Lcd.fillCircle(79, 140, 10, 0xFF00FF)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Metamorphosis!", 5, 175)

hints.text: Butterflies change completely as they grow! This amazing change is called METAMORPHOSIS.
Stage 1: Egg -- a tiny dot on a leaf. Display it with a small white circle.
Stage 2: Caterpillar -- it eats and eats! Stage 3: Chrysalis -- it wraps up to transform.
Stage 4: BUTTERFLY! It emerges with beautiful wings! Draw wings as colored circles.
Each stage gets a higher-pitched sound, building excitement to the final transformation!

questions.hint: It starts as an egg, then hatches into a caterpillar, then wraps in a chrysalis, and finally becomes a butterfly!
questions.prompt: Put the butterfly life cycle in the correct order! 🦋

questions.hint: Caterpillars are VERY hungry! They eat and eat to grow big and strong.
questions.prompt: What does a caterpillar do A LOT before it becomes a chrysalis? 🐛

questions.hint: The caterpillar wraps itself up in something special while it changes into a butterfly.
questions.prompt: What is a chrysalis? 🪦

options.text: A protective shell where the caterpillar transforms

questions.hint: Butterflies lay their eggs on plants so the caterpillars have food to eat when they hatch!
questions.prompt: Where does a butterfly lay its eggs? 🥚

title: Frog Life Cycle
description: Frogs transform too! From egg to tadpole to froglet to adult frog. Show the frog life cycle stages on your M5Stick!
story_text: Ribbit! Chip went to the pond and found something cool -- frog eggs! Did you know frogs start as tiny jelly-like eggs in the water? Then they become tadpoles with tails, grow legs, lose their tail, and finally become grown-up frogs! Let's put it in order!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Frog Cycle!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Frog Life Cycle!", 2, 5)
stages = [("1. Eggs", 0xFFFFFF), ("2. Tadpole", 0x8B4513), ("3. Froglet", 0x00FF00), ("4. Adult Frog!", 0x00AA00)]
y = 30
for name, color in stages:
    Lcd.setTextColor(color, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 10, y)
    Lcd.fillCircle(110, y + 5, 8, color)
    Speaker.tone(300 + stages.index((name, color)) * 60, 200)
    time.sleep(1)
    y = y + 28
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Metamorphosis!", 5, 150)

hints.text: Frogs go through METAMORPHOSIS just like butterflies! They change shape completely as they grow.
Stage 1: EGGS in jelly-like clusters in water. Tiny white dots!
Stage 2: TADPOLE with a tail, lives in water, breathes through gills like a fish!
Stage 3: FROGLET grows legs, tail shrinks. Stage 4: ADULT FROG lives on land and water!
Frogs start in water and end on land. Their whole body transforms! Thats metamorphosis!

questions.hint: Start with the egg in water, then the swimming tadpole, then it grows legs, becomes a froglet, and finally a full-grown frog!
questions.prompt: Put the frog life cycle in the correct order! 🐸

questions.hint: Tadpoles live underwater! They breathe like fish do.
questions.prompt: How does a tadpole breathe? 🐟

questions.hint: The body absorbs the tail as the frog grows. It slowly gets smaller and smaller!
questions.prompt: What happens to a tadpole's tail as it grows into a frog? 🐸

questions.hint: Baby tadpoles need to swim right away! They need to be somewhere wet.
questions.prompt: Where do frogs lay their eggs? 🥚

title: Food Chain Fun
description: A food chain shows what eats what! Sun -> Grass -> Rabbit -> Fox. Build a food chain display on your M5Stick!
story_text: Chip is hungry, and so is everything in nature! A food chain shows who eats what. It starts with the SUN giving energy to plants. Then animals eat the plants, and bigger animals eat the smaller animals. Let's put it all in order and learn who's who!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Food Chain!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Food Chain!", 20, 5)
chain = [("Sun", 0xFFFF00), ("Grass", 0x00FF00), ("Rabbit", 0xFFFFFF), ("Fox", 0xFF9800)]
y = 30
for name, color in chain:
    Lcd.fillCircle(20, y + 5, 8, color)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 35, y)
    if name != "Fox":
        Lcd.drawString("->", 85, y)
    Speaker.tone(300 + chain.index((name, color)) * 60, 150)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 28
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Energy flows!", 15, 145)
Lcd.drawString("Sun to plants", 5, 163)
Lcd.drawString("to animals!", 10, 178)

hints.text: A FOOD CHAIN shows how energy flows from one living thing to another. It starts with the SUN!
The Sun gives energy to GRASS (plants make food from sunlight). Show the Sun as a yellow circle.
GRASS is eaten by a RABBIT. The rabbit gets energy from the grass.
The RABBIT is eaten by a FOX. The fox gets energy from the rabbit. Thats a food chain!
Draw arrows between each step. Energy flows: Sun -> Grass -> Rabbit -> Fox. Everything is connected!

questions.hint: Start with the sun! Then think: plants use sunlight, caterpillars eat plants, birds eat caterpillars, and hawks eat birds!
questions.prompt: Put this food chain in order from energy source to top predator! ☀️➡️🦅

activities.hint: Producers MAKE their own food from sunlight. Consumers EAT other living things. Decomposers break down dead plants and animals!
activities.prompt: Match each living thing to its role in the food chain! 🌎

title: Solid, Liquid, Gas
description: Matter comes in 3 forms: solid (like ice), liquid (like water), gas (like steam)! Show all 3 states on your M5Stick display!
story_text: Everything around you is made of MATTER, and matter comes in three forms! SOLIDS keep their shape (like a rock). LIQUIDS flow and take the shape of their container (like water). GASES spread out and fill up space (like the air you breathe). Let's sort them!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_rect"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">35</field></shadow></value><value name="WIDTH"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="HEIGHT"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">blue</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("3 States!", 25, 5)
Lcd.fillRect(20, 35, 30, 30, 0x0000FF)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("SOLID", 55, 43)
Lcd.drawString("(keeps shape)", 55, 58)
Speaker.tone(300, 200)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.fillCircle(35, 105, 15, 0x00BFFF)
Lcd.fillRect(15, 105, 45, 15, 0x00BFFF)
Lcd.drawString("LIQUID", 65, 98)
Lcd.drawString("(flows)", 65, 113)
Speaker.tone(400, 200)
time.sleep(1)
for i in range(4):
    Lcd.fillCircle(20 + i * 15, 160, 5, 0xCCCCCC)
Lcd.drawString("GAS", 80, 155)
Lcd.drawString("(spreads out)", 65, 170)
Speaker.tone(500, 200)

hints.text: Everything around you is MATTER, and it comes in 3 states: SOLID, LIQUID, and GAS!
SOLID keeps its shape -- draw a square for ice. Solids dont change shape!
LIQUID flows and fills its container -- draw a blob with a flat top for water.
GAS spreads everywhere -- draw tiny scattered dots for steam. Gas fills any space!
Water can be all 3: ice (solid), water (liquid), steam (gas). Temperature changes the state!

questions.hint: Can you hold an ice cube? Does it keep its shape? Solids keep their shape!
questions.prompt: Is an ice cube a solid, liquid, or gas? 🧊

questions.hint: Can you pour juice? Does it take the shape of the cup? That is what liquids do!
questions.prompt: Is juice a solid, liquid, or gas? 🧃

questions.hint: You can't see air, but it fills up the whole balloon. Gases spread out to fill their container!
questions.prompt: What state of matter is the air inside a balloon? 🎈

questions.hint: When solids get warm, they can melt! Ice melts into water.
questions.prompt: What happens when you heat an ice cube? 🔥🧊

questions.hint: Steam rises up and spreads through the air. You can see it but you can't hold it!
questions.prompt: Which of these is a GAS? 💨

questions.hint: You can pick it up and it stays the same shape. That means it is a solid!
questions.prompt: A wooden block is a ___. It keeps its shape! 🧱

title: Push and Pull
description: Forces make things move! A PUSH moves things away, a PULL brings things closer. Use the tilt sensor to feel push and pull forces!
story_text: Hey, Chip wants to know: how do things move? There are two main forces -- PUSH moves things AWAY from you, and PULL brings things TOWARD you! When you kick a ball, that's a push. When you open a drawer, that's a pull. Let's figure out which is which!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Push & Pull!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Push & Pull!", 10, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("PUSH ->", 10, 35)
Lcd.drawString("Moves AWAY", 10, 52)
Lcd.fillRect(90, 35, 20, 20, 0xFF0000)
Speaker.tone(500, 200)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.drawString("<- PULL", 10, 80)
Lcd.drawString("Comes CLOSER", 10, 97)
Lcd.fillRect(90, 80, 20, 20, 0x0000FF)
Speaker.tone(300, 200)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Tilt to feel", 10, 125)
Lcd.drawString("GRAVITY pull!", 10, 143)
x = int(Imu.getAccel()[0] * 50)
Lcd.fillCircle(67 + x, 175, 10, 0xFFFF00)

hints.text: PUSH and PULL are forces! A push moves something AWAY from you. A pull brings it CLOSER.
Show PUSH with an arrow going right (->). The red square gets pushed away!
Show PULL with an arrow going left (<-). The blue square gets pulled closer!
GRAVITY is a pull force! It pulls everything DOWN toward Earth. Thats why things fall!
Tilt your M5Stick -- the ball moves because gravity PULLS it. Gravity is always pulling!

activities.hint: Push means you move something AWAY from you. Pull means you bring something TOWARD you!

left.text: Pushing a friend on a swing

activities.prompt: Is each action a PUSH or a PULL? Match them up! 💪

questions.hint: The magnet sticks to the fridge -- it is pulling toward the metal!
questions.prompt: A magnet on the fridge holds up a picture. Is the magnet pushing or pulling? 🧲

questions.hint: A bigger push means more force! More force makes things move faster and farther.
questions.prompt: What happens if you push a toy car harder? 🚗

title: Simple Machines
description: Simple machines make work easier! Levers, wheels, ramps, and pulleys are everywhere. Display the 4 simple machines on your M5Stick!
story_text: Chip loves building things! Did you know there are SIX simple machines that help people do work? A ramp helps you roll things up. A lever helps you lift heavy stuff. A wheel helps things roll. Let's match each simple machine to something you've seen in real life!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Machines!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Simple Machines!", 2, 5)
machines = [("Lever", 0xFF0000), ("Wheel", 0x0000FF), ("Ramp", 0x00FF00), ("Pulley", 0xFF00FF)]
y = 30
for name, color in machines:
    Lcd.fillRect(10, y, 15, 15, color)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 30, y + 2)
    Speaker.tone(400, 100)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 25
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("They make work", 5, 135)
Lcd.drawString("EASIER!", 35, 155)

hints.text: SIMPLE MACHINES are tools that make work easier! You use them every day without realizing it.
A LEVER is like a seesaw -- it lifts heavy things! A WHEEL helps things roll instead of drag.
A RAMP (inclined plane) makes it easier to move things UP. Like a wheelchair ramp!
A PULLEY uses a rope and wheel to lift heavy things. Like a flag pole!
Display all 4 with colored icons. These simple tools are the building blocks of ALL machines!

activities.hint: Think about how each machine works! A ramp is a flat surface that goes up. A lever rocks back and forth. A pulley uses a rope to lift things.
activities.prompt: Match each simple machine to a real-life example! 🔧

title: Landform Explorer
description: Earth has many landforms! Mountains, valleys, plains, and islands. Show different landforms on your M5Stick with simple shapes!
story_text: Chip is going on a world tour! The Earth has so many cool shapes on its surface -- tall mountains, flat plains, deep valleys, and islands surrounded by water. Let's flip through some cards to learn about each landform, and then take a quiz to show what you know!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Landforms!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:

Lcd.fillRect(18, 100, 14, 25, 0x8B4513)

hints.text: LANDFORMS are natural features of Earth. Mountains go UP, valleys go DOWN, plains are FLAT, islands are surrounded by water!
Draw a MOUNTAIN: tall brown rectangles stacked to make a peak shape. Mountains are the tallest landforms!
Draw a PLAIN: a flat green rectangle. Plains are large, flat areas of land.
Draw an ISLAND: a brown shape with blue water around it. Islands are land surrounded by water!
Earth has SO many landforms! Each one was shaped over millions of years by wind, water, and earth movement.

back.text: A very tall, rocky landform that rises high above the land around it. Some have snow on top!
A low area of land between hills or mountains. Rivers often flow through valleys.
A large, flat area of land. Great for farming! Prairies and grasslands are plains.
Land that is completely surrounded by water on all sides. Hawaii is made of islands!
A very dry area that gets very little rain. Deserts can be hot or cold!
A raised area of land, smaller than a mountain. Hills have gentle slopes and rounded tops.

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about different landforms! 🌍

questions.hint: Think about which landform has water all around it!
questions.prompt: Which landform is completely surrounded by water? 🌊

questions.hint: This is a large, FLAT area of land. Perfect for growing crops!
questions.prompt: Which landform is flat and great for farming? 🌾

questions.hint: It sits LOW between two high places. Rivers often flow through it!
questions.prompt: A low area between two mountains is called a ___. 🏔️🌾🏔️

questions.hint: This rises VERY high above the land around it. Some have snow on top!
questions.prompt: Which landform is the TALLEST? 💪

title: What Do Plants Need?
description: Discover the four things every plant needs to grow big and strong!
story_text: Chip is planting a garden today! But wait -- what does a plant actually NEED to be happy and healthy? Plants can't go to the fridge for a snack like we can. They need four special things from nature: sunlight, water, air, and nutrients from the soil. Let's find out what each part of a plant does!

questions.hint: Plants love something bright and warm that comes from the sky every day!
questions.prompt: Which of these does a plant need to grow? 🌱

questions.hint: When it rains, plants are so happy! They drink this from the ground through their roots.
questions.prompt: What do plants drink to stay alive? 💧

questions.hint: We breathe OUT this gas, and plants breathe it IN! It starts with a C.
questions.prompt: Plants breathe in a gas from the air. What is it called? 🌬️

questions.hint: Roots grow down into the ground to soak up minerals and nutrients from the dirt!
questions.prompt: Where do plants get nutrients (plant food) from? 🌾

questions.hint: Two of these are real plant needs. One is something a person wears -- plants don't wear clothes!
questions.prompt: Which of these is NOT something a plant needs? 🤔

activities.hint: Roots are underground and drink water. Leaves are flat and catch sunshine. The stem is like a straw going up. Flowers make seeds!

right.text: Soak up water from the soil
Carry water up to the leaves

activities.prompt: Match each plant part to its job! 🌿

title: Seed Travelers
description: Find out how seeds hitch rides on the wind, water, and animals to travel the world!
story_text: Chip found a dandelion puff and blew on it -- WHOOSH! All the little seeds floated away on the wind! Seeds are amazing travelers. Some fly on the breeze, some float on water, some hitch rides on animal fur, and some get eaten by birds and dropped far away. Let's follow seeds on their incredible journeys!

activities.hint: Dandelion puffs float on air, coconuts can float on the ocean, burrs are sticky and grab onto fur, berries get eaten, and maple seeds spin like tiny helicopters!
activities.prompt: Match each seed to HOW it travels! 🌍✨

back.text: Dandelions have fluffy parachutes and maple seeds have wings that spin like helicopters. The wind carries them far away!
Coconuts have a hard, waterproof shell. They can float across the ocean to a new beach and grow into a palm tree!
Burr seeds have tiny hooks that grab onto animal fur or your socks! The animal carries them to a new spot.
Birds and animals eat yummy berries. The seeds inside pass through their tummy and get dropped in a new place with built-in fertilizer!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn how seeds travel! 🌱✈️

title: Pollination Partners
description: Meet the busy bees and other pollinators that help flowers make seeds!
story_text: Buzz buzz! Chip met a friendly bee in the garden today. Did you know bees are SUPER important helpers for flowers? When a bee visits a flower to drink sweet nectar, yellow powder called pollen sticks to its fuzzy body. Then the bee flies to another flower and delivers the pollen. This is called POLLINATION, and it helps flowers make seeds for new plants!

questions.hint: First the bee lands on a flower and drinks. Pollen sticks to it. Then it flies to a NEW flower and the pollen rubs off. Now that flower can make seeds!

items.text: A bee visits a flower to drink nectar
Pollen sticks to the bee's fuzzy body
The bee flies to a different flower
Pollen rubs off onto the new flower
The flower can now make seeds!

questions.prompt: Put the pollination steps in the correct order! 🐝🌺

activities.hint: Bees love bright colors! Butterflies need a flat place to land. Hummingbirds poke their long beaks into tube-shaped flowers. Bats come out at night!

right.text: Flat flowers it can land on

activities.prompt: Match each pollinator to the kind of flowers it visits! 🌻

title: Life Cycle: Butterfly
description: Watch the incredible transformation from tiny egg to beautiful butterfly!
story_text: Chip found a tiny egg on a leaf, no bigger than a pinhead! Over the next few weeks, it will go through one of the most AMAZING transformations in nature. First it hatches into a hungry caterpillar that eats and eats. Then it wraps itself in a chrysalis and something magical happens inside. Finally, out comes a beautiful butterfly with colorful wings! Let's explore each stage!

questions.hint: It starts as a tiny egg, then hatches into a caterpillar. The caterpillar wraps up in a chrysalis, and finally a butterfly comes out!
questions.prompt: Put the butterfly life cycle in the correct order! 🦋✨
