activities.hint: The egg is tiny and on a leaf. The caterpillar eats like crazy! The chrysalis is where the big change happens. The butterfly flies and starts the cycle again!

right.text: Tiny and laid on a leaf by the mother
Eats lots of leaves and grows bigger
Body transforms inside a hard shell
Flies, drinks nectar, and lays new eggs

activities.prompt: Match each stage to what happens during it! 🔍

title: Habitat Homes
description: Explore four amazing habitats and match animals to their perfect homes!
story_text: Chip is going on a world adventure! Every animal lives in a special place called a HABITAT -- it has the right food, water, and shelter for them to survive. Polar bears love the freezing Arctic, clownfish swim in warm ocean coral reefs, monkeys swing through steamy rainforests, and camels walk across hot, dry deserts. Let's explore these amazing places and find out who lives where!

activities.hint: Think about what each animal needs! Polar bears need cold and ice. Clownfish need warm water and coral. Monkeys need trees to swing from. Camels can go a long time without water in the hot desert!
activities.prompt: Match each animal to the habitat where it lives! 🌍🐾

questions.hint: This place is full of sand and gets very little rain. It can be VERY hot during the day!
questions.prompt: Which habitat is hot and dry with very little rain? ☀️

questions.hint: The Arctic is FREEZING cold! Animals there need to stay warm and hide from predators in the snow.
questions.prompt: Why do polar bears have thick, white fur? 🐻‍❄️

options.text: To stay warm and blend in with the snow

questions.hint: The name gives a big clue! RAIN-forest. It rains a lot there, and it is warm and green!
questions.prompt: What makes the rainforest special? 🌿

options.text: It is warm and gets lots of rain

questions.hint: Camels are amazing! Their humps store fat for energy, and their bodies are great at saving water.
questions.prompt: How does a camel survive in the desert? 🐫

options.text: It can store water and go days without drinking
It only comes out at night

questions.hint: It sounds like the word "habit" with "at" on the end. It means an animal's natural home!
questions.prompt: Which word means the place where an animal naturally lives? 🏠

title: Push and Pull
description: Discover the two forces that make EVERYTHING move -- pushes and pulls!
story_text: Whoa, Chip just figured out something HUGE! Everything that moves does so because of a PUSH or a PULL! When you kick a soccer ball, that's a push -- you move it AWAY from you. When you pull a wagon, that's a pull -- you bring it TOWARD you. Let's see if you can tell the difference!

activities.hint: Push means you move something AWAY from you. Pull means you bring something TOWARD you! Think about which direction the thing moves.
activities.prompt: Is each action a PUSH or a PULL? Match them up! 💪

questions.hint: You send the ball AWAY from you down the lane. That means it is a push!
questions.prompt: You roll a bowling ball down the lane. Is that a push or a pull? 🎳

questions.hint: The dog is bringing you TOWARD it by tugging the leash. That is a pull!
questions.prompt: A dog walks you on a leash! The leash tugs you forward. Is that a push or a pull? 🐶

questions.hint: These two forces are opposites -- one moves things away, one brings things closer!
questions.prompt: What are the TWO main forces that make things move? 🤔

questions.hint: The magnet sticks TO the fridge -- it attracts the metal. That is a pull!
questions.prompt: A magnet on the fridge holds a picture. Is the magnet pushing or pulling? 🧲

title: Bigger Push, Bigger Change
description: Find out what happens when you push harder or softer!
story_text: Chip has been experimenting! Watch this -- if you give a ball a tiny little push, it barely rolls. But if you give it a BIG push, it zooms across the room! The HARDER you push or pull, the MORE something moves. Let's explore how the size of a force changes what happens!

questions.hint: A bigger push means more force! More force = more speed and more distance!
questions.prompt: What happens if you push a toy car HARDER? 🚗💨

questions.hint: A small push gives only a small amount of force. The ball moves, but not very far!
questions.prompt: You give a ball a gentle tap with your finger. What happens? 👆⚾

options.text: It rolls just a little bit
It does not move at all

questions.hint: The bigger force wins! The box moves in the direction of the STRONGER push.
questions.prompt: Two kids push a box. One pushes gently, one pushes HARD. Whose side does the box move toward? 📦

questions.hint: A bigger kick means a bigger force. Bigger force = the ball goes farther!
questions.prompt: You want to kick a soccer ball really far. What should you do? ⚽🦶

questions.hint: Think about how much force each one uses. A tiny tap is the smallest force. A running kick is the biggest!

items.text: Tiny finger tap on a ball

questions.prompt: Put these pushes in order from LEAST change to MOST change! 💪

questions.hint: Start with the smallest push (barely moves) and end with the biggest push (zooms super far)!
questions.prompt: Put these in order: what happens when you push a ball harder and harder? ⚽

title: Friction: Slippery vs Sticky
description: Learn how friction slows things down -- and why some surfaces are slippery and others are sticky!
story_text: Whee! Chip just tried sliding on the kitchen floor in socks -- SO slippery! But then Chip tried sliding on the carpet -- NOT slippery at all! That's because of FRICTION. Friction is a force that slows things down when surfaces rub together. Smooth surfaces have LOW friction (slippery!) and rough surfaces have HIGH friction (sticky!). Let's explore!

questions.hint: Rough surfaces grab onto things more. Carpet is bumpy and rough, so it has lots of friction!
questions.prompt: Which surface has MORE friction? 🤔

questions.hint: Grass is rough and bumpy -- it creates more friction, which makes it harder to push the box!
questions.prompt: You push a box across a smooth wooden floor, then across grass. Where is it HARDER to push? 📦

questions.hint: The bumps create more friction so you don't slip! Friction helps you grip the ground.
questions.prompt: Why do sneakers have bumpy bottoms? 👟

options.text: To grip the ground with friction

questions.hint: Friction is a force that works AGAINST motion. It rubs and slows things down!
questions.prompt: What does friction do to a moving object? 🛑

activities.hint: Smooth and wet surfaces are slippery (low friction). Rough and bumpy surfaces are grippy (high friction). Grass is in between!
activities.prompt: Match each surface to its friction level! 🎯

title: Simple Machines Around Us
description: Learn about ramps, levers, wheels, and pulleys -- the machines hiding in everyday life!
story_text: Chip went on a scavenger hunt and found MACHINES everywhere! But these are not big machines with motors -- they are SIMPLE machines that make work easier. A ramp helps you roll heavy things up. A lever helps you lift stuff. A wheel helps things move. A pulley uses a rope to lift things high. Let's learn about each one!

back.text: A flat surface that goes up at an angle. It helps you move heavy things up without lifting them straight up! Example: a wheelchair ramp.
A bar that rests on a point (called a fulcrum) and helps you lift heavy things. Push one end down, the other end goes up! Example: a seesaw.
A wheel that spins around a rod (axle). It helps things roll instead of slide, which is much easier! Example: a bicycle wheel.
A wheel with a rope around it. Pull the rope DOWN to lift something UP! Example: a flagpole -- you pull the rope to raise the flag.

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about simple machines! 🔧

activities.hint: Think about how each machine works! A ramp goes up at an angle. A lever rocks back and forth on a point. A pulley uses a rope over a wheel.
activities.prompt: Match each simple machine to a real-life example! 🎯

title: Gravity: What Goes Up...
description: Learn about Earth's invisible pulling force that keeps everything on the ground!
story_text: Chip tried jumping REALLY high today, but kept coming back down! Why? Because of GRAVITY! Gravity is an invisible force that PULLS everything toward the center of the Earth. That is why when you toss a ball up, it always comes back down. Without gravity, we would all float away into space! Let's learn about this amazing force!

questions.hint: Gravity pulls everything DOWN toward the ground. The ball falls because gravity is pulling it!
questions.prompt: What happens when you drop a ball? 🎾

options.text: It falls down to the ground
It flies up to the sky

questions.hint: Earth has a special pulling force called gravity. It keeps us on the ground!
questions.prompt: Why don't we float away into space? 🌍

options.text: Because gravity pulls us toward Earth
Because the air pushes us down

questions.hint: Gravity always pulls things DOWN. Even when you throw something up, gravity brings it back!
questions.prompt: You throw a ball straight up in the air. What does gravity do? 🎾⬆️

options.text: Nothing -- the ball keeps going up forever

questions.hint: Gravity brings things TOWARD the Earth. That is a pull!
questions.prompt: Is gravity a push or a pull? 🤔

questions.hint: The Moon is smaller than Earth, so its gravity is weaker. Less gravity = higher jumps!
questions.prompt: On the Moon, astronauts can jump SUPER high! Why? 🌙👨‍🚀

options.text: The Moon has less gravity than Earth
There is no air on the Moon

questions.hint: This is a tricky one! Gravity actually pulls ALL objects at the same speed, but AIR slows down light things like feathers. Without air, they would all fall at the same speed!
questions.prompt: If you drop these objects from the same height, gravity pulls them all down! Put them in order from what you THINK falls fastest to slowest. ⬇️

title: Mountains, Valleys & Plains
description: Learn about the amazing shapes of Earth's surface -- from tall mountains to flat plains!
story_text: Pack your hiking boots -- Chip is going on a geography adventure! Earth's surface isn't all the same. Some parts are SUPER tall like mountains, some are low and flat like plains, and some are tucked between hills like valleys. Each landform is special and different animals and plants live in each one. Let's explore!

back.text: A very tall, rocky landform that rises high above the land around it. Mountains can have snow on top and animals like mountain goats live there!
A low area of land between hills or mountains. Rivers often flow through valleys, and many farms are found in valleys because the soil is rich!
A large, flat area of land that stretches far and wide. Plains are great for farming and you can see for miles! Prairies are a type of plain.
A raised area of land that is shorter than a mountain. Hills have gentle slopes and rounded tops -- perfect for rolling down!
A deep, narrow valley with steep sides, carved by a river over millions of years. The Grand Canyon is a famous one!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about landforms on Earth! 🌍

activities.hint: Think about each landform's shape! Mountains are tall, valleys are low, plains are flat, hills are shorter than mountains, and canyons are deep and narrow.
activities.prompt: Match each landform to its description! 🧩

title: Rivers, Lakes & Oceans
description: Explore the amazing bodies of water on Earth -- from tiny streams to giant oceans!
story_text: Splash! Chip is exploring bodies of water today! Did you know that water covers MORE than half of Earth's surface? There are rushing rivers, calm lakes, salty oceans, and bubbly streams. Each body of water is different, and each one is home to special plants and animals. Let's dive in!

activities.hint: Rivers FLOW and move toward the ocean. Lakes are surrounded by land and stay still. Oceans are HUGE and salty. Streams are like baby rivers. Ponds are like baby lakes!

right.text: Flowing water that moves to the sea

activities.prompt: Match each body of water to its description! 🌊

questions.hint: This body of water is SO big that it covers most of Earth's surface! It's salty too.
questions.prompt: Which body of water is the BIGGEST? 🌍

questions.hint: If you've been to the beach, you might have tasted the water. It's salty!
questions.prompt: What kind of water do oceans have? 🦬

questions.hint: Rivers flow downhill and keep going until they reach a bigger body of water!
questions.prompt: Where does a river usually end up? 🌊

options.text: It flows into the ocean or a lake

questions.hint: This body of water sits in the middle of the land. You can walk all the way around it!
questions.prompt: Which body of water is surrounded by land on ALL sides? 🏞️

questions.hint: Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Southern, and Arctic -- count them up!
questions.prompt: How many oceans does Earth have? 🌎

title: Slow Changes: Erosion & Weathering
description: Discover how wind, water, and ice slowly change the shape of Earth over a long time!
story_text: Chip just learned something mind-blowing! Earth's surface is ALWAYS changing, but some changes happen so slowly that you can't even see them! Wind blows tiny bits of rock away. Water wears down mountains over millions of years. Ice cracks rocks apart. These slow changes are called EROSION and WEATHERING. Let's watch Earth change in slow motion!

questions.hint: Start with rain! Then water collects, picks up rocks and dirt, cuts deeper and deeper, and after a VERY long time, a canyon forms!

items.text: Water carries away tiny rocks and soil
The stream cuts deeper over time
A canyon forms after millions of years

questions.prompt: Put these steps of river erosion in order! How does a river carve a canyon? 🌊🏜️

questions.hint: Weathering is about rocks breaking apart! Wind, water, and ice slowly break rocks into smaller and smaller pieces.
questions.prompt: What is WEATHERING? 🪨

options.text: When rocks break down into smaller pieces

questions.hint: Erosion is when wind or water MOVES broken pieces of rock and soil to a new place!
questions.prompt: What is EROSION? 🌊

options.text: When broken rock and soil are carried away

questions.hint: This is a VERY slow process. Think about the biggest number!
questions.prompt: How long does it take for a river to carve a deep canyon? ⏳

questions.hint: Weathering is caused by natural forces. Which option involves a force of nature acting on rocks?
questions.prompt: Which of these can cause weathering? 🤔

questions.hint: Think about how long it takes a river to carve a canyon. Is that fast or slow?
questions.prompt: Is erosion a SLOW change or a FAST change? 🐢

options.text: Slow -- it takes a very long time

title: Fast Changes: Earthquakes & Volcanoes
description: Learn about Earth's powerful quick changes -- earthquakes shake the ground and volcanoes erupt with lava!
story_text: Whoa, hold on tight! Chip is learning about Earth's FASTEST and most POWERFUL changes! Unlike erosion that takes millions of years, earthquakes can shake the ground in SECONDS, and volcanoes can shoot lava into the sky in MINUTES! These fast changes can reshape the land very quickly. Let's find out how they work!

back.text: A sudden shaking of the ground caused by rocks deep underground moving and shifting. Earthquakes can crack roads and knock down buildings!
An opening in Earth's surface where hot melted rock (lava), ash, and gases escape from deep inside Earth. When a volcano erupts, it can build new land!
When rocks, mud, and soil slide quickly down a hillside. Heavy rain or earthquakes can trigger landslides!
When water covers land that is usually dry. Heavy rain or melting snow can cause rivers to overflow their banks!
Hot melted rock that flows out of a volcano. When lava cools down, it turns into new rock and can even create new islands!

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about Earth's fast changes! 🌋

questions.hint: Earthquakes happen because of what's going on DEEP below the ground. Giant pieces of rock move and bump into each other!
questions.prompt: What causes an earthquake? 🌍

options.text: Rocks deep underground moving and shifting

questions.hint: Volcanoes are VERY hot inside! Think about what kind of super-hot stuff would come out.
questions.prompt: What comes out of a volcano when it erupts? 🌋

questions.hint: Unlike erosion, these events happen VERY quickly! An earthquake can last just seconds!
questions.prompt: Are earthquakes and volcanoes SLOW or FAST changes? ⚡

options.text: Slow -- they take millions of years
Fast -- they can happen in minutes or seconds

questions.hint: Lava is melted rock. When it cools down, it hardens back into rock -- brand new rock!
questions.prompt: What can happen when lava from a volcano cools down? 🔥→🪨

questions.hint: Erosion takes millions of years (slow like a turtle). Earthquakes happen in seconds (fast like lightning)!
questions.prompt: Which is a SLOW Earth change and which is a FAST Earth change? 🐢⚡

options.text: Erosion is slow, earthquakes are fast
Earthquakes are slow, erosion is fast

title: Protecting Our Land
description: Learn how people help protect Earth's land, water, and animals!
story_text: Chip has a big heart and wants to help protect our planet! Earth's land and water are precious, and people can do lots of things to take care of them. We can plant trees to stop erosion, keep rivers clean, protect animal homes, and recycle to reduce waste. Let's become Earth protectors together!

questions.hint: Tree roots grab onto the soil like little fingers. They hold the dirt in place so rain doesn't wash it away!
questions.prompt: Why do people plant trees on hillsides? 🌳

options.text: To hold the soil in place and prevent erosion

questions.hint: Without tree roots holding the soil, rain and wind can carry the dirt away. That's erosion!
questions.prompt: What happens when too many trees are cut down? 🪵

options.text: The soil washes away more easily

questions.hint: Earth protectors keep our land clean! Which choice helps keep our planet healthy?
questions.prompt: What is one way YOU can help protect Earth? 🌍

options.text: Waste water by leaving the tap on

questions.hint: National parks are special places where nature is kept safe so animals and plants can thrive!
questions.prompt: Why are national parks important? 🏞️

options.text: They protect land, water, and animals from being harmed
They are places to build new buildings

questions.hint: Animals drink from rivers and lakes, fish live in them, and people need clean water too!
questions.prompt: Keeping rivers and lakes clean helps ___ 💧

options.text: Fish, plants, and people who need clean water
Make the water turn into lava

activities.hint: Think about what causes each problem, then find the action that fixes it! Trees stop erosion, keeping water clean helps fish, parks protect animals, and recycling reduces trash.

right.text: Plant trees to hold the soil

activities.prompt: Match each problem with the solution that helps protect Earth! 🌍❤️

title: Meet the Notes
description: Meet the musical notes! Each note has its own special sound. Program your M5Stick to play Do, Re, Mi, Fa, Sol -- the first five notes of the musical scale!
story_text: Chip starts humming. "Do... Re... Mi..." he sings. "Every song is made from these special sounds called NOTES! Do is like home base -- it feels warm and safe. Re goes a little higher. Mi goes even higher! Each note has its own color on my screen and its own sound from my buzzer. Press the buttons to play each note and hear the difference. Do, re, mi, fa, sol, la, ti, do!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Do!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">white</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">262</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">400</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Meet the Notes!", 10, 10)
notes = [("Do", 262), ("Re", 294), ("Mi", 330), ("Fa", 349), ("Sol", 392)]
y = 40
for name, freq in notes:
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 50, y)
    Speaker.tone(freq, 400)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    y = y + 22

hints.text: Musical notes have names: Do, Re, Mi, Fa, Sol, La, Ti! Each one sounds a little higher than the last. Find the Sound tab to start!
The first note is Do -- set frequency to 262. Drag a "play tone" block and set duration to 400ms.
Add more tone blocks below for Re (294), Mi (330), Fa (349), and Sol (392). Each number is a little bigger = a little higher!
Put a "wait" block (0.5 seconds) between each note so they do not all play at once.
Add a Display text block before each tone to show the note name. Now you can READ and HEAR each note!

story.title: The Sound of Music
story.narrative: Chip discovers that every song is made from special sounds called notes. Meet do, re, and mi!

create.title: My Note Song
create.prompt: Pick 3 favorite notes and play them in a pattern. That is the start of YOUR song!

explore.title: Play the Notes
explore.instructions: The buzzer plays each note. The display shows the note name and a color. Press buttons to cycle through do, re, mi, fa, sol!
explore.device_interaction: Buzzer plays musical notes. Display shows note name with color. Buttons navigate notes.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click buttons to hear and see each musical note.

celebrate.title: Note Master!
celebrate.summary: You met the musical notes! Do, re, mi -- every song in the world uses these sounds.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child learned basic solfege (do, re, mi, fa, sol) with pitch discrimination exercises.

title: High and Low
description: Dive deeper into pitch! High notes and low notes create melodies when combined. Program your M5Stick to play a pattern that goes up and then back down!
story_text: Chip tilts himself up and his buzzer goes EEEEE! He tilts down and it goes OOOOO! "Tilt UP = high sound! Tilt DOWN = low sound!" he laughs. "Pitch is how HIGH or LOW a sound is. A tiny bird sings high, a big bear growls low. The cool thing is, pitch is actually about how FAST the sound vibrates. Higher sounds vibrate faster! Let us explore pitch by tilting and listening!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_buzzer_tone" x="20" y="20"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">262</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">300</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_wait"><value name="SECONDS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">0.4</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Up and Down!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Going UP...", 10, 35)
up = [262, 330, 392, 523]
for freq in up:
    Speaker.tone(freq, 300)
    time.sleep(0.4)
Lcd.drawString("Going DOWN...", 10, 60)
down = [523, 392, 330, 262]
for freq in down:
    Speaker.tone(freq, 300)
    time.sleep(0.4)
Lcd.drawString("Like a mountain!", 10, 90)

hints.text: When notes go from LOW to HIGH, the melody goes UP -- like climbing a hill! Low to high means the frequency numbers get bigger.
Start with a low note: 262 (Do). Then play 330 (Mi), 392 (Sol), and 523 (high Do). Each is higher!
Now go back DOWN: 523, 392, 330, 262. The melody comes back down the hill!
Add waits between notes so you can hear each one clearly. Try 0.4 seconds.
Display "Going UP" and "Going DOWN" as labels. Your melody goes up like a mountain and back down!

story.title: The Pitch Roller Coaster
story.narrative: Chip discovers that tilting controls pitch! Up is high, down is low. Explore the relationship between movement and sound.

create.title: Pitch Story
create.prompt: Use high and low sounds to tell a story! Maybe a bird (high) talks to a bear (low). What do they say?

explore.title: Tilt to Play
explore.instructions: Tilt the device up to raise the pitch and down to lower it. Watch the wave on the display change speed!
explore.device_interaction: IMU tilt controls buzzer frequency. Display shows frequency wave stretching/compressing.
explore.simulator_fallback: Use a slider to control pitch. See and hear the result.

celebrate.title: Pitch Pro!
celebrate.summary: You understand pitch! High sounds vibrate fast, low sounds vibrate slow. You can hear the difference!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child explored pitch as frequency through kinesthetic interaction (tilt = pitch change).

title: Beat Keeper
description: Be the beat keeper! A metronome helps musicians keep steady time. Build a metronome on your M5Stick that clicks at a steady beat with a visual counter!
story_text: Chip starts tapping. TAP... TAP... TAP... TAP... "That is a BEAT!" he says. "Music has a heartbeat, just like you do! A STEADY BEAT goes on and on at the same speed. Try tapping along with my buzzer. GREEN dot means you are ON the beat. Red means you missed it. Let us see how many beats in a row you can hit! Ready... tap... tap... tap..."
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Beat: 1</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">white</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">600</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Beat Keeper!", 15, 10)
for measure in range(4):
    for beat in range(4):
        Lcd.fillRect(0, 50, 135, 40, 0x000000)
        Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
        if beat == 0:
            Lcd.drawString("Beat: 1 (STRONG)", 5, 60)
            Speaker.tone(600, 80)
        else:
            Lcd.drawString("Beat: " + str(beat + 1), 30, 60)
            Speaker.tone(400, 50)
        time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Great timing!", 15, 100)

hints.text: A metronome goes TICK TICK TICK at a steady speed. The first beat of each group is usually STRONGER. Lets build one!
Start with a click sound: tone 600 at 50ms -- short and sharp like a metronome tick!
Add a wait of 0.5 seconds, then repeat. Put it in a loop of 4 beats -- that makes one measure!
Make beat 1 special! Use a HIGHER tone (600) for beat 1 and lower (400) for beats 2, 3, 4.
Display the beat number as it counts: 1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 2, 3, 4. You are keeping time like a real musician!

story.title: The Rhythm Heart
story.narrative: Chip discovers that music has a heartbeat called a steady beat. Learn to tap along!

create.title: My Beat Song
create.prompt: Create a beat pattern by tapping different rhythms. Fast taps, slow taps, pauses. Make it YOUR rhythm!

explore.title: Tap the Beat
explore.instructions: The buzzer plays a steady beat. Press the button in time! Display shows green for on-beat, red for off-beat.
explore.device_interaction: Buzzer plays steady clicks. Buttons tap along. Display shows beat visualization with accuracy dots.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click in time with the audio beat.

celebrate.title: Beat Keeper!
celebrate.summary: You can feel and keep a steady beat! That is the foundation of all music.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child practiced maintaining a steady beat, a foundational rhythmic skill.

title: High and Low Sounds!
description: Test your ear! Can you tell high sounds from low sounds? Program a quiz on your M5Stick that plays a sound and shows if it was high or low!
story_text: Chip is at the music store! Some instruments make high sounds and some make low sounds. Can you tell which is which?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Sound Quiz!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">800</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">500</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Sound Quiz!", 15, 10)
import random
sounds = [(200, "LOW"), (400, "MEDIUM"), (800, "HIGH")]
for i in range(4):
    freq, label = sounds[random.randint(0, 2)]
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 40, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("Listen...", 30, 50)
    Speaker.tone(freq, 500)
    time.sleep(0.7)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 40, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("That was " + label + "!", 10, 50)
    Lcd.drawString("(" + str(freq) + " Hz)", 25, 75)
    time.sleep(1.5)

hints.text: Lets make a quiz! Play a random sound and show if it was high, medium, or low. Can your ears tell the difference?
Pick three sounds: LOW (200), MEDIUM (400), and HIGH (800). Each is very different!
Play one sound, wait a moment, then show the answer on the display. Was it high or low?
Use a loop to play several rounds. Each time pick a different sound to keep it interesting!
Show the frequency number too -- now you can connect what you HEAR with the number. Higher number = higher pitch!

questions.hint: Small animals usually make high-pitched sounds!
questions.prompt: A tiny bird singing makes what kind of sound?

questions.hint: Big animals usually make low, deep sounds!
questions.prompt: A big bear growling makes what kind of sound?

questions.hint: Whistles make a sharp, high-pitched sound!
questions.prompt: A whistle makes what kind of sound?

questions.hint: Big drums go BOOM -- that is a low sound!
questions.prompt: A big drum makes what kind of sound?

questions.hint: The flute is small and makes high sounds. The tuba is big and makes low sounds!
questions.prompt: Which instrument makes a HIGHER sound?

title: Fast or Slow Music!
description: Listen and decide -- is the music fast or slow? Build a tempo game where your M5Stick plays at random speeds and you guess the tempo!
story_text: Music can be fast like a race car or slow like a sleepy cat! Match each activity to its tempo.
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Tempo Game!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">150</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Tempo Game!", 15, 10)
import random
tempos = [(0.8, "Adagio (Slow)"), (0.4, "Moderato (Medium)"), (0.15, "Presto (Fast!)")]
for i in range(3):
    wait, name = tempos[random.randint(0, 2)]
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 35, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("Listen...", 30, 50)
    for j in range(6):
        Speaker.tone(440, 100)
        time.sleep(wait)
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 35, 135, 80, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name, 5, 50)
    time.sleep(2)

hints.text: Tempo is the speed of the beat. Can you tell if beats are fast or slow? Lets play a guessing game!
Create three speeds: Slow (0.8s between beats), Medium (0.4s), and Fast (0.15s).
Play 6 beats at one speed. The tone is the same each time -- only the SPACE between beats changes!
After the beats play, show the answer: was it Adagio, Moderato, or Presto?
Loop it 3 times to play 3 rounds. Try guessing before the answer shows -- train your ear!

activities.hint: Think about how you move -- fast activities need fast music!
activities.prompt: Match each activity to fast or slow music!

title: Pattern Songs
description: Patterns are everywhere in music! A pattern is something that repeats. Program your M5Stick to play a musical pattern -- two notes that repeat over and over!
story_text: Chip plays: do-re-mi, do-re-mi, do-re-mi. "Did you hear that?" he says. "The same three notes keep REPEATING! That is a PATTERN in music! Patterns make songs fun because your brain KNOWS what comes next. Listen: do-re-mi... what comes next? DO-RE-MI! You guessed it! Now let us make our own patterns. I will play one, you repeat it with the buttons!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_buzzer_tone" x="20" y="20"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">300</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_wait"><value name="SECONDS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">0.3</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Pattern Songs!", 10, 10)
for i in range(4):
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("A", 60, 60)
    Speaker.tone(440, 300)
    time.sleep(0.4)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0x0000FF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("B", 60, 60)
    Speaker.tone(330, 300)
    time.sleep(0.4)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("AB AB AB AB!", 10, 100)

hints.text: A pattern repeats! Think AB AB AB -- like clap-stomp, clap-stomp. Lets make a sound pattern with two different tones.
Drag a "play tone" block with frequency 440 (note A). Add a "wait" block after it (0.3 seconds).
Now add a SECOND tone block with frequency 330 (note E). Add another wait. Thats your A-B pattern!
Wrap both tones inside a Loops "repeat" block. Set it to repeat 4 times -- now your pattern plays over and over!
Add Display blocks to show "A" and "B" as each note plays. Can you see AND hear the pattern?

story.title: The Pattern Band
story.narrative: Chip discovers that music is full of repeating patterns. Learn to hear, repeat, and create them!

create.title: My Pattern Song
create.prompt: Create a musical pattern with 3-4 notes that repeats. Play it for Chip! Can he predict what comes next?

explore.title: Copy the Pattern
explore.instructions: Buzzer plays a short note pattern. Listen, then repeat it using the buttons! Display shows the pattern visually.
explore.device_interaction: Buzzer plays pattern. Buttons replay it. Display shows note sequence as colored dots.
explore.simulator_fallback: Listen to patterns and click to repeat them.

celebrate.title: Pattern Pro!
celebrate.summary: You can hear, repeat, and create musical patterns! Patterns are the building blocks of songs.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child practiced musical pattern recognition and creation, connecting to mathematical pattern skills.

title: Fast and Slow
description: Explore tempo more! Allegro means fast and Adagio means slow. Program two different tempos and use buttons to switch between them!
story_text: Chip plays a song really fast, then really slow. "SAME song, totally different feeling!" he says. "Fast music makes you want to DANCE! Slow music makes you want to RELAX. The speed of music is called TEMPO. Tilt me forward to speed up and backward to slow down! Watch the metronome on screen change. Can you find the perfect tempo for your favorite song?"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Tempo Switch!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">330</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Tempo Switch!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Adagio (slow):", 10, 40)
for i in range(4):
    Speaker.tone(330, 200)
    time.sleep(0.8)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Allegro (fast):", 10, 70)
for i in range(8):
    Speaker.tone(330, 100)
    time.sleep(0.2)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Presto (super fast):", 10, 100)
for i in range(12):
    Speaker.tone(330, 50)
    time.sleep(0.1)

hints.text: Musicians use Italian words for speed: Adagio = slow, Allegro = fast, Presto = super fast! Lets hear them all.
For Adagio, play a tone and wait 0.8 seconds between beats. Nice and slow like walking.
For Allegro, same tone but wait only 0.2 seconds. Much faster, like jogging!
For Presto, wait only 0.1 seconds! Thats super fast, like running!
Use the display to label each tempo section. The same beat sounds totally different at different speeds!

story.title: The Speed DJ
story.narrative: Chip discovers that the same song feels totally different at different speeds. Explore tempo!

create.title: Tempo Remix
create.prompt: Take a simple melody and play it at 3 different tempos. How does the feeling change? Which do you like best?

explore.title: Tempo Controller
explore.instructions: Tilt the device to control tempo. Forward = faster, backward = slower. The display shows a metronome keeping time.
explore.device_interaction: IMU tilt controls buzzer tempo. Display shows animated metronome at current BPM.
explore.simulator_fallback: Use a slider to control tempo. See the metronome react.

celebrate.title: Tempo Master!
celebrate.summary: You understand tempo! Fast, slow, and everything in between -- tempo changes the mood of music.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child explored tempo (speed) and its relationship to musical expression and emotion.

title: Loud and Soft
description: Practice dynamics! Crescendo means getting louder, decrescendo means getting softer. Program a melody that starts soft and gets loud, then fades away!
story_text: Chip whispers: "piano..." then SHOUTS: "FORTE!" He giggles. "In music, PIANO means soft and FORTE means loud. Musicians use dynamics to make music interesting -- sometimes quiet like a secret, sometimes loud like a celebration! Shake me gently for piano and shake me hard for forte! The harder you shake, the louder I play!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_buzzer_tone" x="20" y="20"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">100</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_wait"><value name="SECONDS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">0.3</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Dynamics!", 25, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Crescendo...", 10, 35)
durations = [100, 200, 300, 400, 500]
for dur in durations:
    Speaker.tone(440, dur)
    time.sleep(dur / 1000 + 0.1)
time.sleep(0.3)
Lcd.drawString("Decrescendo...", 10, 60)
for dur in [500, 400, 300, 200, 100]:
    Speaker.tone(440, dur)
    time.sleep(dur / 1000 + 0.1)
Lcd.drawString("pp < ff > pp", 15, 90)

hints.text: Crescendo means getting LOUDER (like sunrise getting brighter). Decrescendo means getting SOFTER (like sunset fading).
Start with a short quiet tone (100ms). Then play a slightly longer one (200ms). Each note gets longer = louder!
Keep increasing: 300ms, 400ms, 500ms. You just made a crescendo -- going from soft to loud!
Now reverse it: 500ms, 400ms, 300ms, 200ms, 100ms. Getting softer -- thats a decrescendo!
Display the music symbols: pp (very soft), ff (very loud). Musicians read these on sheet music!

story.title: Whisper and Shout
story.narrative: Chip learns that music uses soft (piano) and loud (forte) to express feelings. Explore dynamics!

create.title: Dynamic Story
create.prompt: Tell a musical story using loud and soft. Start quiet (sneaking), get loud (surprise!), then quiet again (sleeping).

explore.title: Shake to Play
explore.instructions: Shake the device gently for soft (piano) sounds and hard for loud (forte) sounds. The IMU measures your shake intensity!
explore.device_interaction: IMU shake intensity controls buzzer volume. Display shows dynamic level: pp, p, mf, f, ff.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click buttons for different volume levels.

celebrate.title: Dynamics Star!
celebrate.summary: You know piano and forte! Using loud and soft makes music come alive with expression.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child explored musical dynamics (volume control) through kinesthetic interaction (shake intensity).

title: Beat Keeper!
description: Build a visual beat keeper! Watch a circle pulse on your screen in time with the beat. Program your M5Stick to show beats you can see AND hear!
story_text: Chip wants to be a drummer! But first, Chip needs to learn to count beats. Help Chip count the right number of beats!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">500</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">100</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Beat Keeper!", 15, 10)
for i in range(8):
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 100, 35, 0xFF9800)
    Speaker.tone(500, 100)
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(str(i + 1), 62, 95)
    time.sleep(0.3)
    Lcd.fillCircle(67, 100, 35, 0x000000)
    time.sleep(0.3)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("8 beats!", 35, 145)

hints.text: A beat keeper shows you the beat visually! Each beat will flash a circle on screen AND play a click sound.
Draw a filled orange circle in the center of the screen. Thats your beat indicator!
Play a short click (tone 500, 100ms) at the same time as the circle appears.
After a short wait, erase the circle (draw a black circle over it). Wait again. Thats one beat!
Put it all in a loop for 8 beats. Display the beat number inside the circle so you can count along!

questions.hint: Count each drum: 1, 2!
questions.prompt: How many drum beats do you see?

questions.hint: Count each note: 1, 2, 3, 4!
questions.prompt: How many music notes are there?

questions.hint: Clap along: 1, 2, 3!
questions.prompt: How many claps in this pattern?

questions.hint: Keep counting: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6!
questions.prompt: Count the tambourine shakes!

title: My First Song
description: Time to compose your first song! A song is a series of notes played in order. Program your M5Stick to play a simple melody using different notes!
story_text: Chip is bouncing with excitement. "WE ARE GOING TO WRITE A SONG!" he shouts. "A song is just notes in a special order. Pick 4 notes -- any notes you like! Arrange them in an order that sounds good to you. Press both buttons to play it back and hear YOUR song! You are a real composer now! Mozart started with just a few notes too, you know."
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">My Song!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">purple</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">262</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">300</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF00FF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("My First Song!", 10, 10)
time.sleep(0.5)
melody = [(262, 300), (262, 300), (392, 300), (392, 300), (440, 300), (440, 300), (392, 600)]
for freq, dur in melody:
    Speaker.tone(freq, dur)
    time.sleep(dur / 1000 + 0.1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Twinkle!", 30, 60)

hints.text: A song is notes played one after another -- like telling a story with sounds! Lets build a simple melody.
Start with "play tone" at 262 (Do) for 300ms. This is your first note!
Add more tone blocks: 262, 392, 392, 440, 440, 392. Put a small wait between each one.
Make the last note longer! Change its duration to 600ms. Longer notes sound like the end of a phrase.
Add a title on the display. Does it sound familiar? You just played the start of Twinkle Twinkle!

story.title: The Composer
story.narrative: Chip is ready to write a song! Choose notes, arrange them, and become a composer!

create.title: My Masterpiece
create.prompt: Compose a 4-note song and give it a name. Play it for someone and tell them why you chose those notes!

explore.title: Build Your Melody
explore.instructions: Select 4 notes using the buttons. The display shows a simple staff with your notes. Press both buttons to play it back!
explore.device_interaction: Buttons select notes. Display shows simple staff notation. Buzzer plays back composition.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click to place notes on a staff and play them back.

celebrate.title: Composer!
celebrate.summary: You wrote your first song! Real composers start exactly like this -- with a few notes and a big idea.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child composed a short melody, developing creative expression through musical composition.

title: Rhythm Patterns
description: Rhythm is the pattern of long and short sounds in music! Program your M5Stick to play different rhythm patterns using long and short notes!
story_text: Chip draws circles on his display. "In music, we write down rhythm using special symbols!" he says. "A filled-in circle with a stick is a QUARTER NOTE -- it gets one beat. TAH! A hollow circle with a stick is a HALF NOTE -- it gets TWO beats. TAH-AH! If I put them together: TAH TAH TAH-AH, that is a rhythm pattern! Let us read and tap rhythm patterns together!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_buzzer_tone" x="20" y="20"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">400</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">200</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_wait"><value name="SECONDS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">0.3</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Rhythm Patterns!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Long-Short-Short:", 10, 40)
for i in range(2):
    Speaker.tone(400, 400)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    Speaker.tone(400, 150)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    Speaker.tone(400, 150)
    time.sleep(0.2)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Short-Short-Long:", 10, 70)
for i in range(2):
    Speaker.tone(400, 150)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    Speaker.tone(400, 150)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    Speaker.tone(400, 400)
    time.sleep(0.5)

hints.text: Rhythm is the pattern of LONG and SHORT sounds. Think of it like this: LOOONG-short-short is one rhythm!
A long note has a bigger duration number (like 400ms). A short note is smaller (like 150ms). Try both!
Make the pattern: Long (400ms) - Short (150ms) - Short (150ms). Add small waits between.
Put it in a repeat loop (2 times) so the pattern plays twice. Thats a rhythm!
Now try the OPPOSITE pattern: Short-Short-Long. Does it sound different? Display the pattern name!

story.title: Reading Rhythms
story.narrative: Chip discovers that music can be written down! Learn to read and tap quarter notes and half notes.

create.title: My Rhythm
create.prompt: Create a 4-beat rhythm pattern using quarter notes and half notes. Write it and play it!

explore.title: Tap the Rhythm
explore.instructions: Display shows rhythm notation (quarter and half notes). Tap the button to play each rhythm. Buzzer confirms timing!
explore.device_interaction: Display shows rhythm patterns. Buttons tap rhythm. Buzzer confirms correct timing.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click to tap rhythms shown on screen.

celebrate.title: Rhythm Reader!
celebrate.summary: You can read and tap rhythm patterns with quarter notes and half notes! You are reading music!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child learned basic rhythm notation (quarter notes, half notes) and practiced reading/performing rhythms.

title: Music and Math
description: Music and math are best friends! Notes have mathematical relationships. Program your M5Stick to explore how doubling a frequency makes a note one octave higher!
story_text: Chip has a big idea. "What if I told you that MUSIC and MATH are secret best friends?" he says. "Number patterns like 1-2-3-1-2-3 are just like musical patterns like do-re-mi-do-re-mi! And counting beats is just... counting! Even the way notes split in half -- a half note is TWO quarter notes -- that is fractions! Let us turn math patterns into music and music patterns into math!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Music + Math!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">262</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">500</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Music + Math!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Low Do: 262", 10, 35)
Speaker.tone(262, 500)
time.sleep(0.6)
Lcd.drawString("262 x 2 = 524!", 10, 55)
Speaker.tone(524, 500)
time.sleep(0.6)
Lcd.drawString("Thats an OCTAVE!", 10, 75)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("Try La: 220", 10, 95)
Speaker.tone(220, 500)
time.sleep(0.6)
Lcd.drawString("220 x 2 = 440!", 10, 115)
Speaker.tone(440, 500)
time.sleep(0.6)

hints.text: Music is full of math! When you DOUBLE a frequency, the note sounds the same but HIGHER. This jump is called an octave.
Play Do at 262. Now play 262 x 2 = 524. They sound similar but one is higher -- thats one octave up!
Try another: La at 220. Double it to 440. Same note name, one octave higher! Math is everywhere in music.
Display the math equation: "262 x 2 = 524" so you can see the doubling pattern.
Half a frequency goes DOWN an octave: 440 / 2 = 220. Music uses multiplication and division!

story.title: Music Meets Math
story.narrative: Chip discovers that music and math are best friends! Patterns, counting, and fractions connect them.

create.title: Math Music Mashup
create.prompt: Pick a number pattern (like 1-3-5-1-3-5) and turn it into a melody. Does it sound good? Experiment!

explore.title: Pattern Translator
explore.instructions: Enter a number pattern and hear it as music. Enter a music pattern and see it as numbers. The display shows both side by side!
explore.device_interaction: Display shows number/music parallel. Buzzer plays musical version. Buttons input patterns.
explore.simulator_fallback: Enter patterns and see/hear the cross-subject connection.

celebrate.title: Cross-Subject Champion!
celebrate.summary: You discovered that music and math are connected through patterns, counting, and fractions!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child explored cross-subject connections between mathematical patterns and musical patterns.

title: Concert Time
description: Its concert time! Combine everything you learned -- pitch, tempo, dynamics, and rhythm -- to perform a complete song on your M5Stick. You are the composer!
story_text: Chip puts on a tiny bow tie. "Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the TinkerSchool Concert!" he announces. "You have learned notes, pitch, beat, patterns, tempo, dynamics, composition, and rhythm notation. Now it is time to perform! Compose a piece using everything you know, practice it, and then perform it for your family. Every great musician started with their first concert. This is YOURS!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">My Concert!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">262</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">300</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("My Concert!", 15, 10)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Mary Had a", 20, 35)
Lcd.drawString("Little Lamb", 15, 55)
melody = [(330,300),(294,300),(262,300),(294,300),(330,300),(330,300),(330,600),(294,300),(294,300),(294,600),(330,300),(392,300),(392,600)]
for freq, dur in melody:
    Speaker.tone(freq, dur)
    time.sleep(dur / 1000 + 0.1)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Bravo!", 40, 90)
Speaker.tone(262, 100)
time.sleep(0.1)
Speaker.tone(330, 100)
time.sleep(0.1)
Speaker.tone(392, 100)
time.sleep(0.1)
Speaker.tone(523, 400)

hints.text: A concert is where musicians perform a whole song! You will play Mary Had a Little Lamb. The notes are E-D-C-D-E-E-E.
Start with Mi (330), Re (294), Do (262), Re (294), Mi (330), Mi (330), Mi (330 but longer!). Thats the first line!
Second line: Re (294), Re (294), Re (294 longer), Mi (330), Sol (392), Sol (392 longer).
Make some notes longer by increasing duration to 600ms. Short notes are 300ms. This creates the rhythm!
Add a title screen and a Bravo ending with a quick scale going up! You just gave a concert!

story.title: The Grand Concert
story.narrative: Welcome to the TinkerSchool Concert! Compose, practice, and perform your music for an audience!

create.title: My Concert Piece
create.prompt: Compose a piece with at least 8 notes. Include dynamics (loud/soft) and a tempo. Give it a title and perform it!

explore.title: Concert Prep
explore.instructions: Chip creates a personalized music review. Then compose your concert piece using all your skills!
explore.device_interaction: Full device: buzzer plays compositions, display shows notation, IMU controls dynamics and pitch.
explore.simulator_fallback: Full interactive music creation and playback in the browser.

celebrate.title: Concert Star!
celebrate.summary: You performed your first concert! You are a real musician who can create, read, and perform music.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child completed the music curriculum with a composition and performance capstone.

title: Meet the Instrument Families!
description: Instruments come in families! Strings, winds, brass, and percussion all sound different. Program your M5Stick to play sounds that imitate each instrument family!
story_text: Hey there, music explorer! Chip here! Did you know that instruments live in families, just like people do? There are FOUR big instrument families, and each one makes sound in a different way. Let's meet them all!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Instruments!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">800</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Instruments!", 10, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Strings:", 10, 40)
Speaker.tone(440, 800)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.drawString("Percussion:", 10, 65)
Speaker.tone(150, 100)
time.sleep(0.2)
Speaker.tone(150, 100)
time.sleep(0.2)
Speaker.tone(150, 100)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.drawString("Winds:", 10, 90)
Speaker.tone(600, 600)
time.sleep(0.8)
Lcd.drawString("Brass:", 10, 115)
Speaker.tone(350, 500)
time.sleep(0.6)

hints.text: Instrument families group similar instruments! Strings vibrate strings, percussion you hit, winds you blow, brass you buzz into. Lets hear each one!
For strings (like violin), use a long smooth tone: frequency 440, duration 800ms. Strings make long flowing sounds!
For percussion (like drums), use short quick tones: frequency 150, duration 100ms. Add three in a row with tiny waits!
For winds (like flute), use a higher tone: frequency 600, duration 600ms. Wind instruments often sound light and airy!
For brass (like trumpet), try frequency 350, duration 500ms. Label each one on the display so you remember which is which!

cards.back: Strings make sound when you pluck, strum, or use a bow! Examples: violin, guitar, cello, harp
Woodwinds make sound when you blow air through them! Examples: flute, clarinet, saxophone, oboe
Brass instruments are shiny metal and you buzz your lips to play them! Examples: trumpet, trombone, tuba, French horn
Percussion instruments make sound when you hit, shake, or scrape them! Examples: drums, xylophone, tambourine, maracas

activities.prompt: Flip each card to learn about the instrument families!
Match each instrument to its family!

questions.hint: Think about instruments like the flute and clarinet -- what do you do to play them?
questions.prompt: Which instrument family makes sound by blowing air?

questions.hint: You shake a tambourine! Which family includes instruments you hit or shake?
questions.prompt: A tambourine belongs to which instrument family?

title: Musical Notes
description: Learn the musical scale! Notes go from low Do all the way up to high Do. Program your M5Stick to play all 8 notes of the C major scale!
story_text: Time to learn about musical notes! Notes tell musicians how LONG to play a sound. Some notes are long and slow, and some are short and quick. Let's discover them together!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Scale Time!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">purple</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">262</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">400</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF00FF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Musical Scale!", 10, 10)
scale = [(262,"Do"), (294,"Re"), (330,"Mi"), (349,"Fa"), (392,"Sol"), (440,"La"), (494,"Ti"), (523,"Do!")]
y = 30
for freq, name in scale:
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name + " " + str(freq), 10, y)
    Speaker.tone(freq, 400)
    time.sleep(0.5)
    y = y + 18

hints.text: A musical scale is like a staircase of sounds going UP! Do Re Mi Fa Sol La Ti Do -- each step is higher.
Start with Do at 262. Each note is a specific frequency: Re=294, Mi=330, Fa=349.
Keep going up: Sol=392, La=440, Ti=494, and high Do=523. Notice each number gets BIGGER!
Add a wait (0.5 seconds) between each note so you can hear them clearly one at a time.
Display each note name as it plays. When you run it, you will hear the scale going up like a staircase!

cards.back: A whole note lasts 4 beats. It looks like an empty circle. Hold the sound for a long time: 1-2-3-4!
A half note lasts 2 beats. It looks like an empty circle with a stick (stem). Hold for: 1-2!
A quarter note lasts 1 beat. It looks like a filled-in circle with a stem. One quick beat: 1!
An eighth note lasts half a beat. It looks like a quarter note with a flag on top. Super quick!

activities.prompt: Flip to learn how many beats each note gets!

questions.hint: A WHOLE note takes up a WHOLE measure -- that's 4 beats!
questions.prompt: How many beats does a whole note get?

questions.hint: It's called a HALF note because it's half of a whole note. Half of 4 is...
questions.prompt: How many beats does a half note get?

questions.hint: The eighth note has a little flag and it goes by super fast!
questions.prompt: Which note is the shortest?

questions.hint: A whole note = 4 beats. A quarter note = 1 beat. So 4 quarter notes = 1 whole note!
questions.prompt: How many quarter notes fit in the same time as one whole note?

questions.hint: The whole note has no stem. The half note is the one with an open circle AND a stem!
questions.prompt: Which note looks like an empty circle with a stem?

title: Rests and Silence
description: Silence is part of music too! A rest is when you stop playing for a moment. Program a melody with rests to hear how silence makes music more interesting!
story_text: Shh! Chip has a secret to tell you... silence is part of music too! Rests are like invisible notes -- they tell you when to be quiet. Without rests, music would be one big never-ending sound!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_buzzer_tone" x="20" y="20"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">300</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_wait"><value name="SECONDS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">1</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Rests & Silence!", 5, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Note, Note, REST!", 5, 35)
for i in range(3):
    Speaker.tone(440, 300)
    time.sleep(0.4)
    Speaker.tone(440, 300)
    time.sleep(0.4)
    Lcd.drawString("...", 60, 60)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    Lcd.fillRect(55, 55, 50, 20, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Music needs silence!", 5, 90)

hints.text: In music, silence is called a REST. Imagine a song with no pauses -- it would be like talking without breathing!
Play two notes (tone 440, 300ms each) with short waits between them.
Now add a LONGER wait of 0.8 seconds. That silence IS the rest -- a musical pause!
The pattern is: Note-Note-REST, Note-Note-REST. Put it in a repeat loop (3 times).
Display "..." during the rest to show silence. Music is made of sounds AND silences together!

cards.back: A whole rest means 4 beats of silence. It looks like a little hat hanging from a line. Shhh for 1-2-3-4!
A half rest means 2 beats of silence. It looks like a little hat sitting ON a line. Quiet for 1-2!
A quarter rest means 1 beat of silence. It looks like a zigzag squiggle. Quick pause!
An eighth rest means half a beat of silence. It looks like the number 7. A tiny pause!

activities.prompt: Learn about the different types of rests!
Match each rest to how many beats of silence it gets!

questions.hint: When you see a rest, you take a break from making sound!
questions.prompt: What does a rest in music tell you to do?

questions.hint: Just like notes, the WHOLE rest is the longest -- 4 beats of quiet!
questions.prompt: Which rest gives you the longest silence?

questions.hint: Imagine talking without ever pausing -- rests give music breathing room!
questions.prompt: Why are rests important in music?

options.text: They give music space to breathe and create rhythm
They mean the music is over

questions.hint: A quarter rest matches a quarter note -- just 1 beat!
questions.prompt: How many beats of silence does a quarter rest get?

title: Loud and Soft (Dynamics)
description: Learn the dynamic markings musicians use! From pianissimo (very soft) to fortissimo (very loud). Make your M5Stick demonstrate all the dynamic levels!
story_text: Have you ever whispered a secret? Or cheered really loud at a game? Music does that too! Musicians use special Italian words to say how loud or soft to play. Let's learn these cool words!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_text" x="20" y="20"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">pp - soft</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">30</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">white</field><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">440</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">100</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Dynamic Levels!", 5, 10)
levels = [("pp", 80), ("p", 150), ("mp", 250), ("mf", 350), ("f", 500), ("ff", 700)]
y = 30
for name, dur in levels:
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(name + " ", 10, y)
    Speaker.tone(440, dur)
    time.sleep(dur / 1000 + 0.2)
    y = y + 18
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("6 levels!", 30, 140)

hints.text: Musicians use letters for volume: pp (pianissimo/very soft), p (piano/soft), mp (mezzo-piano/medium-soft), mf (mezzo-forte/medium-loud), f (forte/loud), ff (fortissimo/very loud)!
Start with pp: play a very short tone (80ms). Thats the softest level!
Increase step by step: p (150ms), mp (250ms), mf (350ms), f (500ms), ff (700ms). Each one longer!
Display each dynamic marking as it plays so you learn the symbols.
Can you hear 6 different loudness levels? Real musicians practice controlling their volume just like this!

cards.back: Very very soft! Like a tiny mouse tiptoeing. Pianissimo means "very soft" in Italian.
Soft and gentle, like a whisper. Piano means "soft" in Italian. (Yes, the instrument is named after this!)
Medium soft -- like your normal talking voice but a little quieter. Mezzo means "medium."
Medium loud -- like your regular talking voice. This is the most common dynamic!
Loud and strong! Like cheering for your favorite team. Forte means "strong" in Italian.
Very very loud! Like thunder rumbling. Fortissimo means "very strong" in Italian.

activities.prompt: Flip to learn the dynamics from softest to loudest!

questions.hint: Start with the softest (pp = very very soft) and end with the loudest (ff = very very loud)!
questions.prompt: Put these dynamics in order from SOFTEST to LOUDEST!

questions.hint: Think about how loud each one is -- start with the quietest!
questions.prompt: Put these sounds in order from SOFTEST to LOUDEST!

questions.hint: Forte comes from Italian. Think of a fort -- big and strong!
questions.prompt: What does 'forte' mean in music?

questions.hint: The piano instrument was actually named because it could play soft AND loud!
questions.prompt: What does 'piano' mean as a dynamic marking?

questions.hint: Mezzo forte has 'forte' (loud) in it, so it's the louder one!
questions.prompt: Which dynamic is louder: mf or mp?

questions.hint: Pianissimo, forte, fortissimo -- these all come from the country shaped like a boot!
questions.prompt: What language do most musical terms come from?

title: Musical Patterns
description: Create your own musical pattern! Use three notes to make an ABC pattern, then rearrange them. You are a pattern composer!
story_text: Chip loves patterns! Clap-clap-snap, clap-clap-snap -- did you hear the pattern? Music is made of patterns too! Rhythms repeat, melodies repeat, and finding patterns is the secret to being a great musician!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_buzzer_tone" x="20" y="20"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">262</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">250</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_wait"><value name="SECONDS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">0.3</field></shadow></value><next><block type="m5_buzzer_tone"><value name="FREQUENCY"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">330</field></shadow></value><value name="DURATION"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">250</field></shadow></value></block></next></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Pattern Composer!", 2, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("ABC ABC:", 10, 35)
for i in range(2):
    Speaker.tone(262, 250)
    time.sleep(0.3)
    Speaker.tone(330, 250)
    time.sleep(0.3)
    Speaker.tone(392, 250)
    time.sleep(0.3)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("CBA CBA:", 10, 60)
for i in range(2):
    Speaker.tone(392, 250)
    time.sleep(0.3)
    Speaker.tone(330, 250)
    time.sleep(0.3)
    Speaker.tone(262, 250)
    time.sleep(0.3)
Lcd.drawString("Same notes,", 10, 90)
Lcd.drawString("different order!", 10, 110)

hints.text: With 3 notes (A, B, C) you can make many patterns! ABC goes up, CBA goes down. Lets try both!
Pick three notes: A=262 (Do), B=330 (Mi), C=392 (Sol). Play them in order: A, B, C.
Repeat the ABC pattern twice using a loop. That makes it feel like a real musical phrase!
Now reverse it: C, B, A. Same three notes but going DOWN instead of up!
Label both patterns on the display. ABC sounds like climbing up, CBA sounds like coming down!

questions.hint: This pattern goes: short, short, long! Two quick beats then one held beat.
questions.prompt: Put this rhythm pattern in the right order: Quarter, Quarter, Half!

questions.hint: Go from shortest to longest! Half + half + 1 + 2 = 4 beats total.
questions.prompt: Arrange these to make 4 beats: Eighth, Eighth, Quarter, Half

questions.hint: Eighth is the quickest, then quarter, then half, then whole is the longest!
questions.prompt: Order these note types from SHORTEST to LONGEST!

questions.hint: The whole note fills up a whole measure in 4/4 time!
questions.prompt: A whole note gets ___ beats.

questions.hint: Each half note = 2 beats. Two of them = 2 + 2 = 4 beats = 1 whole note!
questions.prompt: Two half notes equal ___ whole note(s).

questions.hint: 1 beat + 1 beat + ? = 4 beats. You need 2 more beats. Which note lasts 2 beats?
questions.prompt: A quarter note + a quarter note + a ___ note = 4 beats.

questions.hint: A whole note = 4 beats. What note equals 1 beat each?
questions.prompt: Four ___ notes fit in the same time as one whole note.

questions.hint: Remember from the dynamics lesson? Forte means loud or strong!
questions.prompt: The word 'forte' means ___ in music.

title: Fast or Slow? (Tempo)
description: Discover tempo -- the speed of music! Learn Italian words for fast, medium, and slow.
story_text: Hey music explorer! Chip just noticed something cool -- some songs make you want to dance super fast, and others make you want to sway slowly like a tree in the breeze. The SPEED of music has a special name: TEMPO! And musicians use fancy Italian words to describe it. Ready to learn them?

activities.hint: Presto is the fastest -- like a rocket! Adagio is the slowest -- like a turtle.
activities.prompt: Match each tempo word to its meaning! Can you learn these Italian words?

questions.hint: Tempo is all about SPEED!
questions.prompt: What does TEMPO mean in music?

options.text: How fast or slow the music is
How high or low the notes are

questions.hint: Think of a slow turtle -- adagio means slow and gentle.
questions.prompt: Which tempo word means SLOW?

questions.hint: Lullabies are slow and gentle. That's adagio!
questions.prompt: You're listening to a lullaby. What tempo is it?

questions.hint: Presto is the fastest tempo.
questions.prompt: Which tempo is the FASTEST of all?

questions.hint: Andante comes from the Italian word for walking!
questions.prompt: Andante means music moves at what kind of speed?

title: Loud and Soft (Dynamics)
description: Learn the musical words for loud and soft! Piano means soft and forte means loud.
story_text: Psst! Chip wants to tell you a secret... SURPRISE! Music can whisper softly or SHOUT with excitement! Musicians use special Italian words called DYNAMICS to say how loud or soft to play. Let's learn them!

questions.hint: In music dynamics, piano means SOFT!
questions.prompt: What does PIANO (p) mean in music dynamics?

questions.hint: Forte means strong and loud!
questions.prompt: What does FORTE (f) mean?

questions.hint: Whispering is very soft -- piano (p) means soft!
questions.prompt: A singer whispers very gently. Which dynamic marking fits?

questions.hint: Mezzo is Italian for medium!
questions.prompt: What does 'mezzo' mean in mezzo forte (mf)?

questions.hint: Fortissimo has two f's -- double the forte means EXTRA loud!
questions.prompt: Which dynamic marking means VERY VERY loud?

activities.hint: pp is the tiniest whisper, and ff is the biggest boom.
activities.prompt: Match each dynamics word to how loud it is!

title: Getting Louder, Getting Softer
description: Ride the dynamics roller coaster! Learn about crescendo and decrescendo.
story_text: Buckle up, music friend! Chip is taking you on a dynamics ROLLER COASTER! Sometimes music starts super quiet and gets louder and louder and LOUDER -- that's called a CRESCENDO! And sometimes it starts loud and gets softer and softer and softer -- that's a DECRESCENDO!

questions.hint: Start with the tiniest whisper (pp) and build up to the biggest boom (ff)!
questions.prompt: Put these dynamics in order from SOFTEST to LOUDEST!

questions.hint: Start with the quietest animal and end with the loudest!
questions.prompt: A crescendo goes from soft to loud! Put these in CRESCENDO order!

questions.hint: Start with the LOUDEST sound and end with the quietest!
questions.prompt: A decrescendo goes from loud to soft! Put these in DECRESCENDO order!

questions.hint: Crescendo means growing louder!
questions.prompt: What is a CRESCENDO?

questions.hint: Decrescendo is the opposite of crescendo!
questions.prompt: What is a DECRESCENDO?

questions.hint: Going from soft to loud is a crescendo!
questions.prompt: The music starts at pp and ends at ff. What happened?

questions.hint: The crescendo symbol starts small and opens wide!
questions.prompt: What does the crescendo symbol look like?

options.text: A long line that opens up like this: <

title: Musical Feelings
description: Discover how tempo and dynamics work together to create different moods and emotions in music!
story_text: Chip just had a big discovery! When you combine tempo (speed) and dynamics (volume) together, music can make you feel SO many different things! Let's explore how music makes us FEEL!

activities.hint: Think about how each combo would FEEL!
activities.prompt: Match each tempo + dynamics combo to the mood it creates!

questions.hint: Slow + loud feels dramatic!
questions.prompt: A scary movie scene has music that is slow and very loud. How does it make you feel?

questions.hint: Slow and soft for a lullaby!
questions.prompt: You want to write a lullaby. What tempo and dynamics should you use?

questions.hint: Fast and loud for superheroes!
questions.prompt: A superhero theme song is usually what tempo and dynamic?

questions.hint: Fast + soft = sneaky!
questions.prompt: A spy sneaks through a dark building. The music is fast but very quiet. Which mood?

questions.hint: Absolutely!
questions.prompt: Can the SAME song change moods by changing tempo and dynamics?

options.text: No, a song always stays the same mood
Yes! Changing speed and volume changes the feeling
Only if you change the notes
Only if you add more instruments

title: Perform with Expression
description: Put it all together! Learn to read performance instructions and conduct an orchestra with expression.
story_text: Chip is SO proud of you! You've learned about tempo AND dynamics -- now it's time to put them TOGETHER and perform with EXPRESSION! Today YOU are the conductor!

questions.hint: A dramatic ending builds up!
questions.prompt: Put these performance instructions in order for a dramatic ending!

questions.hint: A lullaby should gently fade away!
questions.prompt: Put these in order for a gentle lullaby that fades away!

questions.hint: A march builds energy!
questions.prompt: Order these steps for a march that gets the crowd pumped up!

questions.hint: It starts with F and means strong!
questions.prompt: The Italian word for LOUD in music is ___.

questions.hint: Think of the tempo word that sounds like a gentle sigh.
questions.prompt: The Italian word for SLOW in music is ___.

questions.hint: It starts with C and means growing!
questions.prompt: When music gets gradually LOUDER, it is called a ___.

questions.hint: It is the opposite of crescendo!
questions.prompt: When music gets gradually SOFTER, it is called a ___.

questions.hint: When you SHOW your feelings, you express yourself!
questions.prompt: Playing music with feeling and emotion is called playing with ___.

title: High and Low on the Staff
description: Learn how notes go up and down on the musical staff -- high notes live at the top and low notes live at the bottom!
story_text: Hey, music explorer! Chip just climbed a musical LADDER! Notes that are HIGH on the staff sound squeaky and bright, and notes that are LOW sound deep and rumbly. Let's figure out where all the notes live!

activities.prompt: Match each sound to whether it is HIGH or LOW!

questions.hint: Start with the deepest sound and go up!
questions.prompt: Put these notes in order from LOWEST to HIGHEST!
