    time.sleep(0.4)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("2 4 6 8!", 30, 155)

hints.text: Sort means put in order! 8, 2, 6, 4 becomes 2, 4, 6, 8 when sorted smallest to biggest.
Show the unsorted numbers in red first. They are all mixed up!
Draw sorted numbers as bars: small bars for small numbers, tall bars for big numbers.
Each bar gets taller from left to right -- you can SEE that it is sorted!
Tones go up too -- you can HEAR the sorting! Visual + audio makes it clear.

activities.hint: Think about what each item IS. A dog is an animal, a car is a vehicle...
activities.prompt: Match each thing to the group it belongs in!

title: Code the Robot
description: Tell a robot what to do! Give your M5Stick robot a sequence of commands: move forward (beep), turn (flash), stop (display). Think like a programmer!
story_text: Chip introduces a little robot on his display. "This is Bolt!" he says. "Bolt does EXACTLY what you tell him -- no more, no less. If you say FORWARD, he goes forward one step. If you say TURN RIGHT, he turns right. But you have to give the instructions in the right order! If you say turn right when he should go forward, he will go the wrong way. Let us program Bolt to reach the goal!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">GO!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">50</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">green</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Robot Commands!", 5, 10)
commands = [("FORWARD!", 0x00FF00, 400), ("FORWARD!", 0x00FF00, 400), ("TURN!", 0x0000FF, 600), ("FORWARD!", 0x00FF00, 400), ("STOP!", 0xFF0000, 200)]
for cmd, color, freq in commands:
    Lcd.fillRect(0, 40, 135, 50, 0x000000)
    Lcd.setTextColor(color, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString(cmd, 30, 55)
    Speaker.tone(freq, 200)
    if cmd == "TURN!":
        Power.setLed(255)
        time.sleep(0.3)
        Power.setLed(0)
    time.sleep(0.8)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("5 commands done!", 5, 100)

hints.text: A robot follows commands in order! FORWARD means go ahead, TURN means change direction, STOP means halt. Lets give commands!
Display "FORWARD!" in green with a beep. The robot moves ahead!
Then "TURN!" in blue with a different sound and LED flash. The robot turns!
More FORWARD commands, then "STOP!" in red. Each command is a step in the robots program.
Count the commands: 5 total! Programming a robot is just giving it the right commands in the right order!

story.title: Meet Bolt the Robot
story.narrative: Bolt the robot follows instructions exactly. Learn to give clear, step-by-step commands!

create.title: My Robot Challenge
create.prompt: Design a course for Bolt. Place the goal and obstacles. Then write the commands to guide him through!

explore.title: Command Bolt
explore.instructions: Press Button A for forward, Button B for turn right. Give Bolt a sequence of commands to reach the goal. The display shows him following each step!
explore.device_interaction: Display shows grid with robot. Buttons input commands. Robot follows them step by step.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click command buttons and watch the robot move on screen.

celebrate.title: Robot Commander!
celebrate.summary: You gave step-by-step instructions to a robot! This is exactly how real programmers think.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child practiced algorithmic thinking by programming a virtual robot with sequential commands.

title: Odd One Out
description: Which one does not belong? Display a group of shapes where one is different and highlight the odd one out! Train your observation skills!
story_text: Chip shows four things on his display: apple, banana, grapes, and a shoe. "One of these things is NOT like the others!" he says. "Apple, banana, and grapes are all FRUITS. But a shoe is NOT a fruit! The shoe is the ODD ONE OUT. To find it, you need to figure out what the others have in COMMON and find the one that does not fit. It is like being a detective for your brain!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">25</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">60</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">15</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">red</field><field name="FILL">TRUE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Odd One Out!", 15, 10)
Lcd.fillCircle(25, 55, 15, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillCircle(67, 55, 15, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillRect(95, 40, 30, 30, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillCircle(25, 110, 15, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Find the odd one!", 5, 80)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.drawCircle(95 + 15, 55, 20, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.drawCircle(95 + 15, 55, 21, 0xFFFF00)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)

Lcd.drawString("Others: circles", 10, 160)
Speaker.tone(523, 300)

hints.text: ODD ONE OUT means finding the thing that is DIFFERENT from all the others. One of these is not like the rest!
Draw 3 red circles and 1 red SQUARE. They are the same color but one SHAPE is different!
Display "Find the odd one!" and wait for the viewer to think.
Highlight the answer by drawing a yellow circle AROUND the square. Thats the odd one!
Explain WHY its different: the square is the only non-circle. Comparing and classifying is a key thinking skill!

story.title: The Misfit Finder
story.narrative: Chip learns to spot which item does not belong by finding what the others share!

create.title: My Odd One Out
create.prompt: Create your own Odd One Out puzzle. Pick 3 things that go together and 1 that does not. Can someone solve it?

explore.title: Find the Odd One
explore.instructions: Display shows 4 items. Press the button when the odd one is highlighted. Explain WHY it does not belong!
explore.device_interaction: Display shows groups of 4. Buttons select the odd item. Buzzer confirms correct answer.
explore.simulator_fallback: Click on the item that does not belong.

celebrate.title: Logic Star!
celebrate.summary: You can find the odd one out by looking for what things have in common. That is powerful logical reasoning!
celebrate.parent_note: Your child practiced categorical reasoning and logical exclusion, key analytical thinking skills.

title: Build a Solution
description: Build solutions step by step! Given a problem, design a solution using logical blocks. Like building with LEGO -- one piece at a time!
story_text: Chip presents a challenge: "How do we get a ball across a room without touching it?" he asks. "There is not just ONE answer! You could blow on it, use a fan, roll it down a ramp, kick it... There are MANY ways to solve a problem! The best problem solvers think of LOTS of ideas and then pick the best one. Let us practice creative problem solving where YOUR ideas matter!"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Solution!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:

Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Build Solution!", 5, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF0000, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Problem: Dark!", 5, 25)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Solution:", 5, 48)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)

Speaker.tone(350, 150)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("2. Press switch", 5, 82)
Speaker.tone(400, 150)
time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.drawString("3. Light on!", 5, 99)
Power.setLed(255)
Speaker.tone(523, 300)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Problem solved!", 5, 125)
Power.setLed(0)

hints.text: Every problem has a solution! The key is to THINK about steps before you start. Like building with blocks!
State the PROBLEM first: Its dark! We need light!
Plan the SOLUTION in steps: 1. Find the switch, 2. Press it, 3. Light comes on!
Make step 3 actually DO something -- turn on the LED! The solution WORKS!
Show "Problem solved!" Building solutions step by step is what engineers and programmers do every day!

story.title: The Idea Machine
story.narrative: Chip faces open-ended challenges. Learn that many problems have more than one solution!

create.title: My Invention
create.prompt: Think of a problem at home or school. Design 3 different solutions. Pick your favorite and explain why it is best!

explore.title: Creative Challenges
explore.instructions: Chip presents a challenge with multiple possible solutions. Think of ideas, try them, and see what works! There is no single right answer.
explore.device_interaction: Display shows challenge scenarios. Buttons select approaches. Multiple correct paths.
explore.simulator_fallback: Choose and try different solution approaches for each challenge.

celebrate.title: Solution Builder!
celebrate.summary: You are a creative problem solver who thinks of multiple solutions! Real engineers and inventors think just like you.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child practiced creative problem solving with open-ended challenges, building innovative design thinking skills.

title: Puzzle Quest
description: The ultimate puzzle challenge! Combine patterns, sorting, logic, and cause-effect to solve a multi-step puzzle on your M5Stick!
story_text: Chip puts on a thinking cap. "We have trained our brains SO much!" he says. "Pattern spotting, sequencing, sorting, cause and effect, decomposition, mazes, algorithms, logic, and creative problem solving! Now it is time for the Puzzle Quest -- the ultimate brain challenge. Chip will create personalized puzzles just for YOU. Every puzzle uses a different thinking skill. Ready to prove you are a master thinker?"
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Puzzle Quest!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Puzzle Quest!", 10, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Challenge 1:", 10, 25)
Lcd.drawString("2,4,6,?,10", 20, 40)
time.sleep(1.5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Answer: 8!", 30, 55)
Speaker.tone(400, 200)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Challenge 2:", 10, 75)
Lcd.drawString("Sort: 5,1,3", 20, 90)
time.sleep(1.5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Answer: 1,3,5!", 15, 105)
Speaker.tone(500, 200)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Challenge 3:", 10, 125)
Lcd.drawString("IF rain THEN ?", 10, 140)
time.sleep(1.5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Umbrella!", 30, 155)
Speaker.tone(600, 400)
Lcd.drawString("You win!", 30, 178)

hints.text: Puzzle Quest tests ALL your problem-solving skills! Patterns, sorting, and logic -- three challenges in one!
Challenge 1 is a NUMBER pattern: 2, 4, 6, ?, 10. The rule is +2, so the answer is 8!
Challenge 2 is SORTING: put 5, 1, 3 in order. Smallest to biggest: 1, 3, 5!
Challenge 3 is CAUSE AND EFFECT: IF it rains, THEN bring an umbrella!
Play victory sounds for each answer. You combined patterns, sorting, and logic. You are a Puzzle Champion!

story.title: The Grand Puzzle Quest
story.narrative: Chip has prepared the ultimate brain challenge testing all your problem-solving skills!

create.title: Puzzle Show and Tell
create.prompt: Create your own puzzle using any thinking skill and challenge someone to solve it!

explore.title: Quest Map
explore.instructions: Chip creates a personalized quest based on your thinking growth areas. Each puzzle station tests a different skill!
explore.device_interaction: Full device: display shows quest map, buzzer celebrates, IMU for interactive puzzles.
explore.simulator_fallback: Full interactive puzzle review in the browser.

celebrate.title: Puzzle Quest Champion!
celebrate.summary: You completed the Puzzle Quest! You are a master thinker who can solve any problem.
celebrate.parent_note: Your child completed a comprehensive review of all computational thinking and problem-solving skills.

title: Story Order!
description: Put story events in the right order! Read the mixed-up events and number them 1, 2, 3 to make the story make sense!
story_text: Chip wrote a story but the pages got mixed up! Put the events in order so the story makes sense.
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Fix Order!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Story Order!", 15, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF0000, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Wrong order:", 5, 25)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("C. Ate pizza!", 5, 40)
Lcd.drawString("A. Got hungry", 5, 55)
Lcd.drawString("B. Made pizza", 5, 70)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Right order:", 5, 95)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
steps = ["1. Got hungry", "2. Made pizza", "3. Ate pizza!"]
for i, step in enumerate(steps):
    Lcd.drawString(step, 5, 110 + i * 18)
    Speaker.tone(350 + i * 80, 150)
    time.sleep(0.5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Makes sense!", 15, 170)

hints.text: Stories need to be in ORDER or they dont make sense! You cant eat pizza before making it!
Show the events in WRONG order first. Eating comes before cooking? That is silly!
Wait, then show the CORRECT order: 1. Got hungry, 2. Made pizza, 3. Ate pizza!
Each correct step gets a higher-pitched sound, building up to the happy ending!
Putting events in order is called SEQUENCING. Its key for stories, science experiments, and code!

questions.hint: What do you do FIRST when you wake up?
questions.prompt: Put the morning routine in order!

questions.hint: First you plant the seed, then water it...
questions.prompt: Put the plant growing story in order!

title: Two-Step Word Problems
description: Some problems need TWO steps to solve! Display a word problem on your M5Stick, show step 1, then step 2, then the final answer!
story_text: Chip has some tricky puzzles for you today! These word problems need TWO steps to solve. That means you will do one math step, then use that answer to do a SECOND step. Ready to be a math detective? Let's go!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Problem:</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("2-Step Problem!", 5, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("You have 3 apples", 2, 25)
Lcd.drawString("Get 4 more, eat 2", 2, 42)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Step 1: 3+4=7", 10, 70)
Speaker.tone(400, 150)
time.sleep(1.5)
Lcd.drawString("Step 2: 7-2=5", 10, 92)
Speaker.tone(400, 150)
time.sleep(1.5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF0000, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Answer: 5 apples!", 5, 120)
Speaker.tone(523, 300)

hints.text: Some math problems need TWO steps! Like: I have 3 apples, get 4 more, eat 2. You need to ADD first, then SUBTRACT!
Display the problem first. Wait a moment so the reader can think about it.
Show Step 1: 3 + 4 = 7. Thats the first operation -- adding!
Show Step 2: 7 - 2 = 5. Take away the 2 you ate. Thats the second operation!
Show the final answer with a victory sound. Breaking big problems into small steps makes them easy!

questions.hint: Step 1: 5 + 3 = 8 apples. Step 2: 8 - 2 = 6 apples!
questions.prompt: Sam has 5 apples 🍎. He picks 3 more, then eats 2. How many does Sam have now?

questions.hint: Step 1: 10 - 4 = 6 stickers. Step 2: 6 + 3 = 9 stickers!
questions.prompt: Mia has 10 stickers ⭐. She gives 4 to her friend, then gets 3 new ones. How many stickers does Mia have?

questions.hint: Step 1: 8 - 3 = 5 birds. Step 2: 5 + 5 = 10 birds!
questions.prompt: There are 8 birds 🐦 on a fence. 3 fly away, then 5 more land. How many birds are on the fence?

questions.hint: Step 1: 7 + 6 = 13 crayons. Step 2: 13 - 3 = 10 crayons!
questions.prompt: Tom has 7 crayons 🖍️. He finds 6 more, then loses 3. How many crayons does Tom have?

questions.hint: Step 1: 12 - 5 = 7 cookies. Step 2: 7 + 2 = 9 cookies!
questions.prompt: A box has 12 cookies 🍪. Dad takes 5 out, then Mom puts 2 back in. How many cookies are in the box?

title: Guess and Check
description: Guess and Check is a problem-solving strategy! Make a guess, test it, try again. Build a number guessing game on your M5Stick!
story_text: Chip loves guessing games! Today we will play number detective. Chip will give you clues about two mystery numbers, and you will use GUESS AND CHECK to find them. Make a guess, see if it works, and adjust. Let's solve some mysteries!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Guess the #!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">20</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Guess & Check!", 10, 10)
secret = 7
guesses = [3, 5, 7]
y = 35
for g in guesses:
    Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
    Lcd.drawString("Try: " + str(g), 10, y)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    if g < secret:
        Lcd.drawString("Too low!", 70, y)
        Speaker.tone(300, 200)
    elif g > secret:
        Lcd.drawString("Too high!", 70, y)
        Speaker.tone(300, 200)
    else:
        Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
        Lcd.drawString("YES!", 70, y)
        Speaker.tone(523, 400)
    time.sleep(0.8)
    y = y + 25
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Guess & Check!", 10, y + 10)

hints.text: GUESS AND CHECK: pick a number, test it, learn from the result, try again! Its a strategy for solving problems.
Set up a secret number (like 7). Display guesses one at a time.
For each guess, check: is it too low, too high, or correct? Use if blocks to decide!
Too low gets a low sound, too high gets a high sound, correct gets a victory sound!
Show all guesses on screen: Try 3 = too low, Try 5 = too low, Try 7 = YES! Thats guess and check!

questions.hint: Try guessing! If one number is 3, the other is 7. Is 7 - 3 = 2? No, that is 4 apart. Try 4 and 6!
questions.prompt: I am thinking of two numbers that add up to 10. One number is 2 more than the other. What are they? The smaller number is ___.

questions.hint: What number plus itself equals 8? Try 4 + 4!
questions.prompt: Two numbers add up to 8. They are the SAME number. What is each number? ___

questions.hint: Try guessing! Is it 6? 6 + 5 = 11 -- too small. Try 7! 7 + 5 = 12!
questions.prompt: I am thinking of a number. If I add 5 to it, I get 12. What is my number? ___

questions.hint: 12 minus 5 equals... guess and check!
questions.prompt: Two numbers add up to 12. One part is 5. What is the other part?

questions.hint: 15 minus 9 equals... think about what you need to add to 9 to get 15!
questions.prompt: Two numbers add up to 15. One part is 9. What is the other part?

questions.hint: 20 minus 8 equals... you can count up from 8 to 20!
questions.prompt: Find the missing part! The whole is 20.

title: Working Backward
description: Sometimes you start with the answer and work BACKWARD to find the start! Display a math puzzle that works backward step by step!
story_text: Sometimes you know the END of a story but not the BEGINNING! Chip calls this "working backward" -- like rewinding a movie. If you know where you ended up, you can figure out where you started. Let's practice!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Work Backward!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Work Backward!", 5, 10)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("? + 3 = 8", 25, 35)
Lcd.drawString("What is ?", 30, 55)
time.sleep(2)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Go backward:", 10, 80)
Lcd.drawString("8 - 3 = 5!", 25, 100)
Speaker.tone(523, 300)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Check: 5+3=8", 15, 125)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Correct!", 35, 150)
Speaker.tone(600, 300)

hints.text: WORKING BACKWARD means starting from the ANSWER and going step by step back to the START. Like a detective!
Show the puzzle: ? + 3 = 8. We know the answer (8) but not the start (?).
To work backward from 8: subtract 3. Show "8 - 3 = 5!" -- we found the missing number!
Always CHECK your answer! 5 + 3 = 8. Yes, it works! Display the check step.
Working backward is used in math, mystery solving, and debugging code! Start from what you KNOW.

questions.hint: Start from the end (10) and undo each step! Subtract instead of add.
questions.prompt: A number puzzle! Put the steps in REVERSE order to find the starting number.

questions.hint: If you are un-making a cake, start with the last step and go backward!
questions.prompt: Put these BACKWARD to undo the recipe! 🍳

questions.hint: Work backward! You ended with 11 and added 4. So 11 - 4 = 7. You started with 7!
questions.prompt: I started with some marbles. I found 4 more and now I have 11. How many did I START with?

questions.hint: You ended with 5 after giving away 3. Undo it: 5 + 3 = 8. You started with 8!
questions.prompt: I had some pencils. I gave away 3 and now I have 5. How many did I START with? ✏️

questions.hint: Work backward from 14! You added 6, so undo it: 14 - 6 = 8.
questions.prompt: I had some books. I bought 6 more and now I have 14. How many did I START with? 📚

questions.hint: Work backward! Undo finding 2: 9 - 2 = 7. Undo spending 5: 7 + 5 = 12!
questions.prompt: I had some coins. I spent 5 and found 2. Now I have 9. How many did I START with? 🪙

title: Compare with Venn
description: A Venn diagram shows what is the SAME and DIFFERENT between two things! Draw overlapping circles and sort items into the right sections!
story_text: Chip learned about a cool tool called a Venn diagram! It uses two overlapping circles to sort things. Items that belong to BOTH groups go in the middle where the circles overlap. Let's try sorting!
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_circle"><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">45</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">80</field></shadow></value><value name="RADIUS"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">35</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">red</field><field name="FILL">FALSE</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Venn Diagram!", 10, 5)
Lcd.drawCircle(45, 80, 35, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.drawCircle(90, 80, 35, 0x0000FF)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFF0000, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Cat", 20, 55)
Lcd.drawString("Meow", 18, 70)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("4", 60, 65)
Lcd.drawString("legs", 57, 80)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x0000FF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Dog", 85, 55)
Lcd.drawString("Bark", 83, 70)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Both have 4 legs!", 2, 130)

hints.text: A VENN DIAGRAM uses two overlapping circles. What is ONLY in circle 1? ONLY in circle 2? BOTH (in the overlap)?
Draw two circle outlines that overlap in the middle. Red for cats, blue for dogs.
In the RED only area, write what only cats do: Meow! In the BLUE only: Bark!
In the OVERLAP (middle), write what BOTH have: 4 legs! Thats what they have in common.
Venn diagrams help you COMPARE things. Scientists and thinkers use them to organize ideas!

activities.prompt: Sort these animals! Match each animal to where it lives. 🐾

questions.hint: A cat has fur AND 4 legs, so it goes in the overlapping part!
questions.prompt: In a Venn diagram of "Has Fur" and "Has 4 Legs," where does a cat go? 🐱

questions.hint: A snake has no fur and no legs! It goes outside both circles.
questions.prompt: In a Venn diagram of "Has Fur" and "Has 4 Legs," where does a snake go? 🐍

questions.hint: What do apples and bananas have in common? They are both fruits!
questions.prompt: Apples and bananas are both ___.

questions.hint: Ducks can fly AND swim! They go in the overlapping part.
questions.prompt: In a Venn diagram of "Can Fly" and "Can Swim," where does a duck go? 🦆

title: Logic Puzzles
description: Use logic to solve puzzles! IF clue 1 says "not red" and clue 2 says "not blue", THEN the answer must be green! Show logical deduction!
story_text: Chip's favorite thing is a good brain teaser! Logic puzzles are like detective work -- you use CLUES to figure out the answer. Sometimes you have to think about what MUST be true. Ready to give your brain a workout?
starter_blocks_xml: <xml xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml"><block type="m5_display_clear" x="20" y="20"><field name="COLOR">black</field><next><block type="m5_display_text"><value name="TEXT"><shadow type="text"><field name="TEXT">Logic Puzzle!</field></shadow></value><value name="X"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">5</field></shadow></value><value name="Y"><shadow type="math_number"><field name="NUM">10</field></shadow></value><field name="COLOR">yellow</field></block></next></block></xml>
solution_code:
Lcd.fillScreen(0x000000)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Logic Puzzle!", 10, 5)
Lcd.setTextColor(0xFFFFFF, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("3 colors:", 5, 25)
Lcd.fillCircle(30, 48, 10, 0xFF0000)
Lcd.fillCircle(67, 48, 10, 0x00FF00)
Lcd.fillCircle(105, 48, 10, 0x0000FF)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.drawString("Clue 1: NOT red", 5, 68)
Lcd.drawString("X", 25, 38)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.drawString("Clue 2: NOT blue", 5, 85)
Lcd.drawString("X", 100, 38)
time.sleep(1)
Lcd.setTextColor(0x00FF00, 0x000000)
Lcd.drawString("Answer: GREEN!", 10, 110)
Lcd.drawCircle(67, 48, 14, 0xFFFF00)
Speaker.tone(523, 400)
Lcd.drawString("Logic wins!", 20, 135)

hints.text: Logic puzzles give CLUES to find the answer. We eliminate wrong answers until only the RIGHT one is left!
Show 3 colored circles: red, green, blue. One of these is the answer!
Clue 1 says NOT red -- cross it out! That leaves green and blue.
Clue 2 says NOT blue -- cross it out! Only GREEN is left. Thats the answer!
Circle the answer in yellow. Logic means using clues to rule out wrong answers. Its like being a detective!

questions.hint: ALL dogs have tails. Buddy IS a dog. So Buddy MUST have a tail!
questions.prompt: All dogs have tails. Buddy is a dog. Does Buddy have a tail? 🐶

questions.hint: Sara > Ben > Lily. Lily is shorter than both Ben and Sara!
questions.prompt: Sara is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Lily. Who is the SHORTEST? 📏

questions.hint: Cross out the ones that do NOT have the toy. Not red, not green... only blue is left!
questions.prompt: There are 3 boxes: red, blue, and green. The toy is NOT in the red box. The toy is NOT in the green box. Where is the toy? 🎁

questions.hint: Every cat in the room is orange. Whiskers is a cat in the room. So Whiskers is orange!
questions.prompt: Every cat in this room is orange. Whiskers is in this room. Whiskers is a cat. What color is Whiskers? 🐱

questions.hint: Monday > Tuesday > Wednesday. Monday is when Chip ate the most!
questions.prompt: On Monday, Chip ate more than Tuesday. On Wednesday, Chip ate less than Tuesday. Which day did Chip eat the MOST? 😋

questions.hint: The rule says: raining = umbrella. It is raining. So Chip brings an umbrella!
questions.prompt: If it is raining, Chip brings an umbrella. It IS raining today. What does Chip bring? ☔

title: If-Then Clue Puzzles
description: Use clues to crack the case! If something is true, then what must follow?
story_text: Detective Chip reporting for duty! Today we are solving If-Then clue puzzles. Here is how it works: I give you a RULE and a CLUE, and you figure out the answer. For example, IF it is sunny THEN we wear sunglasses. It IS sunny today -- so what do we wear? Sunglasses! Let's crack some cases!

questions.hint: The rule says: feathers = bird. A robin has feathers, so a robin is a bird!
questions.prompt: Rule: IF an animal has feathers, THEN it is a bird. A robin has feathers. What is a robin?

questions.hint: The IF-THEN rule says veggies = dessert. Sam ate veggies, so Sam gets dessert!
questions.prompt: Rule: IF you eat all your veggies, THEN you get dessert. Sam ate all his veggies. What happens? 🥦🍰

questions.hint: Triangle = 3 sides. That is the rule!
questions.prompt: Rule: IF it is a triangle, THEN it has 3 sides. This shape is a triangle. How many sides does it have? 🔺

questions.hint: It is NOT a cat. It CAN fly. Dogs and fish cannot fly. A parrot can fly!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: The mystery pet is NOT a cat. Clue 2: The mystery pet can fly. What is the mystery pet? 🔎

questions.hint: Round + edible + orange colored. A basketball is not edible. A banana is not round. It is an orange!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: It is round. Clue 2: You can eat it. Clue 3: It is orange. What is it? 🤔

questions.hint: Look at the last digit of 16. What number is it?
questions.prompt: Rule: IF a number is even, THEN it ends in 0, 2, 4, 6, or 8. The number 16 is even. It ends in ___.

questions.hint: 4 equal sides and 4 corners. It is not a rectangle because all sides are EQUAL.
questions.prompt: Clue: Chip is thinking of a shape with 4 equal sides and 4 corners. It is a ___.

questions.hint: E is in the list A, E, I, O, U. So E is a...
questions.prompt: Rule: IF it is a vowel, THEN it is A, E, I, O, or U. The letter E is a ___.

questions.hint: 8 legs + webs. What creepy-crawly makes webs?
questions.prompt: Clue 1: I have 8 legs. Clue 2: I spin webs. I am a ___.

title: True or False Detectives
description: Put on your detective hat! Decide if each statement is true or false.
story_text: Chip loves playing True or False! Some statements are TRUE -- they are correct facts. Other statements are FALSE -- they are wrong or silly. Your job is to be a detective and figure out which is which. Think carefully before you decide!

questions.hint: A square always has 4 equal sides. That is what makes it a square!
questions.prompt: True or False: All squares have 4 sides. ⬛

questions.hint: Fish breathe underwater using gills. They cannot breathe on land!
questions.prompt: True or False: Fish can breathe on land. 🐟

questions.hint: Count it out: 5 + 3 = 8, not 9. This statement is false!
questions.prompt: True or False: 5 + 3 = 9 🧠

questions.hint: The Sun is the closest star to Earth. It is a giant ball of hot gas!
questions.prompt: True or False: The Sun is a star. ⭐

questions.hint: A circle is perfectly round with no corners at all!
questions.prompt: True or False: A circle has corners. ⭕

questions.hint: When water gets very cold it freezes and becomes ice. True!
questions.prompt: True or False: Ice is frozen water. 🧊

activities.prompt: Match each statement to whether it is TRUE or FALSE! 🔎

title: What Doesn't Belong?
description: Spot the odd one out! Find the item that breaks the pattern.
story_text: Chip is playing a sorting game! In each group, most items share something in common -- but ONE item does NOT belong. Your job is to find the sneaky item that does not fit. Look at size, shape, color, or what things DO. Ready to spot the odd one out?

questions.hint: Three of these are fruits that you can eat. A car is NOT a fruit!
questions.prompt: Which one does NOT belong? 🍎 🍌 🍓 🚗

questions.hint: 2, 4, and 8 are all EVEN numbers. 7 is the only ODD number!
questions.prompt: Which one does NOT belong? 2, 4, 7, 8 🔢

questions.hint: Dogs, cats, and hamsters all have fur and live on land. A fish lives in water!
questions.prompt: Which one does NOT belong? 🐶 🐱 🐠 🐹

questions.hint: Triangle, square, and circle are basic shapes. A star has points -- it is a special shape!
questions.prompt: Which one does NOT belong? ▶️ ■️ ⭐ ●

questions.hint: Red, Blue, and Green are all colors. Happy is a feeling, not a color!
questions.prompt: Which one does NOT belong? Red, Blue, Happy, Green 🎨

activities.prompt: Match each group to the item that does NOT belong in it! 🔍

title: Mystery Number
description: Use clues to figure out the secret number! Think like a detective.
story_text: Chip has locked a secret number inside a puzzle box! To open it, you need to read the clues and figure out what the number is. Each clue narrows it down. By the end, only ONE number fits all the clues. Can you crack the code?

questions.hint: Even numbers between 1 and 10: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10. Greater than 6: 8 and 10. Between 1 and 10 means not 10. It is 8!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: I am between 1 and 10. Clue 2: I am even. Clue 3: I am greater than 6. What am I? ___

questions.hint: Odd numbers under 20 where digits add to 8: 1+7=8. The answer is 17!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: I am less than 20. Clue 2: I am odd. Clue 3: My digits add up to 8. What am I? ___

questions.hint: Single digit, odd, between 4 and 8: that gives us 5 and 7. Try 5!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: I am a single digit. Clue 2: I am odd. Clue 3: I am greater than 4 and less than 8. What am I? ___

questions.hint: Two-digit numbers with same digits under 30: 11 and 22. Try 11!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: I am a two-digit number. Clue 2: Both my digits are the same. Clue 3: I am less than 30. What am I? ___

questions.hint: Between 10 and 20, even, AND in the count-by-5s list (5, 10, 15, 20). Even ones: 10 and 20!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: I am between 10 and 20. Clue 2: I am even. Clue 3: You say me when you count by 5s. What am I? ___

questions.hint: 4 legs + meow + pet = cat!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: I have 4 legs. Clue 2: I say 'meow.' Clue 3: I am a pet. What am I? 🔎

questions.hint: More than 5 cents, less than 25 cents. That is 10 cents -- a dime!
questions.prompt: I am a coin. I am worth more than a nickel but less than a quarter. What coin am I? 🪙

questions.hint: Starts with T and comes after Monday. Monday, then... Tuesday!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: I am a day of the week. Clue 2: I start with the letter T. Clue 3: I come right after Monday. What day am I? 📅

questions.hint: No corners and looks like a ball. That is a circle!
questions.prompt: Clue 1: I am a shape. Clue 2: I have no corners. Clue 3: I look like a ball from the front. What am I? 💬

title: Matrix Puzzles
description: Complete the grid! Figure out the rule for each row and column to fill in the missing piece.
story_text: Chip found a mysterious puzzle grid! It is like a mini treasure map with rows and columns. Each row follows a RULE, and each column follows a RULE too. Your job is to find the pattern and fill in the missing piece. Think about what changes across each row and what changes down each column!

questions.hint: Row 2 starts at 3 and goes up by 1. So 3, 4! Or: column 2 is 2, and 2+2=4!
questions.prompt: Grid puzzle! Row 1: 1, 2. Row 2: 3, ___. Rule: each row goes up by 1, each column goes up by 2.

questions.hint: Row 1 goes 1 star, 2 stars. Row 2 goes 3 stars, then? Each row adds 1 more star. 3+1=4!
questions.prompt: Grid puzzle! Row 1: ⭐, ⭐⭐. Row 2: ⭐⭐⭐, ___. How many stars go in the blank? ___

questions.hint: Row 2 starts at 6, goes up by 2: 6, 8! Or: column 2 is 4, plus 4 = 8!
questions.prompt: Grid puzzle! Row 1: 2, 4. Row 2: 6, ___. Rule: rows go up by 2, columns go up by 4.

questions.hint: Row 2: 15 + 5 = 20. Or column 2: 10 + 10 = 20. Both rules give 20!
questions.prompt: Grid puzzle! Row 1: 5, 10. Row 2: 15, ___. Rule: rows go up by 5, columns go up by 10.

questions.hint: A, B, C, ___. The alphabet goes A B C D! The missing letter is D.
questions.prompt: Grid puzzle! Row 1: A, B. Row 2: C, ___. The letters go in order! What letter fills the blank?

activities.prompt: Match each grid rule to its missing piece! 🧩

title: Growing Patterns
description: Find the rule in growing patterns! Numbers that get bigger each time follow a secret rule.
story_text: Hey superstar! Chip found something amazing -- numbers that GROW! Some patterns get bigger and bigger by following a secret rule. Like 1, 3, 5, 7... see how each number is 2 more than the last? Let's crack the code of growing patterns!

questions.hint: Start with the smallest number and add 2 each time: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9!
questions.prompt: Put these numbers in order to make a growing pattern that adds 2 each time!

questions.hint: Count by 5s! 5, 10, 15, 20. Each number is 5 more than the last.
questions.prompt: Arrange these numbers to show a pattern that adds 5 each time!

questions.hint: 7 plus 2 equals...
questions.prompt: 1, 3, 5, 7, ___. What comes next? (Adding 2 each time)

questions.hint: 8 plus 2 equals...
questions.prompt: 2, 4, 6, 8, ___. What comes next? (Adding 2 each time)

questions.hint: 30 plus 10 equals...
questions.prompt: 10, 20, 30, ___. What comes next? (Adding 10 each time)

questions.hint: 9 plus 3 equals...
questions.prompt: 3, 6, 9, ___. What comes next? (Adding 3 each time)

title: Complex Patterns
description: Decode tricky repeating patterns like ABC and AABB! Multiple elements repeat together.
story_text: Chip noticed something cool -- some patterns use MORE than two things! Like clap-snap-stomp, clap-snap-stomp. That is an ABC pattern! And some go clap-clap-snap-snap. That is an AABB pattern! Let's become pattern masters!

questions.hint: The pattern is A-B-C: circle, star, heart. After heart, the pattern repeats: heart, circle, star!
questions.prompt: Put these in order to continue the ABC pattern: circle, star, heart, circle, star, ___

questions.hint: AABB means two of the same, then two of the other. After red-red comes blue-blue, then red-red again!
questions.prompt: Continue the AABB pattern: red, red, blue, blue, red, red, ___

questions.hint: Three different things repeat: Sun(A), Moon(B), Star(C). That is an ABC pattern!
questions.prompt: What type of pattern is this? Sun, Moon, Star, Sun, Moon, Star

questions.hint: Two claps then two stomps -- that is two A's then two B's: AABB!
questions.prompt: What type of pattern is this? Clap, Clap, Stomp, Stomp, Clap, Clap, Stomp, Stomp

questions.hint: The pattern is AAB: triangle, triangle, circle. It starts over with triangle!
questions.prompt: What comes next? Triangle, Triangle, Circle, Triangle, Triangle, Circle, ___

questions.hint: ABBA means: first thing, second thing, second thing, first thing. Like a mirror!
questions.prompt: Which group of shapes follows an ABBA pattern?

title: Reading Picture Graphs
description: Learn to read picture graphs and answer questions about the data! Each picture stands for one thing.
story_text: Chip made a picture graph of favorite fruits in class! A picture graph uses little pictures to show information. Each picture means one vote. By counting the pictures, you can answer all kinds of questions. Let's read some picture graphs!

questions.hint: Count the pictures: Apple has 5, which is the most!
questions.prompt: Favorite Fruits: Apple has 5 pictures, Banana has 3 pictures, Grape has 4 pictures. Which fruit got the MOST votes?

questions.hint: Banana has only 3 pictures -- that is the fewest!
questions.prompt: Favorite Fruits: Apple has 5 pictures, Banana has 3 pictures, Grape has 4 pictures. Which fruit got the FEWEST votes?

questions.hint: Dog has 6 and Fish has 2. The difference is 6 minus 2 = 4!
questions.prompt: Favorite Pets: Dog has 6 pictures, Cat has 4 pictures, Fish has 2 pictures. How many MORE kids chose Dog than Fish?

questions.hint: Add them all up: 3 + 5 + 3 = 11 kids voted!
questions.prompt: Favorite Colors: Red has 3 pictures, Blue has 5 pictures, Green has 3 pictures. How many kids voted in total?

questions.hint: Add all the votes: 4 + 7 + 3 + 2 = ...
questions.prompt: Favorite Seasons: Spring has 4 votes, Summer has 7 votes, Fall has 3 votes, Winter has 2 votes. How many votes total? ___

questions.hint: Summer has 7 and Winter has 2. The difference is 7 minus 2 = ...
questions.prompt: Favorite Seasons: Spring has 4, Summer has 7, Fall has 3, Winter has 2. How many MORE votes did Summer get than Winter? ___

questions.hint: Cookies has 8 and Chips has 5. The difference is 8 minus 5 = ...
questions.prompt: Favorite Snacks: Chips has 5 votes, Cookies has 8 votes, Fruit has 6 votes. How many FEWER votes did Chips get than Cookies? ___

questions.hint: Fruit has 6 votes now. 6 plus 2 more = ...
questions.prompt: Favorite Snacks: Chips has 5, Cookies has 8, Fruit has 6. If 2 more kids voted for Fruit, how many votes would Fruit have? ___

title: Collecting Data
description: Learn how to gather and organize information! Ask questions, collect answers, and sort them into groups.
story_text: Chip wants to know what everyone's favorite ice cream flavor is! To find out, we need to COLLECT DATA. That means asking a question, writing down the answers, and organizing them. Let's learn how to be awesome data collectors!

questions.hint: A good survey question is clear and specific. Asking about a favorite sport gives a clear answer!
questions.prompt: Which is a good survey question to collect data about your classmates?

questions.hint: A picture graph or tally chart organizes the data so everyone can see it clearly!
questions.prompt: Chip asked 10 friends their favorite color. 4 said blue, 3 said red, 2 said green, 1 said yellow. What is the best way to show this data?

options.text: Write a long story about it
Make a picture graph or tally chart
Just remember it in your head
Draw a picture of a rainbow

questions.hint: IIII with a cross line = 5, plus II = 2 more. 5 + 2 = 7!
questions.prompt: Tally marks: IIII means 4, IIII with a line through means 5. How many does IIII II mean?

questions.hint: First you need to COLLECT the data by asking the question! Then you can organize it.
questions.prompt: You want to find out what game your class likes best. What should you do FIRST?

options.text: Ask everyone which game they like best

right.text: Write down what each person says
Use tally marks or a chart
Which animal got the most votes?

activities.prompt: Match each data collection step to what you do!

title: Data Detectives
description: Draw conclusions from data! Look at the numbers and figure out what they are telling you.
story_text: You are now a Data Detective! Chip has been collecting all kinds of data, and now it is YOUR job to figure out what the data is telling us. Look at the numbers, find patterns, and make smart conclusions. Let's solve some data mysteries!

questions.hint: 3, 5, 7, 9 -- each day has 2 MORE birds than the day before!
questions.prompt: Chip counted birds at the park: Monday 3, Tuesday 5, Wednesday 7, Thursday 9. What pattern do you see?

questions.hint: Sandwiches had 12 votes, which is the most. Most kids prefer sandwiches!
questions.prompt: Lunchbox data: 12 kids brought sandwiches, 5 brought salad, 8 brought pasta. What can you conclude?

questions.hint: The number of sunny days is going UP each month: 10, 12, 15. The pattern suggests April will have about the same or more!
questions.prompt: Chip tracked sunny days: January had 10, February had 12, March had 15. What will probably happen in April?

options.text: About the same or more sunny days

questions.hint: Kickball (9) and Tag (7) together are 16 out of 20 total votes. That is most kids!
questions.prompt: Recess votes: Kickball got 9, Tag got 7, Jump Rope got 4. Is it reasonable to say that most kids prefer Kickball or Tag?

options.text: Yes, 9 + 7 = 16 out of 20 kids chose those two
No, Jump Rope is the most popular
No, everyone likes the same game
We cannot tell from this data

questions.hint: The plant grows 2 inches each week: 2, 4, 6... so Week 4 should be 6 + 2 = 8 inches!
questions.prompt: Plant height data: Week 1 it was 2 inches, Week 2 it was 4 inches, Week 3 it was 6 inches. How tall will it probably be in Week 4?

questions.hint: The pattern adds 2 each month: 3, 5, 7... so next is 7 + 2 = ...
questions.prompt: Book data: Chip read 3 books in January, 5 in February, and 7 in March. If the pattern continues, how many books will Chip read in April? ___

questions.hint: Add them up: 2 + 4 + 1 = ...
questions.prompt: Weather data: It rained 2 days in Week 1, 4 days in Week 2, and 1 day in Week 3. How many rainy days total? ___

questions.hint: 8 plus 2 more equals...
questions.prompt: Sticker chart: Emma earned 4 stickers on Monday, 6 on Tuesday, and 8 on Wednesday. She earns 2 more each day. How many on Thursday? ___

questions.hint: 15 total minus 6 minus 4 = ...
questions.prompt: Snack survey: 15 kids were asked their favorite snack. Crackers got 6 votes and Fruit got 4 votes. How many votes did the OTHER snacks get altogether? ___

title: Break It Down
description: Decompose a big task into small steps. Learn the secret superpower of breaking problems into pieces!
story_text: Hey there, problem solver! Chip here! Did you know that even the BIGGEST problems can be solved if you break them into tiny pieces? Imagine trying to eat a whole pizza in one bite -- that's impossible! But one slice at a time? Easy! Today we're going to learn how to take BIG tasks and break them into small, simple steps. This is called DECOMPOSITION, and it's a superpower that real engineers and scientists use every day!

items.text: Spread peanut butter on one slice
Spread jelly on the other slice

activities.prompt: Put the steps for making a peanut butter and jelly sandwich in the right order!

right.text: Pick up toys off the floor
Dig a hole in the soil
Wake up and get out of bed

activities.prompt: Match each big task to its FIRST small step!

items.text: Draw a big square for the walls
Add a triangle roof on top
Draw a door in the middle

activities.prompt: You want to draw a picture of a house. Put the steps in order!

questions.hint: Think about cutting a pizza into slices -- you break the big thing into smaller pieces!
questions.prompt: What does it mean to DECOMPOSE a problem?

options.text: Break it into smaller, easier steps
Ask someone else to do it
Do it all at once really fast

questions.hint: Small steps are much easier than one giant leap!
questions.prompt: Why is breaking a big task into steps helpful?

options.text: It makes the task take longer
Each small step is easier to do
You don't have to finish it

questions.hint: Even rockets are built one piece at a time! Break it down into parts.
questions.prompt: A scientist needs to build a rocket. What should they do FIRST?

options.text: Build the whole rocket in one day
Give up because it's too hard
Break the project into smaller parts like engine, body, and nose cone

title: Is That Reasonable?
description: Estimate and check if answers make sense. Learn to spot silly answers and find ones that are just right!
story_text: Chip has a riddle for you! If someone told you there are 500 kids in your classroom, would that sound RIGHT? Of course not -- that's way too many! Being a great problem solver means checking if your answer MAKES SENSE. Today we're going to practice estimating -- that means making smart guesses -- and figuring out if answers are reasonable. Let's become answer detectives!

questions.hint: Count on both hands! 5 on one hand plus 5 on the other.
questions.prompt: About how many fingers does a person have?

questions.hint: Most dogs weigh between 10 and 100 pounds. 1,000 pounds is way too heavy -- that's like a small car!
questions.prompt: Sam says his dog weighs 1,000 pounds. Is that reasonable?

options.text: No way! That's heavier than a horse!
Maybe if it's a really big dog

questions.hint: Dentists say you should brush for about 2 minutes -- not too short and not too long!
questions.prompt: About how long does it take to brush your teeth?

questions.hint: 3 plus 4 is 7. The answer 34 is WAY too big -- something went wrong!
questions.prompt: Lily counted 3 apples in one bag and 4 apples in another bag. She says she has 34 apples total. Is that reasonable?

questions.hint: Think about how tall you are -- you're not as tall as a grown-up yet!
questions.template: A 2nd grader is about ___ feet tall.

questions.hint: Think about your own class. Is it closer to 5 kids or 500 kids?
questions.template: There are about ___ kids in a classroom.

questions.hint: Start with 5 and take away 2. Count what's left!
questions.template: If you have 5 cookies and eat 2, you have ___ cookies left.

questions.hint: A bus has lots of seats, but not hundreds -- think about how many rows there are.
questions.template: A school bus can hold about ___ kids.

questions.hint: An estimate isn't a wild guess -- you use what you already know to figure out a close answer!
questions.prompt: What does ESTIMATE mean?

options.text: To guess without thinking at all
To make a smart guess using what you know
To count every single thing perfectly

questions.hint: Checking your work helps you find mistakes early -- like a detective solving a case!
questions.prompt: Why should you check if your answer is reasonable?

options.text: To catch mistakes before they cause problems
You don't need to check answers

title: Planning a Party
description: Use your problem-solving skills to plan an awesome birthday party step by step!
story_text: Guess what? Chip's friend is having a birthday party, and WE get to help plan it! But wait -- planning a party is a BIG job. There are snacks to get, decorations to put up, games to plan, and invitations to send. If we try to do everything at once, it'll be a mess! Let's use our decomposition skills to break the party planning into easy steps. Ready to party plan like a pro?

items.text: Buy snacks and a birthday cake

activities.prompt: Put the party planning steps in a good order!

questions.hint: 8 friends times 2 slices each -- count by 2s!
questions.template: If 8 friends are coming and each gets 2 slices of pizza, we need ___ slices total.

questions.hint: Each kid needs one hat. How many kids are coming?
questions.template: We need ___ party hats if 10 kids are coming to the party.

questions.hint: Start at 2 o'clock and add 2 more hours.
questions.template: The party starts at 2:00 and lasts 2 hours. It ends at ___:00.

questions.hint: Good planners start with a list! Think about what your friends enjoy.
questions.prompt: You need to plan games for the party. What's the BEST first step?

options.text: Buy every game at the store
Make a list of fun games your friends like
Play all the games by yourself first
Skip the games and just eat cake

questions.hint: Good problem solvers find a different way when the first plan doesn't work!
questions.prompt: You forgot to buy balloons! The party is in 1 hour. What's the BEST solution?

options.text: Use paper decorations you already have instead

questions.hint: A checklist helps you keep track of what you've done and what still needs to be done!
questions.prompt: Why does making a checklist help when planning a party?

options.text: So you can check off each step and not forget anything
Because checklists are fun to draw

title: Building a Robot
description: Plan how to build a robot step by step! Break down a big engineering project into small pieces.
story_text: Chip is SO excited about this one -- because today we're planning how to build a ROBOT! Now, you can't just stick some parts together and hope it works. Real engineers plan every step carefully. They figure out what parts they need, what order to put them together, and test everything along the way. Let's think like engineers and plan our robot build step by step!

items.text: Draw a plan of what the robot will look like
Gather all the parts you need

activities.prompt: Put the robot-building steps in the right order!

questions.hint: You draw this on paper before you start building. It shows your idea!
questions.template: Before building, an engineer first makes a ___ to show what the robot will look like.

questions.hint: Count them: body, arms, legs, head. That's how many main parts?
questions.template: A robot needs ___ parts: a body, arms, legs, and a head.

questions.hint: You need to see if everything works correctly. What do scientists do after building something?
questions.template: After building the robot, you should ___ it to make sure it works.

questions.hint: When something is broken, you make it better. What's the word?
questions.template: If something doesn't work, you go back and ___ it.

questions.hint: Engineers don't give up! They find the problem and fix it -- that's called debugging!
questions.prompt: Your robot's arm falls off during testing. What should you do?

options.text: Figure out WHY it fell off and fix that part
Pretend the robot doesn't need arms

questions.hint: If you test each step, you can find and fix problems before they get bigger!
questions.prompt: Why do engineers build robots ONE STEP at a time instead of all at once?

options.text: So they can test each part and catch mistakes early
Because they only have one hand
They don't -- they build everything at once

questions.hint: Planning, gathering, building, and testing are all real steps. But hoping isn't a step!
questions.prompt: Which is NOT a real step in building a robot?

options.text: Wishing really hard that it works

title: Puzzle Challenge!
description: Put all your problem-solving skills together in this capstone challenge! Decompose, estimate, and solve!
story_text: It's CHALLENGE TIME! Chip is SO proud of everything you've learned. You can break big problems into small steps, estimate answers, check if things are reasonable, and plan like an engineer! Now let's put ALL those skills together in one epic puzzle challenge. Show Chip what an amazing problem solver you are!

questions.hint: Decomposition! Break the big task (whole house) into smaller tasks (one room at a time).
questions.prompt: You need to clean the whole house before grandma visits. What's the BEST strategy?

options.text: Try to clean every room at the same time
Break it into rooms and clean one room at a time
Hide the mess under the bed

questions.hint: Even if a book takes only 15 minutes, 200 books would take over 2 days of nonstop reading!
questions.prompt: Tom says he read 200 books yesterday. Is that reasonable?

options.text: No, that's way too many for one day
Maybe if they were picture books

questions.hint: Try one path at a time. If it doesn't work, go back and try a different one -- that's step-by-step problem solving!
questions.prompt: You're lost in a big corn maze. What problem-solving strategy should you use?

options.text: Run as fast as you can in any direction
Pick one path, try it, and if it's wrong go back and try another
Close your eyes and walk straight

questions.hint: This big word starts with D and means splitting something into parts!
questions.template: Breaking a big problem into smaller pieces is called ___.

questions.hint: This word starts with E and means a close-enough guess!
questions.template: Making a smart guess about an answer is called making an ___.

questions.hint: This word means the answer makes sense and sounds right. It starts with R!
questions.template: If an answer doesn't make sense, it is NOT ___.

questions.hint: 3 eggs for each cake, and there are 4 cakes. 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = ?
questions.template: A baker needs 3 eggs per cake. To bake 4 cakes, she needs ___ eggs.

items.text: Practice balancing while someone holds the bike

activities.prompt: You want to learn how to ride a bike. Put the steps in order!

questions.hint: 15 + 12 is about 27. The answer 3,000 is WAY too big -- that's not reasonable!
questions.prompt: You solved a math problem and got the answer 3,000. The problem was 15 + 12. Is your answer reasonable?

options.text: No, 15 + 12 should be close to 27, not 3,000
You need a calculator to know

questions.hint: Great problem solvers use ALL the strategies we learned: decompose, estimate, and check!
questions.prompt: What makes someone a GREAT problem solver?

options.text: Always asking someone else for the answer
Breaking problems into steps, estimating, and checking answers
Giving up quickly when things are hard

title: Repeat After Me
description: Use a loop to repeat a display action instead of writing the same code over and over!
story_text: Hey coder! Chip here! Imagine you wanted to say "Hello!" on your M5Stick screen 5 times. Would you write the SAME line of code 5 times? That sounds boring AND tiring! What if Chip told you there's a magic trick in coding called a LOOP that says "do this thing again and again"? Let's learn how loops save us tons of work!

questions.hint: Without a loop, you need to write the same line once for each time you want it to happen!
questions.prompt: You want to display "Hello!" on the screen 5 times. Without a loop, how many lines of code would you need to write?

questions.hint: A loop is like saying "do this again" -- it REPEATS the code inside it!
questions.prompt: What does a LOOP do in coding?

questions.hint: Loops make code shorter AND easier to change. Want 100 times instead of 5? Just change one number!
questions.prompt: Which is BETTER: writing "display Hello" 5 separate times, or using a loop that repeats it 5 times?

options.text: Writing it 5 times -- more code is always better!
Using a loop -- it is shorter and easier to change!
It does not matter at all
Neither -- you can only display it once

questions.hint: The loop says repeat 3 times, so the beep happens 3 times!
questions.prompt: A loop says "repeat 3 times: beep". How many beeps will you hear?

items.text: Begin a loop that repeats 5 times
The loop goes back to repeat
Loop is done after 5 times -- program ends

activities.prompt: Put these steps in order to display "Hello!" 5 times using a loop!

items.text: Show a smiley face on the screen
Go back and repeat (until done 3 times)

activities.prompt: What happens inside this loop? Put the steps in order: "repeat 3 times: show smiley, wait, clear screen"

questions.hint: Loops do the same thing again and again -- they RE____ code!
questions.prompt: A loop lets you ___ code without writing it over and over.

questions.hint: The loop says repeat 5 times -- so that is exactly how many!
questions.prompt: "Repeat 5 times: show Hello" will display Hello exactly ___ times.

title: Looping Lights
description: Use a loop to blink an LED on and off -- no more writing the same code 10 times!
story_text: Chip has a bright idea -- literally! You know the LED light on your M5Stick? What if we made it blink on and off like a firefly? We could write "LED on, wait, LED off, wait" ten times... OR we could use a LOOP and write it just ONCE! Let's make some looping lights!

questions.hint: Blinking means the light goes ON then OFF, ON then OFF -- two actions that repeat!
questions.prompt: To make an LED blink, what two things do you need to repeat?

options.text: Turn LED on, then turn LED off
Turn LED on, then delete the code
Turn LED off, then turn it off again

questions.hint: Computers are SUPER fast! Without a wait, the light turns on and off so quickly you can't even see it!
questions.prompt: Why do we add a "wait" between turning the LED on and off?

options.text: So we can actually SEE the blink -- without waiting it happens too fast!
The LED will break without waiting

questions.hint: Each time through the loop, the LED goes on then off -- that is 1 blink. The loop repeats 10 times!
questions.prompt: A loop says: "repeat 10 times: LED on, wait, LED off, wait". How many times will the LED blink?

questions.hint: Shorter waits mean less time between on and off -- the blinking speeds up!
questions.prompt: You want the LED to blink FASTER. What should you change?

questions.hint: Each blink is 4 lines (on, wait, off, wait). 10 blinks times 4 lines each = 40 lines! Loops are way better!
questions.prompt: Without a loop, how many lines would you need to blink an LED 10 times? (Each blink needs: LED on, wait, LED off, wait)

questions.hint: First the light goes ___ (it lights up!), then we wait, then it goes off.
questions.prompt: To make the LED blink, we turn it ___, wait, then turn it off.

questions.hint: The number after "repeat" tells the loop exactly how many times to go!
questions.prompt: A loop that says "repeat 10 times" will run the code inside it ___ times.

questions.hint: Without this, the computer goes too fast and we can't see the light change!
questions.prompt: We add a ___ between turning the LED on and off so we can see the blink.

title: Musical Loops
description: Use a loop to play a repeating melody -- make your M5Stick sing the same tune over and over!
story_text: Chip LOVES music! Have you ever noticed that songs repeat parts over and over? The chorus comes back again and again! That's just like a LOOP in coding! Today we're going to use a loop to play a 4-note melody that repeats 3 times on the M5Stick buzzer. Let's make some music with loops!

left.text: Play 4 notes, then play them again
An alarm that never stops ringing

right.text: No loop needed -- just one time

activities.prompt: Match each music idea to how it works with loops in coding!

items.text: Loop goes back and plays the melody again

activities.prompt: Put these steps in order to play a 4-note melody 3 times using a loop!
A melody has 3 notes: beep-boop-bop. Put them in order to play the melody TWICE!

questions.hint: 4 notes played 3 times: 4 + 4 + 4 = 12 notes total!
questions.prompt: A loop plays a 4-note melody 3 times. How many total notes will you hear?

questions.hint: A chorus comes back again and again in a song, just like a loop repeats code!
questions.prompt: How is a song chorus like a loop?

options.text: Both play once and never come back
Both repeat the same thing multiple times

options.text: Add more notes to the melody
Change the repeat number from 3 to 5

title: Count with Loops
description: Display numbers 1 to 10 using a loop and a counting variable -- let the loop do the counting!
story_text: Here's a cool trick, coder! Loops can COUNT for you! Imagine you have a little box called a VARIABLE. The box starts with the number 1 inside it. Each time the loop repeats, the number goes up by 1. So it counts 1, 2, 3, 4... all the way to 10! The loop does all the work. Let's try it!

questions.hint: Think of something you can put things inside -- like a treasure chest or a little ___!
questions.prompt: A variable is like a ___ that holds a number or word inside it.

questions.hint: Start at 1, then add 1 three times: 1 + 1 = 2, 2 + 1 = 3, 3 + 1 = ___
questions.prompt: If a variable starts at 1 and the loop adds 1 each time, after 3 loops the variable equals ___.

questions.hint: We need to display each number from 1 to 10. That is 10 numbers, so 10 repeats!
questions.prompt: To count from 1 to 10, the loop needs to repeat ___ times.

questions.hint: We are counting 1, 2, 3, 4... each number is just 1 more than the last!
questions.prompt: Each time through the loop, the counter variable goes up by ___.

questions.hint: Think of a variable as a labeled box. You give it a name and put a value inside!
questions.prompt: What is a VARIABLE in coding?

options.text: A named box that stores a value (like a number)

questions.hint: The loop starts at 1 and counts up. The very LAST number before it stops is...
questions.prompt: A loop counts from 1 to 10 and displays each number. What will the screen show LAST?

questions.hint: Start at 1. Add 2: now it is 3. Add 2 more: now it is 5. Add 2 more: now it is 7!
questions.prompt: A variable named "count" starts at 1. The loop adds 2 each time. After 3 loops, what is "count"?

questions.hint: With a loop, counting to 100 is just as easy as counting to 10 -- change one number!
questions.prompt: Why is using a loop with a counter variable better than writing display(1), display(2), display(3)... all the way to display(10)?

options.text: It is not better -- writing each line is easier
The loop is shorter and you can easily change how high it counts
You can only display numbers with loops

title: Pattern Maker
description: Use a loop to draw a repeating color pattern across the M5Stick screen -- stripes, stripes, stripes!
story_text: Time to make ART with code! Chip wants to fill the M5Stick screen with colorful stripes. We'll draw a red stripe, then a blue stripe, then a green stripe -- and then do it all over again using a LOOP! The loop will repeat our 3-color pattern across the whole screen. It's like a rainbow factory powered by loops!

items.text: Move to the next position and loop again

activities.prompt: Put these steps in order to draw repeating red-blue-green stripes using a loop!
You see this pattern on screen: red, blue, red, blue, red, blue. What happened in the code? Put the loop steps in order!
To change from red-blue stripes to red-blue-yellow stripes, what order do you put the drawing steps?

questions.hint: 3 colors drawn 4 times: 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = ___
questions.prompt: A loop that draws red-blue-green stripes 4 times will make ___ total stripes.

questions.hint: Count how many times you see the red-blue pair: red-blue (1), red-blue (2), red-blue (3)!
questions.prompt: A pattern that goes red, blue, red, blue, red, blue uses a loop that repeats ___ times.

questions.hint: The repeating part of the code lives inside this thing that starts with L!
questions.prompt: To change the pattern colors, you change the code INSIDE the ___.

questions.hint: The pattern is red-green. Count the pairs: red-green appears 4 times!
questions.prompt: You see this pattern on the screen: red, green, red, green, red, green, red, green. The loop repeats how many times?

questions.hint: Patterns are all about repetition, and that is exactly what loops do best!
questions.prompt: What makes loops PERFECT for drawing patterns?

options.text: Patterns repeat, and loops repeat -- they are a perfect match!
Loops can only draw patterns, nothing else
You cannot make patterns without loops

questions.hint: The width of each stripe is set inside the loop. Make it bigger for wider stripes!

options.text: Change the stripe width inside the loop

title: Button Press Events
description: Learn what events are and how pressing a button makes something happen! Button A shows "YES!" and Button B shows "NO!"
story_text: Hey there, future coder! Chip here! Have you ever pushed a doorbell and heard it ring? Or pressed a button on a remote and the TV turned on? That's called an EVENT! When you press a button, something HAPPENS. In coding, we use events all the time -- press a button, and your program does something cool! Today we're going to learn about button events on our M5StickC Plus 2!

questions.hint: Think about pressing a doorbell -- something HAPPENS because of what you did!
questions.prompt: What is an EVENT in coding?

options.text: Something that happens that a program can respond to

questions.hint: The event is the ACTION that starts everything -- what did YOU do?
questions.prompt: You press Button A on the M5StickC Plus 2. The screen shows "YES!" What is the EVENT?

questions.hint: An event is when something is DONE that causes something else to happen!
questions.prompt: Which of these is a REAL-LIFE example of an event?

options.text: A rock sitting on the ground
Pressing a light switch to turn on a light
A cloud floating in the sky

questions.hint: Without events, a program can't react to button presses, touches, or tilt!
questions.prompt: Why are events important in coding?

options.text: They let programs respond when users do things
They are not important at all

activities.prompt: Match each event to what happens next!

questions.hint: Each button press is its own event! Button A is one event, Button B is another.
questions.prompt: The M5StickC Plus 2 has TWO buttons: A and B. We want Button A to show "YES!" and Button B to show "NO!" How many events do we need?

options.text: Two events -- one for each button

title: If-Then Magic
description: Discover the magic of if-then! If something happens, then do something else. If Button A is pressed, display a smiley face!
story_text: Chip has a magic word for you: IF! Coders use IF all the time. Here's how it works: IF something is true, THEN something happens. Like in real life -- IF it's raining, THEN you grab an umbrella! Computers use the same kind of thinking. Let's learn how to tell our M5StickC Plus 2: IF Button A is pressed, THEN show a smiley face!

questions.hint: IF is a question that checks something. If the answer is yes, THEN the action happens!
questions.prompt: What does IF-THEN mean in coding?

options.text: Do two things at the same time
IF something is true, THEN do an action

questions.hint: Look for the IF and THEN pattern -- something is checked, and then an action follows!
questions.prompt: Which is an IF-THEN statement from real life?

options.text: IF it is cold outside, THEN wear a jacket

questions.hint: The IF part checks a condition. If the condition is NOT true, the THEN part doesn't happen!
questions.prompt: We write: IF Button A is pressed, THEN show a smiley face 😊. What happens when nobody presses Button A?

options.text: Nothing happens -- the condition is not true

questions.hint: The CONDITION is the part right after IF -- it's what the computer checks to see if it's true!
questions.prompt: In the statement "IF Button A is pressed, THEN show smiley face," what is the CONDITION?

questions.hint: An IF statement always has a partner word that means 'do this next'!
questions.template: IF it is raining, ___ bring an umbrella.

questions.hint: What do you do to a button to make it work? You push it or...
questions.template: IF Button A is ___, THEN show a smiley face.

questions.hint: It's the thing the computer checks to see if it's true or false!
questions.template: The part after IF is called the ___.

questions.hint: This is the test or check that the computer does before taking action!
questions.template: IF the ___ is true, THEN the action happens.

questions.hint: The condition (what we check) comes after IF, and the action comes after THEN!
questions.prompt: Which IF-THEN would make the M5StickC Plus 2 show a smiley when you press Button A?

options.text: IF screen is on, THEN show smiley
IF Button A pressed, THEN show smiley
IF smiley exists, THEN press button
THEN press button, IF show smiley

title: If-Else Choice
description: Two paths! If one thing is true, do this. Otherwise (else), do that! If tilted left show a left arrow, else show a right arrow.
story_text: Hey coder! Remember IF-THEN? Well, Chip has an upgrade for you -- IF-ELSE! Sometimes you want your program to choose between TWO options. Like picking what to wear: IF it's sunny, wear sunglasses. ELSE (otherwise), bring a raincoat! Now your code can handle BOTH situations. Let's teach our M5StickC Plus 2 to show different arrows when you tilt it!

questions.hint: ELSE is like Plan B -- it's what happens when the IF condition isn't true!
questions.prompt: What does ELSE mean in an IF-ELSE statement?

options.text: Do this if the IF condition is NOT true

questions.hint: Is it sunny? No, it's cloudy! So the IF part is NOT true, which means we follow the ELSE path.
questions.prompt: IF it is sunny, wear sunglasses. ELSE, bring a raincoat. It's cloudy today. What do you do?

questions.hint: Is it tilted LEFT? Nope! So we go to the ELSE path, which shows the right arrow.
questions.prompt: IF tilted left, show ⬅️. ELSE, show ➡️. The device is tilted to the RIGHT. What shows on screen?

questions.hint: IF-THEN only does something when true. IF-ELSE does one thing when true AND a different thing when false!
questions.prompt: How is IF-ELSE different from just IF-THEN?

options.text: IF-ELSE gives TWO paths -- one if true, one if false

items.text: Check the condition: Is the device tilted left?
IF yes (tilted left): Show left arrow ⬅️ on screen
ELSE (not tilted left): Show right arrow ➡️ on screen
Wait and check again (loop back to step 1)

activities.prompt: Put these steps in the correct order for an IF-ELSE program that shows arrows!

questions.hint: Is 7 greater than 10? No! So the IF part is false, and we follow the ELSE path.
questions.prompt: A game uses IF-ELSE: IF score > 10, show "You win!" ELSE show "Keep trying!" Your score is 7. What do you see?

title: Tilt Controller
description: Use the IMU tilt sensor with conditionals! Tilt the device to move a dot on screen -- left or right!
story_text: Whoa, get ready for something AWESOME! Your M5StickC Plus 2 has a secret superpower -- it can feel when you TILT it! There's a tiny sensor inside called an IMU that knows if you're tilting left, right, up, or down. It's like the device has a sense of balance! Today we're going to combine tilt sensing with IF-ELSE to make a dot move on the screen. Tilt left = dot goes left. Tilt right = dot goes right. Let's go!

activities.prompt: Match each tilt direction to what happens on screen!

questions.hint: It's a three-letter name! It stands for Inertial Measurement Unit.
questions.template: The sensor that detects tilt inside the M5StickC Plus 2 is called the ___.

questions.hint: The dot follows the tilt direction!
questions.template: IF the device tilts left, THEN the dot moves ___.

questions.hint: The IMU gives us data from a special device that detects motion...
questions.template: To make the dot move based on tilt, we combine ___ data with IF-ELSE code.

questions.hint: When the IF condition is NOT true, we use this word for the other path!
questions.template: IF tilted right, move dot right. ___ move dot left.

questions.hint: IMU stands for Inertial Measurement Unit -- it measures how the device moves and tilts!
questions.prompt: What does the IMU sensor do?

questions.hint: When tilted left is TRUE, move left. When it's NOT true (tilted right), move right!
questions.prompt: We want: tilt left = dot left, tilt right = dot right. Which code pattern is correct?

options.text: IF tilted left, move dot LEFT. ELSE move dot RIGHT.
IF tilted left, move dot RIGHT. ELSE move dot LEFT.
ELSE tilted left, IF move dot LEFT.
Move dot LEFT and RIGHT at the same time.

questions.hint: Your phone knows when it's tilted because it has a motion sensor -- just like the M5StickC Plus 2!
questions.prompt: Your phone has a sensor that rotates the screen when you turn it sideways. What kind of sensor is that?

title: Hot or Cold?
description: Build a Hot or Cold game! Use the IMU sensor with conditionals -- tilt toward the target for "WARM!" and away for "COLD!" on screen.
story_text: Time for a GAME! Chip loves the game Hot or Cold -- you know, where someone hides something and says "warmer" when you get close and "colder" when you go away? We're going to build our OWN version! The M5StickC Plus 2 will pick a secret target direction. Tilt TOWARD the target and the screen says "WARM!" Tilt AWAY and it says "COLD!" We'll use everything we learned -- events, IF-ELSE, and the IMU sensor -- all together! Let's build it!

questions.hint: Remember, the IMU senses tilt and motion -- it tells the program which way you're leaning!
questions.prompt: In our Hot or Cold game, what does the IMU sensor do?

options.text: Detects which direction the device is tilted

questions.hint: You tilted AWAY from the target. In Hot or Cold, going the wrong way means you're getting COLDER!
questions.prompt: The target is LEFT. You tilt RIGHT. What does the screen say?

questions.hint: You tilted TOWARD the target! That means you're going the right way -- getting WARMER!
questions.prompt: The target is LEFT. You tilt LEFT. What does the screen say?

questions.hint: This game combines EVERYTHING from this module: the tilt event, the IMU sensor data, and IF-ELSE logic!
questions.prompt: Which coding concepts are we using in the Hot or Cold game? (Pick the BEST answer)

options.text: Events + IF-ELSE conditionals + sensor input -- all together!

questions.hint: When you go toward the target in Hot or Cold, you are getting...

questions.hint: When the IF condition is false, which keyword do we use for the other path?
questions.template: IF tilt direction does NOT match the target, ___ show "COLD!" on the screen.

questions.hint: This three-letter sensor name stands for Inertial Measurement Unit!
questions.template: The ___ sensor tells the program which direction the device is tilted.

items.text: The program picks a secret target direction (e.g., LEFT)
The IMU sensor reads which way the device is tilted
IF the tilt matches the target: show "WARM!" on screen
ELSE (tilt doesn't match): show "COLD!" on screen
Loop back and check the tilt again!
